levels.label: Beginner
levels.description: Foundation Vietnamese grammar — sentence structure, tenses, basic questions, numbers, and everyday expressions.

modules.title: Basic sentence structure
modules.description: In this free grammar lesson, we will teach you the most basic Vietnamese sentence structures. You will be able to use them immediately in your daily life. Let’s check it out!

faqs.question: Is Vietnamese a SVO or SOV?
faqs.answer: The Vietnamese language structure is SVO, meaning sentences are built according to Subject + Verb + Object.

units.title: Subject + Noun (using là)
units.explanation: To give an introduction about a person or a thing, make a statement, use the following sentence structure: Vietnamese language functions as am/is/are in English.

examples.vi: Tôi là David
examples.en: I am David

examples.vi: Anh ấy là giáo viên
examples.en: He is a teacher

examples.vi: Cô ấy là bác sĩ
examples.en: She is a doctor

examples.vi: Chúng tôi là người
examples.en: We are Vietnamese.

examples.vi: Việt Nam
examples.en: We are Vietnamese

examples.vi: Họ là người
examples.en: They are American.

examples.vi: Bánh mì là đồ ăn
examples.en: Banh mi is a Vietnamese food.

examples.vi: Việt Nam
examples.en: Banh mi is a Vietnamese food

units.title: Subject + Adjective
units.explanation: In the Vietnamese language, to-be verbs are not placed before an adjective as it does in English. You just need to add the adjective behind a subject.

examples.vi: Tôi đẹp
examples.en: I am pretty

examples.vi: Anh ấy xấu
examples.en: He is ugly

examples.vi: Chị ấy thông minh
examples.en: She is smart

examples.vi: Chúng tôi ngu ngốc
examples.en: We are stupid

examples.vi: Họ thân thiện
examples.en: They are friendly

examples.vi: Bánh mì ngon
examples.en: Banh mi is delicious

units.title: Subject + Verb
units.explanation: To indicate or state an action, you can use the sentence structure below:

examples.vi: Tôi ăn
examples.en: I eat.

examples.vi: Tôi đã ăn
examples.en: I ate

examples.vi: Anh ấy uống
examples.en: He drinks

examples.vi: Chị ấy đang ngủ
examples.en: She is sleeping

examples.vi: Chúng tôi sẽ học
examples.en: We will study

units.title: Subject + Verb + Object
units.explanation: To make a sentence more detailed and informative, you can add an object to the structure the same way as in English.

examples.vi: Tôi ăn cơm
examples.en: I eat rice

examples.vi: Anh ấy uống nước
examples.en: He drinks water

examples.vi: Chị ấy học
examples.en: She studies Vietnamese.

examples.vi: Chúng tôi đọc sách
examples.en: We read a book

examples.vi: Họ nghe nhạc
examples.en: They listen to music

examples.vi: Con mèo bắt con chuột
examples.en: A cat catches a mouse

modules.title: Present tense in Vietnamese
modules.description: Unlike English, the Vietnamese language doesn’t have specific verb conjugations to indicate tenses. Instead, time adverbs are used to indicate when the action is occurring. We teach you a few time adverbs and the marker “đang”.

faqs.question: How to use the Vietnamese present tense?
faqs.answer: Present simple tense is considered the default tense in Vietnamese.

faqs.question: Is Vietnamese SVO or SOV?
faqs.answer: The Vietnamese language structure is SVO, meaning sentences are built according to Subject + Verb + Object. — Learn more about the Vietnamese Sentence Structure here.

units.title: Present Simple — Affirmative
units.explanation: Present simple tense is considered the default tense in Vietnamese. The typical sentence structure for a present simple tense phrase is as follows:

examples.vi: Cô ấy làm việc hôm nay
examples.en: She goes to work today

examples.vi: Chị ấy học bài mỗi tháng
examples.en: She studies every month

units.title: Using đang
units.explanation: To express that the action is occurring right now, you can use “đang” which means “being/be doing” :

examples.vi: Bây giờ , tôi đang ăn cơm
examples.en: I am eating rice now

examples.vi: Hiện tại , tôi đang uống nước
examples.en: At present, I am drinking water

examples.vi: Anh ấy đang ngủ ngay lúc này
examples.en: He is sleeping now

examples.vi: Cô ấy đang làm việc bây giờ
examples.en: She is working now

examples.vi: Chị ấy đang học bài ngay lúc này
examples.en: She is studying now

units.title: Present Simple — Negative
units.explanation: In the present tense, you just need to add “không” (“not”) before the verb to make a negative sentence:

examples.vi: Cô ấy không làm việc hôm nay
examples.en: She doesn’t work today

examples.vi: Chị ấy không học bài
examples.en: She doesn’t study

modules.title: Past simple tense in Vietnamese
modules.description: Unlike English, there are not any specific verb conjugations to indicate tenses in Vietnamese. Instead, there are time adverbs to indicate when the action is occurring. Today, we’ll learn a few adverbs and two markers “đã”and “rồi” to express past actions in Vietnamese.
modules.main_pattern: Subject + đã + verb + (past tense time adverb)

faqs.question: Is Vietnamese SOV or SVO?
faqs.answer: The Vietnamese language structure is SVO, meaning sentences are built according to Subject + Verb + Object. — Learn more about the Vietnamese Sentence Structure here.

extracted_patterns: Subject + đã + không (not) + Verb + (past time adverb)
Subject + chưa (not yet) + Verb + (past time adverb)

units.title: Past Simple — Affirmative
units.explanation: In order to indicate that an action occurred in the past, “đã” or “rồi” (both mean as “done”) are used with the verb as following below pattern: PATTERN #1 Subject + đã + verb + (past tense time adverb) PATTERN #2 Subject + verb + rồi (already) + (past tense time adverb) Here are few common time adverbs which are used to describe a past action: Hôm qua Yesterday Tuần trước Last week Tháng trước Last month Năm ngoái Last year.

examples.vi: Tôi đã đi học hôm qua
examples.en: I did go to school yesterday

examples.vi: Chị ấy đã đi du lịch
examples.en: She traveled.

examples.vi: Quốc năm ngoái
examples.en: She did travel to Korea last year

examples.vi: Anh ấy ăn cơm rồi
examples.en: He did eat rice already

examples.vi: Tháng trước, chúng tôi cưới nhau rồi
examples.en: Last month, we did marry already

examples.vi: Mình đã học bài tuần trước
examples.en: I did study last week

units.title: Past Simple — Negative
units.pattern: Subject + chưa (not yet) + Verb + (past time adverb)
units.explanation: In past tense, you just need to add “không” (means “not”) or “chưa” (means “not yet”) before verb to make a negative sentence:

examples.vi: Tháng trước, chúng tôi chưa cưới nhau
examples.en: Last month, we did not marry yet

examples.vi: Mình chưa học bài tuần trước
examples.en: I did not study last week yet

modules.title: Future tense in Vietnamese
modules.description: In Vietnamese, expressing the future tense is simple and primarily done using the word “sẽ” before the verb. We teach you how to form future tense sentences, providing and tips for talking about future plans or events in everyday conversation.

faqs.question: How to form the Vietnamese future tense?
faqs.answer: The future tense is formed by adding “sẽ” before the main verb in a sentence. For example, “Tôi sẽ đi” means “I will go.”

faqs.question: Can “sẽ” be used for all types of future actions?
faqs.answer: Yes, “sẽ” can be used for any future actions or events, whether they are certain or just planned.

faqs.question: Do Vietnamese future tense sentences need time adverbs?
faqs.answer: While time expressions like “tomorrow” ( ngày mai ) or “next week” ( tuần sau ) can clarify when something will happen, they are not necessary if the context is clear.

faqs.question: What is the difference between “sắp” and “sẽ”?
faqs.answer: “Sắp” indicates that something is going to happen very soon, implying immediacy, while “sẽ” is used for future actions in general, without specifying when they will occur. For example, “Tôi sắp đi” means “I am about to go,” whereas “Tôi sẽ đi” means “I will go.”

faqs.question: Can “sắp” and “sẽ” be used together?
faqs.answer: No, “sắp” and “sẽ” are not used together because they serve different purposes. “Sắp” emphasizes an action that is about to happen, while “sẽ” is used for general future actions. For example, you would say “Tôi sắp đến” (I am about to arrive) or “Tôi sẽ đến” (I will arrive), but not both.

extracted_patterns: Subject + sẽ không + Verb + (future time adverb)

units.title: Future with sẽ (will)
units.explanation: When you want to express the action which will occur, please use “sẽ” which means “will” before verb:

examples.vi: Tôi sẽ ăn cơm ngày mai
examples.en: I will have rice tomorrow

examples.vi: Chị ấy sẽ học bài 5 ngày sau
examples.en: She will study in next 5 days

examples.vi: Chúng tôi sẽ đi
examples.en: We will go to Ha Noi next week.

examples.vi: Nội tuần sau
examples.en: We will go to Ha Noi in next week

examples.vi: Họ sẽ đi làm tháng sau
examples.en: They will go to work in next month

examples.vi: Năm sau, tôi sẽ lấy vợ
examples.en: Next year, I will marry

examples.vi: Tôi sắp ăn cơm bây giờ
examples.en: I will soon have rice now

examples.vi: Chị ấy sắp học bài một tí nữa
examples.en: She will soon study a little bit later

examples.vi: Chúng tôi sắp đi
examples.en: We will soon go to Ha Noi.

examples.vi: Nội bây giờ
examples.en: We will soon go to Ha Noi now

examples.vi: Họ sắp đi làm một tí nữa
examples.en: They will soon go to work a little bit later

examples.vi: Một tí nữa, tôi sắp lấy vợ
examples.en: A little bit later, I will soon marry

units.title: Future — Negative
units.pattern: Subject + sẽ không + Verb + (future time adverb)
units.explanation: In future tense, you just need to add “sẽ không” (means “will not”) before verb to make a negative sentence:.

examples.vi: Tôi sẽ không ăn cơm ngày mai
examples.en: I will not have rice tomorrow

examples.vi: Chị ấy sẽ không học bài 5 ngày sau
examples.en: She will not study in next 5 days

examples.vi: Chúng tôi sẽ không đi
examples.en: We will not go to Ha Noi next week.

examples.vi: Nội tuần sau
examples.en: We will not go to Ha Noi in next week

examples.vi: Họ sẽ không đi làm tháng sau
examples.en: They will not go to work in next month

examples.vi: Năm sau, tôi sẽ không lấy vợ
examples.en: Next year, I will not marry

units.title: Future — Questions
units.explanation: In order to turn the affirmative sentence into an interrogative one, you just need to add the question words to the future tense sentence. Here are of questions in future tense :

examples.vi: Bạn sẽ đi học ngày mai không?
examples.en: Will you go to school tomorrow?

examples.vi: Bạn sẽ làm gì tuần sau?
examples.en: What will you do next week?

examples.vi: Em sẽ đi đâu tháng sau?
examples.en: Where will you go next month?

examples.vi: Ai sẽ ăn cơm với tôi?
examples.en: Who will eat rice with me?

examples.vi: Khi nào bạn sẽ đi
examples.en: When will you go to America?

examples.vi: Vì sao bạn sẽ nghỉ việc?
examples.en: Why will you resign?

modules.title: Continuous tense in Vietnamese
modules.description: In Vietnamese, the continuous tense is formed by using the word “đang” before the main verb to indicate an ongoing action. This lesson will explain how to construct continuous tense sentences and highlight when to use this form in everyday conversations.

faqs.question: How to form the continuous tense?
faqs.answer: The continuous tense is formed by adding “đang” before the main verb. For example, “Tôi đang ăn” means “I am eating.”

faqs.question: Can “đang” be used for both present and future continuous actions?
faqs.answer: Yes, “đang” is used to indicate ongoing actions in the present. To refer to future ongoing actions, a future marker like “sẽ” can be combined with “đang” (e.g., “Tôi sẽ đang làm việc” – “I will be working”).

faqs.question: Can “đang” be used for past continuous actions?
faqs.answer: Yes, to express past continuous, combine “đang” with a time reference for the past (e.g., “Hôm qua, tôi đang đọc sách” – “Yesterday, I was reading”).

faqs.question: Is “đang” always required to express continuous actions in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Yes, to explicitly express that an action is ongoing, “đang” is required. However, in casual speech, context might make the use of “đang” optional.

examples.vi: Lúc + giờ hôm
examples.en: At + hour yesterday

examples.vi: Thứ 2/Thứ 3/etc tuần trước
examples.en: Last week Monday/Tuesday/etc

examples.vi: Số + giờ trước, Number +
examples.en: Hour(s) ago,

examples.vi: Lúc + giờ ngày
examples.en: At + hour tomorrow

examples.vi: Thứ 2/Thứ 3/etc tuần sau
examples.en: Next week Monday/Tuesday/etc Let’s have a look at a few

examples.vi: Tôi đang ăn cơm bây giờ
examples.en: I ‘m eating rice now

examples.vi: Tôi đang uống nước ngay lúc này
examples.en: I ’m drinking water right now

examples.vi: Hiện tại , anh ấy đang ngủ
examples.en: At the present, He i s sleeping

examples.vi: Tháng trước giờ này , cô ấy đang làm việc
examples.en: At this moment but last month, she was working

examples.vi: Lúc 19h00 hôm qua, chị ấy đang học bài
examples.en: At 19:00 yesterday, she was studying

examples.vi: Lúc 19h00 hôm qua, chị ấy đang học bài
examples.en: At 19:00 yesterday, she was studying 10

examples.vi: Năm sau lúc này, tôi đang ở
examples.en: This time next year, I will be in Japan.

examples.vi: Lúc đó , tôi (sẽ) đang ở nhà
examples.en: At that time, I will be at home

examples.vi: Thứ 5 tuần sau, tôi (sẽ) đang đi phỏng vấn
examples.en: Next Thursday, I will be attending in an interview

units.title: Continuous — Negative
units.explanation: In continuous tense, you just need to add “không” (means “not”) before verb to make a negative sentence:

examples.vi: Tôi không đang ăn cơm bây giờ
examples.en: I ‘m not eating rice now

examples.vi: Tôi không đang uống nước ngay lúc này
examples.en: I ’m not drinking water right now

examples.vi: Hiện tại , anh ấy không đang ngủ
examples.en: At the present, He i s not sleeping

examples.vi: Tháng trước giờ này , cô ấy không đang làm việc
examples.en: At this moment but last month, she was not working

examples.vi: Lúc 19h00 hôm qua, chị ấy không đang học bài
examples.en: At 19:00 yesterday, she was not studying 10

examples.vi: Năm sau lúc này, tôi không đang ở
examples.en: This time next year, I won't be in Japan.

examples.vi: Lúc đó , tôi (sẽ) không đang ở nhà
examples.en: At that time, I will not be

units.title: Continuous — Questions
units.explanation: You just need to add the question words to the continuous tense sentence to make the sentence into the questions. Here are of questions in continuous tense :

examples.vi: Bạn đang đi học ngày mai lúc này không?
examples.en: Are you going to school at this moment tomorrow?

examples.vi: Bạn đang làm gì ?
examples.en: What are you doing ?

examples.vi: Em đang đi đâu tháng sau lúc này?
examples.en: Where are you at this moment next month?

examples.vi: Ai đang ăn cơm với tôi?
examples.en: Who is eating rice with me?

examples.vi: Khi nào bạn đang ở trên máy bay?
examples.en: When are you on

modules.title: How to use pronouns in Vietnamese
modules.description: Learn about how to use pronouns in vietnamese.
modules.main_pattern: Tôi, Mình, Bạn, Con, Cháu, Em etc

units.title: I/You Pronouns (tôi, bạn)
units.explanation: Vietnamese pronouns vary based on age, gender, and social relationship. The most basic are tôi (I) and bạn (you, for peers).
units.note: Anh = older male, Chị = older female, Em = younger person

examples.vi: Tôi là sinh viên.
examples.en: I am a student.

examples.vi: Bạn có khỏe không?
examples.en: Are you well?

examples.vi: Anh ấy là bác sĩ.
examples.en: He is a doctor.

examples.vi: Chị ấy rất đẹp.
examples.en: She is very beautiful.

units.title: Family Pronouns (ông, bà, cô, chú)
units.explanation: Vietnamese uses family terms as pronouns. Ông (grandfather/older man), Bà (grandmother/older woman), Cô (aunt/young woman), Chú (uncle/man).
units.note: These terms show respect and define the relationship.

examples.vi: Ông ơi, ông có khỏe không?
examples.en: Grandpa, are you well?

examples.vi: Bà đi chợ.
examples.en: Grandma goes to the market.

examples.vi: Cô giáo dạy tiếng Việt.
examples.en: The teacher teaches Vietnamese.

examples.vi: Chú ấy là kỹ sư.
examples.en: He (uncle) is an engineer.

units.title: We/They Pronouns (chúng tôi, họ)
units.pattern: Chúng tôi / Chúng ta / Họ + Verb
units.explanation: Chúng tôi (we, exclusive — not including listener), Chúng ta (we, inclusive), Họ (they).
units.note: Chúng tôi excludes the listener; chúng ta includes the listener.

examples.vi: Chúng tôi đi Việt Nam.
examples.en: We are going to Vietnam.

examples.vi: Chúng ta cùng học nhé!
examples.en: Let's study together!

examples.vi: Họ là bạn của tôi.
examples.en: They are my friends.

examples.vi: Chúng tôi rất vui.
examples.en: We are very happy.

modules.title: Plurals in Vietnamese
modules.description: In Vietnamese, forming plurals is not as straightforward as in other languages since nouns do not change form. Instead, words like những and các are used to indicate plurality, along with context to understand whether a noun is singular or plural.

faqs.question: How do you form plurals in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Plurals in Vietnamese are often formed by adding những or các before the noun. For example, “những chiếc xe” (some cars) or “các con mèo” (all the cats).

faqs.question: What is the difference between những and các ?
faqs.answer: Both những and các are used to indicate plurals, but they have slightly different nuances: • Những is commonly used for plural nouns when the context does not imply totality . • Các is used when referring to the entire group or all members of a certain category.

faqs.question: Can nouns be plural without những or các ?
faqs.answer: Yes, Vietnamese nouns can often be plural based on context alone, without needing những or các . For instance, “Tôi thích mèo” can mean “I like cats” without explicitly marking the plural.

faqs.question: Are there plural forms for pronouns?
faqs.answer: Yes, personal pronouns in Vietnamese can be made plural by adding chúng or các before the pronoun. For example, “chúng tôi” (we), “các bạn” (you all).

units.title: General Plurals (những/các)
units.explanation: To make words plurals simply add “những” or “các” next to the counter of a singular noun, then the word will become the plural: • Các is used when referring to the entire group or all members of a certain category. Here is a reminder of the most common counters in Vietnamese: Counter Used for Cái General non-living things Con Animal Cây Pen/Tree/Pencil Tờ Paper/ Newspaper Cuốn Book/ Notebook Bức Letter/Picture/Photo Chiếc Car/Phone/Bike Tấm Cloth/ Photo Viên Ice/ Marble/ Meatball Chai Bottle Ly Glass Lon Can.

examples.vi: Những cái bàn
examples.en: Tables (general plural)

examples.vi: Những cái bàn màu đỏ
examples.en: Tables → The tables are red

examples.vi: Những con mèo
examples.en: Cats (general plural)

examples.vi: Những con mèo đang ăn những con cá
examples.en: Cats → The cats are eating fishes

examples.vi: Các tấm ảnh
examples.en: Photos (general plural)

examples.vi: Các tấm ảnh cũ kỹ
examples.en: Photos → The photos are old

examples.vi: Các viên đá
examples.en: Stones (general plural)

examples.vi: Các viên đá hình con cá
examples.en: Ice cubes → The ice cubes are all shaped as fishes

examples.vi: Những cây bút chì
examples.en: Pencils (general plural)

examples.vi: Những cây bút chì màu đen
examples.en: Pencils → The pencils are black

examples.vi: Những tờ báo
examples.en: Newspapers (general plural)

examples.vi: Những tờ báo đang
examples.en: Newspapers → The newspapers are flying

examples.vi: Những cuốn sách
examples.en: Books (general plural)

examples.vi: Những cuốn sách của ba tôi rất
examples.en: Books → The books of my dad are good

examples.vi: Những bức
examples.en: Tranh →

examples.vi: Những bức tranh thật đẹp!
examples.en: Paintigs → The paintings are beautiful

examples.vi: Những chiếc lá
examples.en: Leaves (general plural)

examples.vi: Những chiếc lá đang rơi
examples.en: Leaves → The leaves are falling

examples.vi: Các chai nước
examples.en: Bottles of water (general plural)

examples.vi: Các chai nước ở trong tủ lạnh
examples.en: Bottles of water → The bottles of water are in the fridge

examples.vi: Các ly rượu
examples.en: Glasses of wine (general plural)

examples.vi: Các ly rượu của mẹ đã hết
examples.en: Glasses of wine → The glasses of wine of my mom are all finished

units.title: Plurals for People
units.explanation: When it concerns people, counters are not used to make the words become plural. Just simply add “những/các” to the singular noun only.

examples.vi: Những cô gái
examples.en: Girls (plural for people)

examples.vi: Những cô gái đang nói chuyện
examples.en: Girls → The girls are talking

examples.vi: Các ca sỹ
examples.en: Singers (plural for people)

examples.vi: Các ca sỹ đang hát
examples.en: Singers → The singers are singing

examples.vi: Những chàng
examples.en: Trai →

examples.vi: Những chàng trai đang hát
examples.en: Boys → The boys are singing

examples.vi: Những diễn viên
examples.en: Actors/Actresses (plural for people)

examples.vi: Những diễn viên đang diễn
examples.en: Actors → The actors are acting

examples.vi: Các bác sĩ
examples.en: Doctors (plural for people)

examples.vi: Các bác sĩ đang cười
examples.en: Doctors → The doctors are laughing

examples.vi: Các ba mẹ
examples.en: Parents (plural for people)

examples.vi: Các ba mẹ đang ngủ
examples.en: Parents → The parents are sleeping

modules.title: Negative sentences in Vietnamese
modules.description: We have learned about the basic Vietnamese sentence structure. We now also need to understand how to negate a sentence to use in simple conversations. In this free lesson we teach you the most common ways to make negative sentences in Vietnamese language.

faqs.question: How to express “not” in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: This is one of the easiest ways to make a negative sentence in Vietnamese. You only need to add “KHÔNG” in front of the verb or adjective, or “KHÔNG PHẢI LÀ” in front of the noun. #1 không + Verb / Adjective #2 không phải là + Noun For example: Tôi không thích mưa. I don’t like rain Anh ấy không ăn kem. He doesn’t eat ice cream Chị ấy không có tiền.

faqs.question: She doesn’t have money How to express “not yet” in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: To express “not yet” you only need to add “CHƯA” in front of the verb or adjective, or “CHƯA PHẢI LÀ” in front of the noun. #1 chưa + Verb / Adjective #2 chưa phải là + Noun For example: Tôi chưa ăn cơm. I haven’t eaten yet Anh ấy chưa tắm. He hasn’t showered yet Cô ấy chưa làm bài tập.

faqs.question: She hasn’t done homework yet How to express “cannot” in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: To express “can’t” you only need to add “KHÔNG THỂ” in front of the verb or adjective, “KHÔNG THỂ LÀ” in front of the noun. #1 không thể + Verb / Adjective #2 không thể là + Noun For example: Tôi không thể bơi. I cannot swim Anh ấy không thể nói. He cannot speak Cô ấy không thể nghe.

faqs.question: She cannot hear How to express “not allowed ” in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: To express not being allowed to do something, add “KHÔNG ĐƯỢC” in front of the verb only . không được + Verb For example: Tôi không được ngủ quá 8 tiếng. I am not allowed to sleep over 8 hours Anh ấy không được cười lớn ở bệnh viện. He is not allowed to laugh at hospital Chị ấy không được nói. She is not allowed to talk

units.title: Negation with không
units.pattern: Subject + không + Verb/Adj / Subject + không phải là + Noun
units.explanation: This is one of the easiest ways to make a negative sentence in Vietnamese. You only need to add “KHÔNG” in front of the verb or adjective, or “KHÔNG PHẢI LÀ” in front of the noun. PATTERN #1 không + Verb / Adjective PATTERN #2 không phải là + Noun.

modules.title: Using adjectives in Vietnamese
modules.description: Vietnamese adjectives are pretty easy to use once you understand the sentence structures required to form sentences. We teach you 3 simple sentence structures to start using Vietnamese adjectives immediately.

faqs.question: How to use adjectives with a subject?
faqs.answer: You can add one or more adjectives behind the subject to make a statement about a person, an animal, a thing, or an event. Subject + Adjective NOTE || Add “và” between 2 or more adjectives For example: Tôi đẹp . I am pretty Anh ấy dễ thương . He is cute Trời nóng và nắng .

faqs.question: The weather is hot and sunny How to use adjectives with a noun?
faqs.answer: Add one or more than one adjective behind a noun t o indicate more details about an object in an SVO sentence. Subject + Adjective For example: Tôi ăn tô phở nóng và rẻ . I eat a hot and cheap Pho Anh ấy thích matcha lạnh và ngọt . He likes a cold and sweet matcha Chị ấy không thích đầm dài và sặc sỡ .

faqs.question: Is it beautiful ?
Is it cold ?
Is it hot ?
How to use adverbs in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: You can add an adverb behind the verb to indicate a state or a level of an action . Verb + Adverb For example: Tôi hát hay . I sing well Anh ấy nấu ăn vào buổi sáng . He cooks in the morning. — You can add an adverb behind or in front the adjective to indicate a level of characteristics. Adjective + Adverb Đẹp hú hồn ! Amazingly beautiful! Lạnh tê tái ! Frozenly cold! — Learn more about Vietnamese adverbs here.

modules.title: Using adverbs in Vietnamese
modules.description: In English, adverbs are formed by adding “-ly” at the end of adjectives (i.e., quick -> quickly). But it is different in Vietnamese. Let’s figure out how to use Vietnamese adverbs for daily conversations.

faqs.question: How to use adverbs in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: You can add an adverb behind the verb to indicate a state or a level of an action . Verb + Adverb For example: Tôi hát hay . I sing well Anh ấy nấu ăn vào buổi sáng . He cooks in the morning. — You can add an adverb behind or in front the adjective to indicate a level of characteristics. Adjective + Adverb Đẹp hú hồn ! Amazingly beautiful! Lạnh tê tái ! Frozenly cold!

faqs.question: How to say always in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Always in Vietnamese is luôn luôn .

faqs.question: How to say very in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Very in Vietnamese is rất .

faqs.question: How to say certainly in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Certainly in Vietnamese is chính xác .

units.note: An adverb of frequency such as “usually”, “sometimes”, “never”, etc is placed in front of the verb.

modules.title: Prepositions in Vietnamese
modules.description: Prepositions play a crucial role in Vietnamese grammar, as they help express relationships between nouns and other words in a sentence. This lesson will explore common Vietnamese prepositions, their meanings, and how to use them correctly in various contexts.

faqs.question: What are the most common prepositions?
faqs.answer: Some common Vietnamese prepositions include trên (on), dưới (under), trước (in front of), sau (behind), and giữa (between).

faqs.question: How do prepositions function in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Prepositions in Vietnamese typically indicate relationships of place, time, and direction, often appearing before a noun or pronoun to provide context, such as “cái bàn ở trên” (the table is on top).

faqs.question: Do prepositions change based on gender?
faqs.answer: No, Vietnamese prepositions do not change based on gender or number; they remain the same regardless of the nouns they refer to.

faqs.question: How to express time using prepositions?
faqs.answer: Time expressions often use prepositions such as trong (in) for durations, like “trong một giờ” (in an hour), or trước (before) and sau (after) for specific times, like “trước 5 giờ” (before 5 o’clock).

extracted_patterns: Subject + ở (to be) + prepositions + object MOST COMMON PREPOSITIONS

units.title: The Particle ở
units.explanation: There is only one preposition particle in Vietnamese which is “ở”. You can use this particle to describe the position of things following this.

examples.vi: Bếp ở sau phòng khách
examples.en: The kitchen is behind the living room.

modules.title: Asking questions in Vietnamese
modules.description: In this lesson, we will help you practice asking questions in Vietnamese with: WH-question words and “Yes or No” questions . Let’s start!

faqs.question: How to say What in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: What in Vietnamese is gì .

faqs.question: The sentence structure to use is Clause + gì?
The sentence structure to use is Clause + ai?

units.title: What, Who, Where
units.explanation: Here are Vietnamese question words, let’s start with What – Who – Where : What Who Where …gì? …ai? Ai…? …đâu? Clause + gì? Clause + ai? / Ai + clause? Clause + đâu? Hôm nay ăn gì ? Bạn là ai ? / Ai là bạn? Bạn đi đâu ? What did you eat today? Who are you? Where do you go? NOTE // “ai” is a question word that can be placed either at the beginning or at the end of the question. There are few common prepositions that you can use with “đâu” such as: ở đâu? = in where/ at where? ; đến đâu? = to where? More here: for ”What”: Cái gì ?!!! What?!! Đây là gì ? What is it? Em đang học gì ? What are you studying? Bạn làm nghề gì ? What do you do? (occupation) Anh đang làm gì ? What are you doing? for ”Who”: Ai ?!!! Who?!! Đây là ai ? Ai đây? Who is it? Cô giáo em là ai ? Ai là cô giáo em? Who is your teacher? Bạn làm việc với ai ? Ai làm việc với bạn? What do you work with? Ai là idol của bạn? Idol của bạn là ai ? Who is your idol? for ”Where”: Ở đâu ?!!! Where?!! Đây là đâu ? Where is it? Em đang ở đâu ? Where are you? Bạn đi đến đâu ? Where do you go? Anh ngồi đâu ? Where are you sitting?

units.explanation: Next, here are Vietnamese question words about When – Why – Which : When Why Which …khi nào? Khi nào…? Vì sao…? Tại sao…? …nào? Clause + khi nào? Khi nào + clause? Vì sao/ Tại sao + Clause? Clause + nào? Anh đi học khi nào ? Khi nào anh đi học? Vì sao anh đi học? Tại sao anh đi học? Anh học trường nào ? When do you go to school? Why do you go to school? Which school do you study at? More here: for ”When”: Khi nào?!! When?!! Khi nào đi picnic? Đi picnic k hi nào ? When do you go on a picnic? Khi nào ăn? Ăn khi nào ? When do you eat? Khi nào tốt nghiệp? Tốt nghiệp khi nào ? When do you graduate? Khi nào bạn đám cưới?Bạn đám cưới khi nào ? When do you get married? NOTE // “khi nào” is a question word that can be placed either at the beginning or at the end of the question. for ”Why”: Vì sao?!!! Tại sao?!!! Why?!!! Vì sao bạn khóc? Why are you crying? Tại sao bạn cười? Why are you laughing? Vì sao anh yêu em? Why do you love me? Tại sao em bỏ anh? Why do you leave me alone? for ”Which”: Cái nào?!! Which one?!! Anh thích ăn món nào ? Which food is your favorite one? Em thích màu nào ? Which color do you like? Bạn muốn mua cái nào ? Which one do you want to buy? Chị muốn bán cái áo nào ? Which shirts do you want to sell?

units.title: How, How Much, How Many
units.pattern: Làm sao? / Bao nhiêu? / Như thế nào?
units.explanation: Next, here are Vietnamese question words about How – How much/How many : How is How to How many / How much …như thế nào? Làm sao… ? (Có) bao nhiêu…? Noun/Adjective/Clause + như thế nào? Làm sao + Verb? Có bao nhiêu + Noun? Thời tiết như thế nào ? Làm sao nấu món này? Có bao nhiêu con cá? How is the weather? How to cook this dish? How many fishes are there? of ”How is”: Cảm thấy như thế nào?!! How is it? Khó như thế nào ? How difficult is it? Dễ như thế nào ? How easy is it? Buổi picnic như thế nào ? How was the picnic day? Buổi concert như thế nào ? How was the concert? of ”How to”: Làm sao đây?!!! What should I do? Làm sao đi đến trường? How to go to school? Làm sao học bài hiệu quả? How to study well? Làm sao đi ngủ sớm? How to sleep early? Làm sao có bồ? How to get a boyfriend? of ”How many / How much” Bao nhiêu tiền? How much is it? Bao nhiêu độ? How many degrees (temperature)? Bao nhiêu ngày? How many days? Bao nhiêu năm? How many years? Có bao nhiêu học sinh ở đây? How many students are there?

modules.title: Numbers in Vietnamese
modules.description: It’s important to learn how to count in Vietnamese. In this lesson, we will help you to master counting from 1 to 1 million in this language in a few minutes!

faqs.question: How to count from 1 to 10 in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: 1 to 10 in

faqs.question: Vietnamese is: 0 Lẻ 1 Một 2 Hai 3 Ba 4 Bốn 5 Năm 6 Sáu 7 Bảy 8 Tám 9 Chín 10 Mười How to say the number 5 in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: From 11 to 19 the number 5 is not pronounced năm but instead lăm .

faqs.question: How to say zero in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: 0 zero in Vietnamese is Lẻ.

faqs.question: How to say 100 in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: 100 in Vietnamese is Một Trăm.

units.title: Counting 1 to 10
units.explanation: For now, let’s brush up counting from 1 to 10 in Vietnamese: Number Reading 0 Lẻ 1 Một 2 Hai 3 Ba 4 Bốn 5 Năm 6 Sáu 7 Bảy 8 Tám 9 Chín 10 Mười.

units.title: Counting 11 to 19
units.explanation: After mastering counting from 1 to 10, let’s read numbers from 11 to 19 by following this rule: 10 (Mười – Ten) + 1 (Một – One) = Mười một (Eleven) Number Reading Number Reading 11 Mười một 16 Mười sáu 12 Mười hai 17 Mười bảy 13 Mười ba 18 Mười tám 14 Mười bốn 19 Mười chín 15 Mười lăm.

units.title: Counting 20 to 99
units.explanation: First, let’s learn how to read 20-30-40-50-60-70-80-90: Number Reading Number Reading 20 Hai Mươi 60 Sáu Mươi 30 Ba Mươi 70 Bảy Mươi 40 Bốn Mươi 80 Tám Mươi 50 Năm Mươi 90 Chín Mươi Then, we apply the same rule of composing to read numbers from 20 to 99. NOTE • When the digit “2-3-4-5-6-7-8-9” is followed by the digit “1”, we don’t read “1” as “một” but we read “mốt” • When the digit “1-2-3-4-5-6-7-8-9” is followed by the digit “5”, we don’t read “5” as “năm” but we read “lăm”.

units.title: Counting 100 to 999
units.explanation: First, let’s learn how to read 100–200-300-400-500-600-700-800-900: Number Reading Number Reading 100 Một Trăm 600 Sáu Trăm 200 Hai Trămi 700 Bảy Trăm 300 Ba Trăm 800 Tám Trăm 400 Bốn Trăm 900 Chín Trăm 500 Năm Trăm Then, we apply the same rule of composing (from 1 to 99) to read numbers from 100 to 999..
units.note: • When the number has the first digit being followed by the digit “0” such as 101, 203, 806, 909, etc, the “0” will be pronounced as “lẻ” (not “không”) • When the digit “2-3-4-5-6-7-8-9” is followed by the digit “1”, we don’t read “1” as “một” but we read “mốt” • When the digit “1-2-3-4-5-6-7-8-9” is followed by the digit “5”, we don’t read “5” as “năm” but we read “lăm”

modules.title: Vietnamese counters
modules.description: There are a lot of different counters in Vietnamese. Also called Vietnamese measure words, here are the 5 most popular. Let’s have a look at them in details. Do not forget to review the Numbers in Vietnamese here, as it will come in useful in this lesson.

faqs.question: What are measure words?
faqs.answer: The concept of measuring words actually exists in other languages! Like in English for certain nouns such as three glasses, or a bar of chocolate for example. The only difference in Vietnamese is that all nouns must have a measure word before them.

faqs.question: What is the Vietnamese counter for people?
faqs.answer: You will use người to count person/people: Number + người 15 người . 15 people Gia đình tôi có 4 người .

faqs.question: My family has 4 people What is the Vietnamese counter for animals?
faqs.answer: You will use con to count animals: Number + con + animal Cá: 15 con . Fish: 15 units Có 15 con cá.

faqs.question: There are 15 fishes What is the Vietnamese counter for books?
faqs.answer: The Vietnamese counter for books is Cuốn. For example: 1 cuốn, 2 cuốn, 3 cuốn (1 book, 2 books, 3 books)

extracted_patterns: Number + cái + non-living thing Bàn: 5 cái Table: 5 units Có 5 cái bàn
Number + con + animal Cá: 15 con Fish: 15 units Có 15 con cá
Number + người 15 người 15 people Gia đình tôi có 4 người My family has 4 people

modules.title: Writing dates in Vietnamese
modules.description: Knowing how to say years, months, and days will allow you to talk about your past activities, future plans and so much more! Learn how to write the date in Vietnamese and how to ask for it.
extracted_patterns: Week day + date + month + year Thứ …, Ngày … Tháng … Năm …

units.title: Days of the Week
units.pattern: Thứ hai, Thứ ba... Chủ nhật
units.explanation: Before anything else, let’s learn the days of the week in Vietnamese: English Vietnamese Monday Thứ hai Tuesday Thứ ba Wednesday Thứ tư Thursday Thứ năm Friday Thứ sáu Saturday Thứ bảy Sunday Chủ Nhật From “Monday ” to “Saturday”, you only need to memorise the structure: Thứ + number in order from 2 to 7 Except for Sunday which is “Chủ Nhật” in Vietnamese.

modules.title: Telling time in Vietnamese
modules.description: It’s important to learn how to give time in Vietnamese. In this lesson, we will help you to master telling time in this language in a few minutes! Let’s start!
modules.main_pattern: Number + Giờ + Number + Phút + Number + Giây

faqs.question: In Vietnamese, when you ask for the time, you use the following phrase: MẤY GIỜ?
faqs.answer: If we break down the words: Mấy: How many Giờ: Hour It is an interesting point about asking the time in Vietnamese.

faqs.question: We do not normally ask “What time” but “How many hours/time?
How to say Afternoon in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Afternoon in Vietnamese in ~ chiều.

extracted_patterns: BÂY GIỜ LÀ MẤY GIỜ? What time is it now? GIVING THE TIME IN
to answer straightly to the question “MẤY GIỜ?”
Number + Giờ + Number + Phút + Number + Giây Giờ : hour (o’clock) Phút : minute Giây : second

examples.vi: Năm mươi 11
examples.en: 50: Fifty / 11: Eleven

examples.vi: Mười một 31
examples.en: 11: Eleven / 31: Thirty-one

examples.vi: Ba mươi mốt 51
examples.en: 31: Thirty-one / 51: Fifty-one

examples.vi: Năm mươi mốt 12
examples.en: 51: Fifty-one / 12: Twelve

examples.vi: Ba mươi
examples.en: Hai 52

examples.vi: Năm mươi
examples.en: Hai 13

examples.vi: Mười ba
examples.en: Thirteen

units.title: Asking for the Time
units.explanation: In Vietnamese, when you ask for the time, you use the following phrase: PATTERN MẤY GIỜ? What time is it? If we break down the words: Mấy: How many Giờ: Hour It is an interesting point about asking the time in Vietnamese. We do not normally ask “What time” but “How many hours/time?” There is another common way to ask about time in Vietnamese:

examples.vi: Ba giờ mười lăm phút năm mươi hai giây
examples.en: It is 7 o'clock.

units.title: Giving the Time
units.explanation: Just follow this pattern to answer straightly to the question “MẤY GIỜ?”.
units.note: You can say “rưỡi” to express “half-past” and “kém” to express “to”

examples.vi: Mười sáu giờ kém mười lăm phút
examples.en: Fifteen minutes to sixteen o’clock It is 5:30

examples.vi: Năm giờ rưỡ i Half-past five
examples.en: It is 8:00

examples.vi: Tám giờ
examples.en: Eight o’clock

modules.title: Expressing "because" in Vietnamese
modules.description: This is one of the easiest Vietnamese grammars. Let’s go through this lesson in a few minutes with us and learn how to express “because” in Vietnamese. “Vì” / “Do” / “Bởi vì” are expressions that mean “because” in Vietnamese.

faqs.question: How to say because in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: “Vì” / “Do” / “Bởi vì” are expressions that mean “because” in Vietnamese. You add NOUN behind “vì” / “do”/ “bởi vì” to indicate a cause or a reason. Vì / Do/ Bởi vì + Noun / Personal pronouns For example: Vì anh! Because of you! Tôi làm việc vì gia đình! I go to work because of my family! — You can also add ADJECTIVE behind “vì” / “do”/ “bởi vì” to indicate a cause or a reason. Vì / Do / Bởi vì + Adjective For example: Tôi ăn vì đói . I eat because I am hungry Tôi uống vì khát .

faqs.question: How to say ” you ” in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: When indicating “You” with no specific gende r and “You” is at the same position level as yours, you can use: Bạn When indicating “You” with no specific gende r and “You” is at a lower position level than yours, you call: Con or Cháu (as a child/ a grandchild) Em (as a younger brother/a younger sister) When indicating “You” with male-gende r and “You” is at a higher position level than yours, you call: Anh (as an older brother) Chú (as an uncle) Ông (as a very old man) When indicating “You” with female gender and “You” is at a higher position level than yours, you call: Chị (as an older sister) Cô (as an aunt) Bà (as a very old woman)

extracted_patterns: Vì / Do/ Bởi vì + Noun / Personal pronouns

units.title: Because with Nouns/Pronouns
units.explanation: You add NOUN behind “vì” / “do”/ “bởi vì” to indicate a cause or a reason. Vietnamese personal pronouns here! PATTERN Vì / Do/ Bởi vì + Noun / Personal pronouns.

examples.vi: Bởi vì chị!
examples.en: Because of you!

examples.vi: Tôi làm việc vì gia đình!
examples.en: I go to work because of my family!

examples.vi: Chuyến bay bị huỷ do thời tiết!
examples.en: The flight was cancelled because of the weather

examples.vi: Bởi vì đồng tiền, cô ấy có thể làm tất cả!
examples.en: She does anything just because of money!

units.title: Because with Adjectives/Clauses
units.explanation: You add ADJECTIVE behind “vì” / “do”/ “bởi vì” to indicate a cause or a reason. PATTERN Vì / Do / Bởi vì + Adjective.

examples.vi: Tôi ăn vì đói
examples.en: I eat because I am hungry

examples.vi: Tôi uống vì khát
examples.en: I drink because I am thirsty

examples.vi: Anh ấy đang
examples.en: Run run do

examples.vi: Anh ấy đổ mồ hôi do nóng nực
examples.en: He is sweating because it’s hot

examples.vi: Chị ấy ngủ bởi vì mệt
examples.en: She sleeps because of being tired

examples.vi: Chị ấy cáu bẩn bởi vì căng thẳng
examples.en: She is angry because of being stressed

units.title: Because...So (vì...nên)
units.explanation: In order to express “because…so…” (or so-called as cause-result relationship), you can use this sentence structure: PATTERN Vì / Do / Bởi vì + (cause) + nên (result).

examples.vi: Vì tôi xinh đẹp nên anh ấy yêu tôi
examples.en: Because I am pretty, he loves me

examples.vi: Vì trời nóng nên tôi đổ mồ hôi
examples.en: Because the weather is hot, I sweat a lot Do

examples.vi: Bởi vì xấu xí nên tôi chưa có chồng
examples.en: Because I am ugly, I don’t have a husband yet

examples.vi: Bởi vì căng thẳng nên tôi không làm việc được.
examples.en: Because I am stressed, I can’t work anymore.

modules.title: Expressing "but" in Vietnamese
modules.description: In Vietnamese, you can express “but” in only one word which is “NHƯNG”. Let’s have a look in this lesson to learn how to use it properly!
modules.main_pattern: Clause 1 + nhưng / nhưng mà / mà + Clause 2

faqs.question: How to use NHƯNG for “but “in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: You can add “NHƯNG” between 2 contrast clauses in 1 sentence or 2 contrast sentences. There are other synonyms for “NHƯNG” such as “ NHƯNG MÀ” / “MÀ” . Note: “MÀ” can only be used between different clauses in a sentence. Clause 1 + nhưng / nhưng mà / mà + Clause 2 For example: Việt Nam có đồ ăn ngon nhưng thời tiết rất nóng Vietnam has good cuisine but the weather is too hot! Bánh mì đắt mà ngon thì OK.

faqs.question: What are some basic sentence structures?
faqs.answer: The most basic sentence structures in Vietnamese are as follow: Subject + “là” + Noun Tôi là David // I am David Subject + Adjective Anh ấy xấu // He is ugly Subject + Verb + Object Chị ấy học Tiếng Việt // She studies Vietnamese

extracted_patterns: Clause 1 + nhưng / nhưng mà / mà + Clause 2

units.title: But with nhưng
units.note: “MÀ” can only be used between different clauses in a sentence. 1 + nhưng / nhưng mà / mà + Clause 2 PATTERN #2 Sentence 1. Nhưng / nhưng mà + Sentence 2

examples.vi: Việt Nam có đồ ăn ngon nhưng thời tiết rất nóng
examples.en: Vietnam has good cuisine but the weather is too hot!

examples.vi: Tôi thích ăn ngọt.
examples.en: I like sweet things.

examples.vi: Nhưng đó không phải là kem.
examples.en: I love sweets. But it’s not ice-cream

examples.vi: Bánh mì đắt mà ngon thì
examples.en: OK. It’s okay for banh mi to be expensive but delicious

examples.vi: Tuyết đẹp nhưng mà lạnh quá!
examples.en: Snow is beautiful but it’s too cold.

examples.vi: Gà chiên ngon.
examples.en: Fried chicken is delicious.

examples.vi: Nhưng mà nó béo quá!
examples.en: Fried chicken is delicious. But it’s too greasy!

examples.vi: Cơm ngon mà cay quá!
examples.en: Rice is delicious but it’s too spicy!

units.title: Although...But (tuy/dù...nhưng)
units.explanation: In order to express the contrast statements in a sentence, you can use phrases such as “Tuy…nhưng” or “Dù…nhưng” with a structure like this: 1 + nhưng + clause 2.

examples.vi: Việt Nam có đồ ăn ngon nhưng thời tiết rất nóng
examples.en: He is handsome, but not smart.

modules.title: Expressing "and" in Vietnamese
modules.description: And in Vietnamese can be expressed using one simple word: và . We teach you 4 ways to use it properly with the corresponding sentence structures.

faqs.question: How to say and in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: And in Vietnamese can be used using the word và .

faqs.question: How to use và with nouns?
faqs.answer: The correct structure to use và with nouns is the following: Noun 1 + và + Noun 2 Tôi thích con mèo và con chó. I love cat and dog.

faqs.question: The correct structure to use và with verbs is the following: Verb 1 + và + Verb 2 How to say with in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: With in Vietnamese can be said by using multiple words. The most common sentence structures are: Subject 1 + Verb + với + Subject 2 Tôi về nhà với mẹ tôi. I come home with my mother someone, something “with” something: Subject 1 + Verb + cùng + Subject 2 Tôi về nhà cùng mẹ tôi. I come home with my mother Subject 1 + với (with) + Subject 2 + verb + cùng nhau (together) Tôi với mẹ về nhà cùng nhau . I come home together with my mom — Learn more about with in Vietnamese here.

faqs.question: Where to find more lessons like this?
faqs.answer: If you prefer to study from home, check out our online courses .

units.title: And with Nouns (và)
units.explanation: Let’s first have a look at how to use và with nouns.

examples.vi: Họ muốn du lịch ở Paris và
examples.en: London They want to travel in Paris and London

examples.vi: Chúng tôi uống nước cam và nước mía
examples.en: We drink orange juice and sugar cane juice

examples.vi: Em sẽ đi du lịch thứ 2 và thứ 3
examples.en: I will travel on Monday and Tuesday

units.title: And with Verbs (và)
units.explanation: Và used with verbs has a similar sentence structure as with nouns:

examples.vi: Chị ấy nên lắng nghe và nói chuyện với bạn
examples.en: She needs to listen and talk to you

examples.vi: Chúng tôi không thích
examples.en: Xem phim

examples.vi: Họ thích
examples.en: Mua xe

units.title: And with Adjectives (và)
units.explanation: Và used with adjectives has a similar sentence structure as with nouns and verbs:

examples.vi: Cái áo này rẻ và đẹp
examples.en: This shirt is cheap and nice

examples.vi: Em rất vui và hạnh phúc
examples.en: I am happy and pleased

units.title: And with Pronouns (và)
units.explanation: Finally, let’s have a look at how to use và with personal pronouns: 1 + và + Pronoun 2 Anh và em yêu nhau You and me love each other Mẹ và con ăn cơm với ba (You and me) Let’s have a meal with dad! Chúng tôi và họ giống nhau We and them are same.

examples.vi: Em và anh đi chơi nhé!
examples.en: (You and me) Let’s hang out!

examples.vi: Chị và em nấu ăn nhé!
examples.en: (You and me) Let’s cook!

modules.title: "Should" & "shouldn't" in Vietnamese
modules.description: In Vietnamese, the concept of “should” is expressed us ing a specific structure, easy to learn in the early stages of language learning. We teach you how to use “nên ” and “Nên làm gì” in every day conversation.

faqs.question: How to say should in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Expressing should in Vietnamese can be done by using the word nên right before the verb. Subject + nên + verb Tôi nên đi học. I should go to school Anh ấy nên đi làm.

faqs.question: He should go to work How to say should not in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: To create a negative sentence and turn should into shouldn’t, add “không” before “nên” : Subject + không nên + verb Tôi không nên đi học. I shouldn’t go to school Anh ấy không nên đi làm.

faqs.question: What should you do everyday?
faqs.answer: Tôi nên đi học mỗi ngày.

faqs.question: I should go to school Is Vietnamese SVO or SOV?
faqs.answer: The Vietnamese language structure is SVO, meaning sentences are built according to Subject + Verb + Object. Learn more about the Vietnamese sentence structure here.

units.title: Should (nên)
units.explanation: Expressing should in Vietnamese can be done by using the word nên right before the verb. tiếng mỗi ngày She should sleep 8 hours everyday.

examples.vi: Chúng tôi nên nghỉ ngơi
examples.en: We should take a rest

examples.vi: Em ấy nên ăn cơm lúc 7h00 tối
examples.en: She should have dinner at 7pm

examples.vi: Anh nên học
examples.en: You should study Vietnamese.

examples.vi: Việt mỗi ngày
examples.en: You should learn Vietnamese everyday

units.title: Should Not (không nên)
units.explanation: To create a negative sentence and turn should into shouldn’t, add “không” before “nên” : tiếng mỗi ngày She shouldn’t sleep 8 hours everyday.

examples.vi: Chúng tôi không nên nghỉ ngơi
examples.en: We shouldn’t take a rest

examples.vi: Em ấy không nên ăn cơm lúc 7h00 tối
examples.en: She shouldn’t have dinner at 7pm

examples.vi: Anh không nên học
examples.en: You should not study Vietnamese too late.

examples.vi: Việt mỗi ngày
examples.en: You shouldn’t learn Vietnamese everyday

units.title: What Should I Do?
units.explanation: This is how you can ask a question with “nên”: tiếng mỗi ngày What should she do everyday? She should sleep 8 hours everyday.

examples.vi: Bạn nên làm gì mỗi ngày?
examples.en: What should you do every day?

examples.vi: Chúng tôi nên nghỉ ngơi mỗi ngày
examples.en: What should you do everyday? We should take a rest

examples.vi: Em ấy nên làm gì lúc 7h00 tối?
examples.en: What should he/she do at 7pm?

examples.vi: Em ấy nên ăn cơm lúc 7h00 tối
examples.en: What should she do every 7pm? She should have dinner at 7pm

examples.vi: Anh nên làm gì mỗi ngày?
examples.en: What should you do every day?

examples.vi: Anh nên học
examples.en: You should study Vietnamese.

examples.vi: Việt mỗi ngày
examples.en: What should I do everyday? You should learn Vietnamese everyday

modules.title: Expressing similarities in Vietnamese
modules.description: The word “ giống như ” in Vietnamese is used to make comparisons or indicate similarity between two things. In this lesson, we will learn how to use “giống như” to express concepts like “like”, “as”, or “similar to”.

faqs.question: How do you express “similar to” in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: To express “similar to,” you can use giống như or tương tự như . For example, “Cô ấy giống như chị của cô ấy” (She is similar to her sister).

faqs.question: Can “giống” be used for both people and things?
faqs.answer: Yes, giống can be used for both people and things to indicate similarities, such as “Quyển sách này giống quyển kia” (This book is similar to that one).

faqs.question: Any difference between “tương tự” and “giống”?
faqs.answer: Yes, tương tự (similar) is often used for formal or abstract comparisons, while giống (like) is more common in everyday speech. Tương tự implies a resemblance but not an exact match, while giống suggests a closer likeness.

faqs.question: How to say more than in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: When you want to compare two things to highlight the superiority of its characteristics such as size, quality, texture, etc in Vietnamese, use this structure: Subject 1 + (not) + Adjective + hơn (more) + Subject 2 Bác sĩ giàu hơn cô giáo. Doctor is richer than teacher Anh ấy cao hơn cô ấy. He is taller than her.

units.title: Similar to (giống)
units.explanation: This sentence structure follows this 1 + giống như (similar to) + Noun 2.

examples.vi: Thời tiết
examples.en: The weather in Ha Noi is similar to Hai Phong.

examples.vi: Việt Nam giống như thời tiết
examples.en: Campuchia Vietnamese weather is similar to Cambodian weather

examples.vi: Sách này giống như sách
examples.en: This book is similar to that book

examples.vi: Mắt kính anh giống như mắt kính tôi
examples.en: Your glasses are similar to my glasses Chai

units.title: Like (giống như)
units.explanation: This sentence structure follows this.

examples.vi: Tôi nhìn giống như
examples.en: I look like my dad

examples.vi: Giọng nói của
examples.en: Em nghe

examples.vi: Cái bánh vị giống như gà chiên
examples.en: The cake tastes like the fried chicken

examples.vi: Bạn chạy giống như con mèo
examples.en: You run like a cat

units.title: As (như)
units.explanation: This sentence structure follows this.

examples.vi: Tôi đẹp giống như ba
examples.en: I am pretty as my dad

examples.vi: Giọng nói của
examples.en: Em hay

examples.vi: Cái bánh ngon giống như gà chiên
examples.en: The cake is delicious as the fried chicken

examples.vi: Bạn nhẹ nhàng giống như con mèo
examples.en: You are soft as like a cat

units.title: As...As (bằng)
units.explanation: This sentence structure follows this 1 + giống như + Clause 2.

examples.vi: Tôi đẹp giống như ba lúc còn trẻ
examples.en: I am pretty as my dad when he was young

examples.vi: Giọng nói của
examples.en: Em hay

examples.vi: Cái bánh ngon giống như gà chiên em ăn hôm
examples.en: The cake is delicious as the fried chicken that i ate yesterday

examples.vi: Bạn nhẹ nhàng giống như con mèo đang ngủ
examples.en: You are soft as like a cat when it is sleeping

modules.title: Making requests in Vietnamese
modules.description: Making requests in Vietnamese is as important as learning how to ask questions! We teach you a few sentence structures you will hear frequently in your Vietnamese studies , so make sure to bookmark this page and come back to it whenever you need a quick review.

faqs.question: How to make requests in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: To ask for help / ask for a favour or ask for someone to do something for you, you can use the verb could có thể .

faqs.question: How to use vui lòng in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: To give a polite request to the customer in a formal way , you can use “Vui lòng…ạ!” by following this pattern below: Subject + vui lòng + verb + ạ! Anh vui lòng không hút thuốc ở đây ạ ! Please do not smoke here, sir!

faqs.question: What is the Vietnamese command form?
faqs.answer: The command form in Vietnamese uses multiple words: You use “…đi!” when ordering or commanding in an aggressive way. You use “…nào!” when ordering or commanding in an encouraging way Không được means to not be allowed to do something . You use “Đừng…” when you want to command someone not to do something . You use “Xin hãy…” when you want to ask for help or favour (please) but in a very desperate way . — Check out sentence examples in our lesson about the Vietnamese command form .

faqs.question: Is Vietnamese SVO or SOV?
faqs.answer: The Vietnamese language structure is SVO, meaning sentences are built according to Subject + Verb + Object. — Learn more about Vietnamese sentence structure here.

extracted_patterns: below: Subject + vui lòng + verb + ạ! Anh vui lòng không hút thuốc ở đây ạ ! Please do not smoke here, sir! What is the

units.title: Could You...Please?
units.pattern: Subject + có thể + Verb + cho + pronoun + được không?
units.explanation: To ask for help / ask for a favour or ask for someone to do something for you, you can use the verb could có thể . Pay attention to the sentence structure below: Anh có thể mua bánh cho em được không ạ ? Could you buy a biscuit for me please? Chị có thể gửi tin nhắn cho em được không ạ ? Could you send a message for me please? Bạn có thể đưa tờ giấy này cho mình được không ạ ? Could you give me this paper please?

examples.vi: Ông có thể dạy
examples.en: Could you teach me Vietnamese, please?

examples.vi: Bạn có thể chỉ đường đến siêu thị cho mình được không ạ ?
examples.en: Could you show me directions to the supermarket please?

examples.vi: Em có thể viết sách
examples.en: Cho anh

units.title: Please...!
units.explanation: To give a polite request to the customer in a formal way , you can use “Vui lòng…ạ!” by following this pattern below:

examples.vi: Các bạn vui lòng không mang thức ăn vào rạp
examples.en: Xem phim

examples.vi: Chị vui lòng đóng cửa ạ!
examples.en: Please close the door, madam!

examples.vi: Anh vui lòng giữ im lặng ạ!
examples.en: Please be quiet!

examples.vi: Anh đừng hút thuốc ở đây
examples.en: Please do not smoke here, sir!

examples.vi: Bạn order món ở đây
examples.en: Please take an order here, friend!

examples.vi: Chị xuất giấy tờ
examples.en: Please show me your papers, madam!

examples.vi: Các bạn không mang thức ăn vào rạp
examples.en: Please do not bring food to the cinema, everyone!

examples.vi: Chị đóng cửa
examples.en: Please close the door, madam!

examples.vi: Anh giữ im lặng
examples.en: Please keep quiet, sir!

modules.title: Expressing "as soon as" in Vietnamese
modules.description: In this grammar lesson we teach you how to express “as soon as” in Vietnamese. There are two common “as soon as” expressions in Vietnamese, which are “Ngay khi” and “Vừa mới” . Let’s have a look.
modules.main_pattern: Ngay khi + clause A, clause B

faqs.question: How to say as soon as in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: To express as soon as in Vietnamese you can use Ngay khi . It is used to emphasize that one action happens immediately after another . It connects two events that are tightly linked in time, with the second event happening right after the first. Ngay khi + clause A, clause B Ngay khi thấy ăn trộm, tôi báo cảnh sát lập tức! I called a police as soon as I found a thief — Another slightly different way to say as soon as in Vietnamese is Vừa mới . It is often used to express actions that happened very recently or to emphasize that something has just been completed. Subject + vừa mới + verb, clause Tôi vừa mới thấy ăn trộm, tôi báo cảnh sát lập tức!

faqs.question: I called a police as soon as I found a thief Ngay khi vs vừa mới , what difference?
faqs.answer: Ngay khi is used to emphasize that one action happens immediately after another . Vừa mới is often used to express actions that happened very recently or to emphasize that something has just been completed.

faqs.question: How to say almost in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Almost in Vietnamese can be expressed by using the words gần như and hầu như . — Learn more about almost in Vietnamese here.

faqs.question: How to say or in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Or in Vietnamese can be expressed by using the words hoặc and hay . In Vietnamese you will use the word “hoặc” to give more than two options in a sentence . N1/ V1/ A1 + hoặc + N2/ V2/ A2 (+ hoặc + N3/ V3/ A3) Cái bàn hoặc cái ghế Table or chair Another way to say “or” in Vietnamese is with the word hay . It is used between nouns, verbs and adjectives. The two words hay and hoặc can be use interchangeably and equally in any situation.

faqs.question: N1/ V1/ A1 + hay + N2/ V2/ A2 (+ hay + N3/ V3/ A3) Con gái hay con trai Girl or boy?
faqs.answer: — Learn more about how to say or in Vietnamese here.

faqs.question: Where to find more lessons like this?
faqs.answer: Check out our Vietnamese Grammar Bank , where you will find free lessons for levels A1 to B1. If you prefer to study from home, check out our online courses .

units.title: As Soon As (vừa mới)
units.explanation: Another slightly different way to say as soon as in Vietnamese is Vừa mới . It also indicates that one action happens right after another, but it conveys a sense that something just happened, or only a short time has passed. It is often used to express actions that happened very recently or to emphasize that something has just been completed.

examples.vi: Cô ấy vừa mới ngồi xuống, cô ấy nhận 1 cuộc gọi
examples.en: She got a call as soon as she just sat down

examples.vi: Tôi vừa mới bị đứt tay, tôi khóc thét!
examples.en: I cried out loud as soon as my finger was cut

examples.vi: Tôi vừa mới bị hiểu lầm, tôi giải thích
examples.en: I explained as soon as I was misunderstood

modules.title: Expressing "not yet" in Vietnamese
modules.description: In Vietnamese, expressing actions that have or have not been completed can be done using specific words like chưa (not yet) and rồi (already). Let’s explore how to use them correctly!

faqs.question: What’s the difference between “chưa” and “rồi” in a sentence?
faqs.answer: “Chưa” is used to indicate that something has not happened yet , while “rồi” confirms that something has already happened .

faqs.question: Can I use “chưa” without “rồi” in a sentence?
faqs.answer: Yes, “chưa” can stand alone to express incompletion. For example: Tôi chưa ăn (I haven’t eaten yet).

faqs.question: Add “chưa” at the end of the sentence to ask if something has happened: Bạn ăn chưa?
Have you eaten yet?
Can I use “chưa” in positive statements?
faqs.answer: No, “chưa” is used in negative or interrogative contexts to indicate an action has not been done yet.

faqs.question: Is it necessary to use “rồi” in affirmative answers?
faqs.answer: While not always required, “rồi” is commonly used in affirmative answers to clearly state that the action has already occurred: Tôi ăn rồi (I’ve already eaten).

units.title: Not Yet (chưa)
units.explanation: The word chưa is used to express that something has not been done yet, or an action has not been completed. It is equivalent to “ not yet ” in English..

examples.vi: Tôi chưa ăn trưa.
examples.en: I haven’t eaten lunch yet .

examples.vi: Anh ấy chưa hoàn thành bài tập về nhà.

examples.en: She hasn’t called me yet .

examples.vi: Tôi chưa đọc cuốn sách đó.
examples.en: I haven’t read that book yet .

examples.vi: Chúng tôi chưa quyết định.
examples.en: We haven’t decided yet .

units.title: Already (rồi)
units.explanation: The word rồi is used to indicate that something has already been done or completed. It corresponds to “ already ” in English and is used for actions that are finished..

examples.vi: Tôi ăn trưa rồi
examples.en: I have eaten lunch already .

examples.vi: Anh ấy hoàn thành bài tập về nhà rồi

examples.vi: Chúng tôi quyết định rồi
examples.en: We have decided already .

units.title: ...Yet? (chưa? question)
units.explanation: This pattern is used to express the question of “ Have you done…yet? ” PATTERN #1 Subject + (đã) + verb + chưa? PATTERN #2 Yes: Rồi, subject + verb + rồi PATTERN #3 Not yet: Chưa, subject + chưa + verb.

modules.title: Expressing desire in Vietnamese
modules.description: To express desire in Vietnamese we can use several sentence structures, to either say “want”, “wish” or “hope” something. We guide you through the uses of muốn, ước and mong.

faqs.question: He wants 10 houses How to use Ước in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: “Ước” in Vietnamese means “To wish” in English. You can use “ước” by following this pattern below: Subject + ước + clause Tôi ước tôi có tiền. I wish I could have money Anh ấy ước anh ấy có thể mua 10 ngôi nhà.

faqs.question: He wishes he could buy 10 houses How to use Mong in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: “Mong” in Vietnamese means “To hope” in English. You can use “mong” by following this pattern below: Subject + mong + clause Tôi mong tôi có tiền. I hope I have money Anh ấy mong anh ấy có thể mua 10 ngôi nhà.

faqs.question: He hopes he can buy 10 houses Do you offer online Vietnamese classes?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! We offer online Vietnamese lessons on our platform. Choose your level, study topic and study time, and you’re good to go! Check out our 7 Day Free Trial to start your learning journey.

extracted_patterns: below: Subject + muốn + noun / verb / adjective Tôi muốn tiền. I want money Anh ấy muốn 10 ngôi nhà. He wants 10 houses

modules.title: How to say "very" in Vietnamese
modules.description: How would you say “very hot”, “too hot” or “extremely hot” in Vietnamese? We teach you how to use the degree adverbs “too/very/extremely” in Vietnamese, using rất , quá and more.

faqs.question: How to say too, very in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: In Vietnamese, in order to express “very” or “too”, you can follow this pattern below: Rất + adjective! Rất nóng! → Việt Nam rất nóng! Too hot! → Vietnam is too hot!

faqs.question: How to say extremely in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: In Vietnamese, in order to express “extremely”, you can follow this pattern below: Cực kỳ + adjective! Cực kỳ nóng! → Việt Nam cực kỳ nóng! Extremely hot! → Vietnam is extremely hot!

faqs.question: What is quá in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Yo express too, very in Vietnamese can also use “ quá ” (same as “ rất ”). However, the pattern is different from “rất” structure: Adjective + quá! Nóng quá ! → Việt Nam nóng quá ! Too hot! → Vietnam is too hot!

faqs.question: How to say must in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: In Vietnamese, there is only one word to express something that you must do or have to do. The word is “phải” . Subject + phải + verb Tôi phải đi học. I have to go to school Anh ấy phải đi làm. He has to go to work — Learn more about must in Vietnamese here.

modules.title: Giving directions in Vietnamese
modules.description: In Vietnamese, giving and understanding directions is essential for everyday communication. This lesson will cover how to ask for and give directions using common phrases, as well as vocabulary for key directional words like “left,” “right,” “straight”.

faqs.question: You can ask, “Làm ơn cho tôi hỏi đường đến…?
Could you please tell me the way to…?
For example, “Làm ơn cho tôi hỏi đường đến nhà ga?
Could you please tell me the way to the train station?
How to say go straight and turn left?
faqs.answer: “Go straight” is Đi thẳng , and “turn left” is Rẽ trái or Quẹo trái .

faqs.question: Is there a polite way to ask for directions?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can use polite phrases like Làm ơn (please) or Xin lỗi (excuse me) before asking for directions, e.g., “

faqs.question: Xin lỗi, bạn có thể chỉ đường cho tôi không?
Excuse me, can you show me the way?
What are the cardinal directions in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: The cardinal directions are Bắc (north), Nam (south), Đông (east), and Tây (west).

units.title: Asking for Directions
units.pattern: Làm sao (how to) + đi đến (to go) + destination?

modules.title: Expressing capacity in Vietnamese
modules.description: In this lesson, you’ll learn how to express ability or capacity in Vietnamese using common phrases like có thể , biết , giỏi , and có khả năng . These structures allow you to talk about what someone can do, knows how to do, or is skilled at, in both casual and formal contexts.

faqs.question: What is the difference between “có thể” and “có khả năng”?
faqs.answer: “ Có thể ” usually expresses the possibility or permission to do something, while “ có khả năng ” emphasizes ability or capacity , often in a more formal or evaluative context.

faqs.question: When should I use “giỏi” instead of “biết”?
faqs.answer: Use “ giỏi ” when you want to say someone is skilled or good at something. Use “ biết ” simply to indicate that someone knows how to do something.

faqs.question: Can these words be used interchangeably?
faqs.answer: Not always. While they all relate to capacity or ability, they differ in tone, formality , and specific meaning . For example, “giỏi” is often used to praise, whereas “biết” is neutral and factual.

faqs.question: How do I make negative statements using these expressions?
faqs.answer: You can add “ không ” in front of the expression. For example: “Tôi không biết nấu ăn.” (I don’t know how to cook.) “Anh ấy không giỏi tiếng Anh.” (He’s not good at English.)

faqs.question: Are these expressions commonly used in daily conversation?
faqs.answer: Yes, especially “ có thể “, “ biết “, and “ giỏi ” are very commonly used in both formal and informal contexts. “ Có khả năng ” is more formal but still useful in written or professional Vietnamese.

units.title: Can (có thể)
units.explanation: “ Có thể ” means “ can ” or “ be able to ” and is used to indicate the ability to perform an action.

levels.label: Elementary
levels.description: Expanding grammar — comparisons, conditionals, conjunctions, modals, and more complex sentence patterns.

modules.title: Basic comparisons in Vietnamese
modules.description: There are four basic comparison structures in Vietnamese: 1 – “Hơn” ~ More 2 – “Ít hơn” ~ Less 3 – “Bằng” ~ As…as 4 – “Nhất” ~ The most! Let’s start!

faqs.question: How to say more than in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: When you want to compare two things to highlight the superiority of its characteristics such as size, quality, texture, etc in Vietnamese, use this structure: Subject 1 + (not) + Adjective + hơn (more) + Subject 2 Bác sĩ giàu hơn cô giáo. Doctor is richer than teacher Anh ấy cao hơn cô ấy.

faqs.question: He is taller than her How to say less than in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: hen you want to compare things on the down side between two things in Vietnamese and express “less”, use this structure: Subject 1 + ít (little) + Adjective + hơn (more) + Subject 2 Tôi ít thông minh hơn cô ấy. I am less smart than her Cafe sữa ít đắng hơn cafe đen.

faqs.question: Milk coffee is less bitter than black coffee How to say as…as in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: When you want to compare two things that have the same characteristics such as size, quality, texture, etc in Vietnamese, use this structure: Subject 1 + (not) + Adjective + bằng (same) + Subject 2 Bác sĩ giàu bằng cô giáo. Doctor is as rich as teacher Anh ấy cao bằng cô ấy.

faqs.question: He is as tall as her Is Vietnamese SVO or SOV?
faqs.answer: The Vietnamese language structure is SVO, meaning sentences are built according to Subject + Verb + Object.

units.title: More Than (hơn)
units.pattern: Subject 1 + (not) + Adjective + hơn (more) + Subject 2 Bác sĩ giàu hơn cô giáo Doctor is richer than teacher Anh ấy cao hơn cô ấy He is taller than her Con hổ dữ hơn con thỏ Tiger is more aggressive than rabbit
units.explanation: When you want to compare two things to highlight the superiority of its characteristics such as size, quality, texture, etc in Vietnamese, use this structure:

units.title: Same As (bằng)
units.pattern: Subject 1 + (not) + Adjective + bằng (same) + Subject 2 Bác sĩ giàu bằng cô giáo Doctor is as rich as teacher Anh ấy cao bằng cô ấy He is as tall as her Con hổ dữ bằng con sư tử Tiger is as aggressive as lion

modules.title: Vietnamese command form
modules.description: To command someone or insist someone do something in Vietnamese you can use đi , nào and a few other words. Let’s have a look at all of them together.

faqs.question: How to use the Vietnamese command form?
faqs.answer: The command form in Vietnamese uses multiple words: You use “…đi!” when ordering or commanding in an aggressive way. You use “…nào!” when ordering or commanding in an encouraging way Không được means to not be allowed to do something . You use “Đừng…” when you want to command someone not to do something . You use “Xin hãy…” when you want to ask for help or favour (please) but in a very desperate way . Check out sentence examples in our lesson.

faqs.question: How to use đi in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: In Vietnamese you use “…đi!” when ordering or commanding in an aggressive way. Verb + đi! Examples: Cút đi ! Get out! Đi ngủ đi ! Go to bed! Ăn đi ! Eat!

faqs.question: How to use Không được in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Không được means to not be allowed to do something . Không được + verb Examples: Không được ngủ No sleeping! Không được ăn No eating! Không được uống No drinking!

faqs.question: How to use Đừng for don’t in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: You use “Đừng…” when you want to command someone not to do something : Đừng (Don’t) + verb Examples: Đừng ngủ Don’t sleep! Đừng ăn Don’t eat! Đừng uống Don’t drink!

extracted_patterns: Verb + đi! / nào! Cút đi ! Get out! Đi ngủ đi ! Go to bed! Ăn đi ! Eat!
Không được + verb Không được ngủ No sleeping! Không được ăn No eating! Không được uống No drinking!
Đừng (Don’t) + verb Đừng ngủ Don’t sleep! Đừng ăn Don’t eat! Đừng uống Don’t drink!

units.title: Command with đi/nào
units.explanation: Both “…đi” and “…nào!” are used to order or command the person whom you are talking to. Just a little difference: You use “…đi!” when ordering or commanding in an aggressive way. You use “…nào!” when ordering or commanding in an encouraging way (to push someone or boost someone up to do something). PATTERN Verb + đi! / nào! Cút đi ! Get out! Đi ngủ đi ! Go to bed! Ăn đi ! Eat!

examples.vi: Chơi đi! Play!
examples.en: Play! (command form)

examples.en: Just rest! Xem TV

units.explanation: Không được means to not be allowed to do something . PATTERN Không được + verb Không được ngủ No sleeping! Không được ăn No eating! Không được uống No drinking!

units.title: Prohibition: đừng
units.explanation: You use “Đừng…” when you want to command someone not to do something : PATTERN Đừng (Don’t) + verb Đừng ngủ Don’t sleep! Đừng ăn Don’t eat! Đừng uống Don’t drink!

units.title: Polite Request: xin hãy
units.explanation: You use “Xin hãy…” when you want to ask for help or favour (please) but in a very desperate way: PATTERN Xin hãy (please) + verb Xin hãy giúp tôi! Please help me! Xin hãy cho tôi một công việc Please give me a job! Xin hãy cứu tôi Please rescue me!

modules.title: Conditional sentences in Vietnamese
modules.description: Conditional sentences in Vietnamese are used to express situations where one action depends on another. This lesson will explore how to form and use conditional sentences with “nếu” (if) for both real and hypothetical scenarios.
modules.main_pattern: Nếu (if) + if-clause A, thì (so) + main clause

faqs.question: How to form conditional sentences?
faqs.answer: Conditional sentences in Vietnamese are typically formed using “nếu” (if) at the beginning of the condition clause, followed by the result clause. For example, “Nếu trời mưa, tôi sẽ ở nhà” (If it rains, I will stay home).

faqs.question: Can conditional sentences in Vietnamese omit the word “nếu”?
faqs.answer: Yes, in some cases, “nếu” can be omitted if the context makes the condition clear, especially in informal speech. For example, “Trời mưa thì tôi sẽ ở nhà” (If it rains, I will stay home) omits “nếu.”

faqs.question: Is the word order in Vietnamese conditional sentences the same as in English?
faqs.answer: Yes, the word order in Vietnamese conditional sentences is similar to English, with the condition clause first (starting with “nếu”) and the result clause following. However, these clauses can also be reversed, just like in English.

faqs.question: Can conditional sentences in Vietnamese omit the word “nếu”?
faqs.answer: Yes, in some cases, “nếu” can be omitted if the context makes the condition clear, especially in informal speech. For example, “Trời mưa thì tôi sẽ ở nhà” (If it rains, I will stay home) omits “nếu.”

extracted_patterns: Trừ khi + conditional-clause, thì (so) + subject + mới + verb

modules.title: Perception verbs in Vietnamese
modules.description: In both English and Vietnamese, we use perception verbs to describe how we experience the world through our senses. These verbs help us express what we see, hear, smell, taste, and feel. In this lesson, we will explore how to use perception verbs in Vietnamese to talk about sensory experiences.

faqs.question: What are the most common perception verbs in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Some of the most common ones include: nghe (to hear), nhìn (to see/look), ngửi (to smell), nếm (to taste), cảm thấy (to feel), and trông có vẻ (to look like/seem).

faqs.question: Can perception verbs be used with both physical and emotional experiences?
faqs.answer: Yes. For example, cảm thấy can be used to express physical sensations ( cảm thấy lạnh – feel cold) or emotions ( cảm thấy buồn – feel sad).

faqs.question: How do I say something “looks/smells/tastes good” in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: You can use structures like: trông ngon , ngửi thơm , or nếm rất ngon to express these ideas.

faqs.question: Are there grammar rules when using these verbs with adjectives?
faqs.answer: Yes. Often, these verbs are directly followed by an adjective. For instance, nghe hay (sounds good), trông đẹp (looks pretty), nếm lạ (tastes strange).

faqs.question: What is the difference between “trông”, “nhìn”, and “thấy”?
faqs.answer: Trông and nhìn imply deliberate looking (active perception). Thấy can be more passive (you noticed something or it came into your perception). E.g. Tôi thấy anh ấy ngoài đường (I saw him on the street).

units.title: I See (tôi thấy)
units.explanation: The verb “thấy” is used to indicate “ I see ” or “ I perceive ” visually or physical things:

examples.vi: Tôi thấy hoa vàng trên cỏ

examples.en: Do you see me?

examples.vi: Em thấy có rất nhiều chim trên trời

examples.vi: Em thấy mặt trời

examples.vi: Chúng tôi thấy nụ cười của
examples.en: We see your smile.

units.title: I Hear (tôi nghe)
units.explanation: The verb “nghe” is used for hearing sounds or noises or information which is not official .

examples.vi: Tôi đang nghe nhạc

examples.vi: Tôi nghe tiếng suối chảy
examples.en: I hear the sound of a flowing stream.

examples.vi: Tôi nghe bạn nói chuyện

units.explanation: The verb “nếm thấy” is used to describe tasting food or drinks.

modules.title: Quantifiers in Vietnamese
modules.description: Quantifiers in Vietnamese help indicate the amount or quantity of something, whether it’s “a few,” “some,” or “none at all.” In this lesson, you’ll learn how to use common quantifiers like một ít , một vài , nhiều , and không có accurately in everyday conversations.

faqs.question: What is the difference between “một ít” and “một chút”?
faqs.answer: Both mean “a little” or “a bit,” but “một chút” is slightly more informal and often used in spoken Vietnamese.

faqs.question: Can quantifiers like “nhiều” (a lot) go before or after the noun?
faqs.answer: In most cases, quantifiers like “nhiều” come before the noun (e.g., “nhiều người” – many people). However, in spoken language, they may sometimes appear after for emphasis.

faqs.question: How do you express “none” or “not any” in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: You can use “không có” (not have) or “chẳng có” (not any, stronger tone) to express the absence of something.

faqs.question: Is there a difference between “một vài” and “một số”?
faqs.answer: Yes . “Một vài” means “a few” with emphasis on small quantity, while “một số” can mean “some” or “a number of,” which can imply a slightly larger, less specific quantity.

faqs.question: Do quantifiers change form based on the noun that follows?
faqs.answer: No , Vietnamese quantifiers do not change form based on the noun. However, they are often used with classifiers (e.g., “một ít nước ” vs. “một ít người “).

extracted_patterns: rất nhiều + noun Tôi có nhiều tiền I have a lot of money. Tôi có nhiều bạn trai I have a lot of boyfriends.

units.title: A Number Of (một số)

examples.vi: Một số nhà hàng sẽ đóng cửa vào cuối tuần

examples.vi: Một số điều anh còn giấu em là gì?

units.explanation: It is used for a small number of items, similar to “a few” or “a several of” in English.

units.explanation: It is used to indicate a small quantity, similar to “ a little ” or “ some ” in English. PATTERN Một ít + noun.

units.explanation: It is used to refer to “ none of… ” in English by following this pattern:
