modules.title: Simultaneous actions in Vietnamese
modules.description: In this free grammar lesson we teach you two phrases to state or to describe two actions happening at the same time in Vietnamese. They are: “Vừa… vừa” “Khi….” / “….khi…”.

faqs.question: Tôi vừa đi học vừa đi làm I both study and work Tôi vừa ăn cơm vừa xem TV I watch TV when I have rice How to use khi in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: To express two actions done at the same time by one or more than one person or one subject, you can use “khi” by following this pattern below: Subject + action 1 + khi + subject + action 2 or Khi + subject + action 1, subject + action 2 You can use “khi” only to describe two short actions which are happening at the same time or as a daily habit.

faqs.question: How to say “ very, too ” in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: In Vietnamese, in order to express “very” or “too”, you can follow this pattern below: Rất + adjective! Rất nóng! → Việt Nam rất nóng! Too hot! → Vietnam is too hot!

extracted_patterns: below: Rất + adjective! Rất nóng! → Việt Nam rất nóng! Too hot! → Vietnam is too hot!

units.title: Both...And (vừa...vừa)
units.explanation: To express two actions done at the same time by one person or one subject, you can use “vừa…vừa…” by following the pattern below:

examples.vi: Chị ấy vừa làm mẹ vừa làm cha
examples.en: She is both a mom and a dad (Background)

examples.vi: Chị ấy đang vừa nhắn tin vừa nghe điện thoại
examples.en: She is texting while she is making a call (Actions at present time)

units.title: When (khi)
units.explanation: To express two actions done at the same time by one or more than one person or one subject, you can use “khi” by following this 1 + khi + subject + action 2 PATTERN #2 Khi + subject + action 1, subject + action 2.

examples.vi: Con chó sủa khi tôi chạy bộ
examples.en: When I was running, the dog barked The dog barks when I go running

examples.vi: Tôi đi làm về khi mẹ tôi đang nấu ăn Khi mẹ tôi nấu ăn, tôi đi làm về
examples.en: I go home when mom is cooking When mom cooks, I am home from work

examples.en: It starts to rain when I am studying When I am studying, it starts to rain Khi ba xem TV,

examples.en: She slept while I was talking.

examples.en: She sleeps when I am talking I talk when she is sleeping Khi em

modules.title: Measurements in Vietnamese
modules.description: In Vietnamese, expressing measurements is essential for daily conversations, whether discussing length, weight, or volume. This lesson will introduce common measurement units, such as meters, kilograms, and liters, and how to use them correctly in sentences.
modules.main_pattern: Bao lâu? / Bao nhiêu + time measurement

faqs.question: What are common units of measurement?
faqs.answer: Common units include mét (meter), kilômet (kilometer), gam (gram), kilôgam (kilogram), and lít (liter).

faqs.question: How do you express length in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Length is typically expressed using units such as mét (meter) or centimét (centimeter), for example, “Chiều dài là hai mét” (The length is two meters).

faqs.question: What difference between trọng lượng & khối lượng?
faqs.answer: Trọng lượng refers to weight (the force exerted by gravity on an object), while khối lượng refers to mass (the amount of matter in an object).

faqs.question: Are there any measurement phrases unique to Vietnamese culture?
faqs.answer: Yes, Vietnamese culture uses specific measurements for things like rice or fabric, such as mấy chục cân (how many dozens of kilograms) or một thước (one measure, often referring to fabric length).

extracted_patterns: to make a question about time:
Bao lâu? / Bao nhiêu + time measurement

units.title: Time Questions
units.explanation: You can follow this.

examples.vi: Bao nhiêu năm?
examples.en: Doing is different from saying.

examples.vi: Nặng bao nhiêu?
examples.en: = How heavy is it?

examples.vi: Dài bao nhiêu?
examples.en: = How long is it?

examples.vi: Mét vuông
examples.en: (m2) Bao

examples.vi: Sâu bao nhiêu?
examples.en: = How deep is it? For area measurement, you use

examples.vi: Rộng bao nhiêu?
examples.en: = How wide is it?

examples.en: = How powerful is it?

units.title: Temperature Degrees
units.explanation: For degree measurement, it is not a metric unit so you omit the measurement after “Bao nhiêu?”. Instead, you follow this pattern:

examples.vi: Ít bánh little/ not a lot
examples.en: How many cakes? → Not a lot of cakes

modules.title: Adjective Comparisons in Vietnamese
modules.description: In this lesson, we explore advanced adjective comparisons in Vietnamese , going beyond basic structures like “hơn” and “nhất” . You’ll learn how to express subtle differences, similarities, and intensities using more nuanced vocabulary and sentence patterns.
modules.main_pattern: Subject 1 + adjective + chẳng kém gì + Subject 2

faqs.question: What are some advanced structures to compare adjectives in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Advanced structures include “so với,” “hơn nhiều,” “ít hơn,” “kém,” and “bằng nhau về mặt…” to express more precise or nuanced comparisons.

faqs.question: Can I use multiple adjectives in one comparison sentence?
faqs.answer: Yes. You can use multiple adjectives by separating them with “và” (and) or structuring the sentence to highlight contrast, such as: “Anh ấy thông minh hơn và nhanh nhẹn hơn tôi.”

faqs.question: How do I express equality using adjectives?
faqs.answer: Use “cũng…như,” “giống như,” or “bằng nhau” with the adjective, such as “Cô ấy đẹp cũng như duyên dáng.”

faqs.question: What is the difference between “hơn” and “kém” in comparisons?
faqs.answer: “Hơn” is used when someone or something has more of a quality (e.g., cao hơn = taller), while “kém” expresses a lack of a quality compared to another (e.g., kém thông minh = less intelligent).

faqs.question: Are there formal vs informal ways to compare adjectives?
faqs.answer: Yes. Phrases like “so với” or “có vẻ như” are more formal, while “hơn,” “kém,” or “giống như” are commonly used in daily conversation.

extracted_patterns: Subject 1 + adjective + còn hơn cả + Subject 2

units.title: No Less Than (chẳng kém gì)
units.pattern: Subject 1 + adjective + chẳng kém gì + Subject 2
units.explanation: This structure is used to express equality or to indicate that something is as good or impressive as another thing. It’s often used to show that two things are on the same level..

examples.vi: Cô ấy xinh đẹp chẳng kém gì diễn viên
examples.en: She is no less beautiful than an actress.

examples.vi: Tôi giỏi chẳng kém gì thiên tài
examples.en: I am no less talented than a genius.

examples.vi: Ông ấy giàu chẳng kém gì tỷ phú
examples.en: He is no less wealthy than a billionaire.

examples.vi: Chị ấy đau khổ chẳng kém gì anh
examples.en: She is no less miserable than you.

examples.vi: Chúng tôi khoẻ mạnh chẳng kém gì con trâu
examples.en: We are no less strong than buffalo .

units.title: More Than (còn hơn cả)
units.pattern: Subject 1 + adjective + còn hơn cả + Subject 2
units.explanation: This structure emphasizes that something exceeds expectations or surpasses the comparison in some way, often indicating a degree that goes beyond what was expected..

examples.vi: Cô ấy xinh đẹp còn hơn cả diễn viên
examples.en: She is even more beautiful than an actress

examples.vi: Tôi giỏi còn hơn cả thiên tài
examples.en: I am even more talented than a genius

examples.vi: Ông ấy giàu còn hơn cả tỷ phú
examples.en: He is even richer than a billionaire

examples.vi: Chị ấy đau khổ còn hơn cả anh
examples.en: She is even more miserable than y ou

examples.vi: Chúng tôi khoẻ mạnh còn hơn cả con trâu
examples.en: We are even stronger than a buffalo

units.title: Both...And Just Like (vừa...vừa giống như)
units.pattern: Subject 1 + vừa + adjective 1 + vừa + adjective 2 + giống như + Subject 2
units.explanation: This structure is used to describe something or someone that possesses two qualities at the same time, and then compares them to something else. This pattern emphasizes the simultaneous existence of two characteristics while making a simile..

examples.vi: Ba tôi vừa vui vẻ vừa sâu sắc giống như bác sĩ tâm lý
examples.en: My father is both cheerful and profound, just like a psychologist.

examples.vi: Bà tôi vừa trẻ vừa đẹp giống như gái đôi mươi
examples.en: My grandmother is both young and beautiful, just like a twenty-year-old girl.

examples.vi: Con mèo của tôi vừa nhỏ vừa nghịch giống như con mèo của bạn
examples.en: My cat is both small and playful, just like your cat.

examples.vi: Điện thoại iPhone vừa tiện vừa nhỏ gọn giống như điện thoại
examples.en: Samsung The iPhone is both convenient and compact, just like a Samsung phone.

examples.vi: Quán cafe này vừa ấm cúng vừa thư giãn giống như 1 tiệm
examples.en: This

modules.title: How to say "with" in Vietnamese
modules.description: Welcome to a beginner lesson on how to say “with” in Vietnamese . We’ll teach you various ways to express “with” in Vietnamese, with multiple to help you understand how to form sentences.

faqs.question: How to use với ?
faqs.answer: Với has exactly the same meaning of “with” in English. It can be used as follow: Subject 1 + Verb + với + Subject 2 Tôi về nhà với mẹ tôi. I come home with my mother Cô ấy ăn tối với chồng . She has dinner with her husband Anh ấy đến trường với vợ.

faqs.question: He goes to school with his wife How to use cùng ?
faqs.answer: Besides “với, you can also use “cùng” to express someone “with” someone, something “with” something: Subject 1 + Verb + cùng + Subject 2 Tôi về nhà cùng mẹ tôi. I come home with my mother Cô ấy ăn tối cùng chồng . She has dinner with her husband Anh ấy đến trường cùng vợ.

faqs.question: He goes to school with his wife How to use với … cùng nhau ?
faqs.answer: When you want to express “together”, this is how you can speak in Vietnamese: Subject 1 + với (with) + Subject 2 + verb + cùng nhau (together) Tôi với mẹ về nhà cùng nhau . I come home together with my mom Cô ấy với chồng ăn tối cùng nhau . She has dinner together with her husband Anh ấy với vợ đến trường cùng nhau .

faqs.question: He goes to school together with his wife How to say with in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: With in Vietnamese can be said by using multiple words. The most common sentence structures are: Subject 1 + Verb + với + Subject 2 Tôi về nhà với mẹ tôi. I come home with my mother someone, something “with” something: Subject 1 + Verb + cùng + Subject 2 Tôi về nhà cùng mẹ tôi. I come home with my mother Subject 1 + với (with) + Subject 2 + verb + cùng nhau (together) Tôi với mẹ về nhà cùng nhau .

faqs.question: I come home together with my mom Where to find more lessons like this?
faqs.answer: Check out our Vietnamese Grammar Bank , where you will find free lessons for levels A1 to B1. If you prefer to study from home, check out our online courses .

units.title: With (với)
units.explanation: Với has exactly the same meaning of “with” in English. It can be used as follow:

examples.vi: Chúng tôi sống với gia đình

examples.vi: Tôi thích học tiếng
examples.en: I like studying Vietnamese with my teacher.

examples.vi: Tôi không thích nghe nhạc với bạn
examples.en: I don’t like listen to music with my friend

units.title: Together (cùng)
units.explanation: Besides “với, you can also use “cùng” to express someone “with” someone, something “with” something:

examples.vi: Chúng tôi sống cùng gia đình
examples.en: We live with our family

examples.vi: Tôi thích học tiếng
examples.en: I like studying Vietnamese together.

examples.vi: Anh cùng cô giáo tôi
examples.en: I like to study English with my teacher

examples.vi: Tôi không thích nghe nhạc cùng bạn

units.title: Together With (với...cùng nhau)
units.pattern: Subject + Verb + với + Object + cùng nhau

examples.vi: Chúng tôi với gia đình sống cùng

With my mom Cô ấy ăn tối với ai? Cô ấy ăn tối với chồng Who does she have dinner with? She has dinner together with her husband Anh ấy đến trường cùng ai?

modules.title: How to say "or" in Vietnamese
modules.description: Choices are part of daily life conversations, such as “would you like to stay home or go for a walk?” We teach you how to give options and choices by saying “or” in Vietnamese .
modules.main_pattern: N1/ V1/ A1 + hoặc + N2/ V2/ A2

faqs.question: How to say or in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Or in Vietnamese can be expressed by using the words hoặc and hay .

faqs.question: How to use hoặc in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: In Vietnamese you will use the word “hoặc” to give more than two options in a sentence .

faqs.question: How to say with in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: With in Vietnamese can be said by using multiple words. The most common sentence structures are: Subject 1 + Verb + với + Subject 2 Tôi về nhà với mẹ tôi. I come home with my mother someone, something “with” something: Subject 1 + Verb + cùng + Subject 2 Tôi về nhà cùng mẹ tôi. I come home with my mother Subject 1 + với (with) + Subject 2 + verb + cùng nhau (together) Tôi với mẹ về nhà cùng nhau . I come home together with my mom — Learn more about how to say with in Vietnamese here.

faqs.question: Where to find more lessons like this?
faqs.answer: Check out our Vietnamese Grammar Bank , where you will find free lessons for levels A1 to B1. If you prefer to study from home, check out our online courses .

extracted_patterns: N1/ V1/ A1 + hay + N2/ V2/ A2 (+ hay + N3/ V3/ A3) Ba hay Mẹ Dad or mom Con gái hay con trai Girl or boy?

units.title: Or: hoặc (statements)
units.explanation: In Vietnamese you will use the word “hoặc” to give more than two options in a sentence . It can be used with nouns, verbs and adjectives. PATTERN N1/ V1/ A1 + hoặc + N2/ V2/ A2 (+ hoặc + N3/ V3/ A3) Trái cam hoặc trái táo Orange or Apple Cái bàn hoặc cái ghế Table or chair.

examples.vi: Đi học hoặc đi du lịch
examples.en: Going to school or traveling

examples.vi: Ăn cơm hoặc uống nước
examples.en: Eating rice or drinking water

examples.vi: Đẹp hoặc xấu hoặc lạ
examples.en: Pretty or ugly or weird Ngon

units.title: Or: hay (statements)
units.explanation: Another way to say “or” in Vietnamese is with the word hay . Just as before, it is used between nouns, verbs and adjectives. The two words hay and hoặc can be use interchangeably and equally in any situation. PATTERN N1/ V1/ A1 + hay + N2/ V2/ A2 (+ hay + N3/ V3/ A3) Ba hay Mẹ Dad or mom Con gái hay con trai Girl or boy?

examples.vi: Làm việc hay học bài
examples.en: Work or Study?

examples.vi: Ngủ hay ăn
examples.en: Sleep or Eat? Vui hay

examples.vi: Cứng hay mềm
examples.en: Tough or soft?

units.title: Or in Questions (hay)
units.explanation: Now that we know how to use or in Vietnamese, let’s see how to use hoặc and hay in questions: Em muốn ăn táo hay ăn cam? Do you want to eat apples or eat oranges? Em yêu ai , ba hay mẹ? Who do you love, dad or mom? Em học giỏi môn nào , Toán hay Văn? Which subject are you good at, Maths or Literature? Chị muốn kem vị gì , cam hoặc bưởi? Which ice cream flavor do you want, orange or grapefruit? Chị muốn xem phim nào , Marvel hoặc Inside out? Which movie do you want to watch, Marvel or Inside out? Cuối tuần chị đi đâu , Việt Nam hoặc Mỹ ? Where do you want to go at the weekend, Vietnam or America?

modules.title: How to say "almost" in Vietnamese
modules.description: In this lesson, you will learn how to express the concept of “almost” in Vietnamese by using the terms “gần như” and “hầu như.” These phrases are essential for describing situations that are nearly complete or almost true.

faqs.question: How to say almost in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Almost in Vietnamese can be expressed by using the words gần như and hầu như .

faqs.question: How to use hầu như ?
faqs.answer: You will use “Hầu như” to express when you almost do something (nearly 90%) which is repeated or can be measured physically such as by quantity, by meters, by time, by days and months .

faqs.question: Subject + hầu như + verb Tôi hầu như đọc tất cả cuốn sách ở đây I almost read all the books here How to use gần như ?
faqs.answer: Another expression for “almost” can be used when you almost do something (nearly 90%) at once or at one specific time/moment!

faqs.question: Subject + gần như + verb Tôi gần như trở thành 1 người khác I almost become another person (at this moment) How to say or in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Or in Vietnamese can be expressed by using the words hoặc and hay . In Vietnamese you will use the word “hoặc” to give more than two options in a sentence . N1/ V1/ A1 + hoặc + N2/ V2/ A2 (+ hoặc + N3/ V3/ A3) Cái bàn hoặc cái ghế Table or chair Another way to say “or” in Vietnamese is with the word hay . It is used between nouns, verbs and adjectives. The two words hay and hoặc can be use interchangeably and equally in any situation.

faqs.question: N1/ V1/ A1 + hay + N2/ V2/ A2 (+ hay + N3/ V3/ A3) Con gái hay con trai Girl or boy?
faqs.answer: — Learn more about how to say or in Vietnamese here.

faqs.question: Where to find more lessons like this?
faqs.answer: Check out our Vietnamese Grammar Bank , where you will find free lessons for levels A1 to B1. If you prefer to study from home, check out our online courses .

examples.vi: Tôi hầu như đọc tất cả cuốn sách ở đây
examples.en: I almost read all the books here The action of “reading” is measured by the number of books

examples.vi: Tôi hầu như không ăn táo
examples.en: I almost not eat any apples The action of “not eating” is measured by the number of apples notes

examples.vi: Anh ấy hầu như không ăn sáng mỗi buổi sáng
examples.en: He almost doesn’t eat breakfast every morning The action of “not eating breakfast” is repeated in every morning

examples.vi: Chị ấy hầu như đi ngủ lúc 8h00 mỗi buổi tối
examples.en: She almost goes to bed at 8pm every night The action of “going to bed is repeated in every night

examples.vi: Chúng tôi hầu như đi làm mỗi ngày
examples.en: We almost go to work everyday The action of “going to work” is repeated in every day

examples.vi: Họ hầu như không ở nhà thứ 7
examples.en: They are almost not at home on Saturday The action of “not being at home” is repeated on every Saturday

units.title: Almost with gần như
units.pattern: Subject + gần như + verb Em gần như quên anh rồi! (Now) I can almost forget you! Tôi gần như trở thành 1 người khác I almost become another person (at this moment)
units.explanation: To express “almost” in Vietnamese you can also use gần như , more specifically when you almost do something (nearly 90%) at once or at one specific time/moment .

examples.vi: Mẹ gần như rất mệt
examples.en: Mom is almost tired (now)

examples.vi: Tôi gần như không ngủ đêm
examples.en: I could not almost sleep last night

examples.vi: Chị ấy gần như thích tôi!
examples.en: She almost liked me (right now)!

modules.title: How to say 'if' in Vietnamese
modules.description: This lesson will cover the most common way to say if in Vietnamese using Nếu and Nếu như . We will also have a look at how to negate this word to form if not in Vietnamese .

faqs.question: How to say if in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: To express if in Vietnamese you’ll need to use the words Nếu or Nếu như . Nếu/ Nếu như + Clause 1, Clause 2 Nếu anh không đến được, nhớ báo em nhá! If you can’t come, let me know!

faqs.question: How to say if not in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: If not in Vietnamese is Nếu không .

faqs.question: How to say or in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Or in Vietnamese can be expressed by using the words hoặc and hay . Learn more about how to say or in Vietnamese here.

units.title: If with nếu/nếu như
units.explanation: To express if in Vietnamese you’ll need to use the words Nếu or Nếu như . Follow this structure to form sentences with these words:

examples.vi: Nếu anh không đến được, nhớ báo em nhá!
examples.en: If you can’t come, let me know!

examples.vi: Nếu không ăn sáng, tôi sẽ bị đau bụng
examples.en: If i don’t eat breakfast, my tummy is hurt

examples.vi: Nếu không ngủ trước 10h tối, tôi sẽ thức cả đêm
examples.en: If I don’t sleep by 10 pm, I will stay awake all night

examples.vi: Nếu ra khỏi phòng, nhớ tắt đèn!
examples.en: If you leave the room, remember to turn off light

examples.vi: Tắt đèn
examples.en: Sau khi ra

examples.vi: Đi ngủ ngay, nếu không muốn bị phạt
examples.en: Go to sleep, if you don’t want a punishment

examples.vi: Nấu ăn đi, nếu muốn ăn
examples.en: Cook now, if you want to eat Hun anh,

units.title: If Not (nếu không)
units.explanation: To turn it into the negative form, do not add any subjects following “Nếu không”. This is how you can use If not in Vietnamese:

examples.vi: Nếu không ôm
examples.en: Em, anh

examples.vi: Nếu không làm việc, em không có tiền
examples.en: If (I)not go to work, I don’t have money

examples.vi: Nếu không đi chơi, em bị stress If (I)not go out,
examples.en: I am stressed

examples.vi: Nếu không đi
examples.en: Toilet, em

modules.title: How to say "together" in Vietnamese
modules.description: In Vietnamese, expressing “together” uses specific words and structures to convey joint actions or shared experiences. It is expressed primarily through words like “cùng” , “cùng nhau” and “với nhau” .

faqs.question: How do you say “together” in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: The word “cùng nhau” is commonly used to mean “together” in Vietnamese.

faqs.question: Can “cùng nhau” formally and informally?
faqs.answer: Yes, “cùng nhau” is versatile and can be used in both formal and informal contexts.

faqs.question: Where do I place “cùng nhau” in a sentence?
faqs.answer: “Cùng nhau” is usually placed before the verb, as in “Chúng ta cùng nhau học tiếng Việt” (We study Vietnamese together).

faqs.question: Is “cùng” alone enough to mean “together”?
faqs.answer: Yes, “cùng” alone can imply “together” and is often used in casual speech. However, “cùng nhau” gives more emphasis to the idea of doing something collectively.

faqs.question: Can “cùng nhau” be used with nouns?
faqs.answer: “Cùng nhau” is primarily used with verbs to describe people doing something together, rather than with nouns.

units.title: Together: cùng
units.explanation: “Cùng” is a straightforward way to say “together.” It’s commonly paired with verbs to show people are doing something together.

examples.vi: Tôi cùng anh ấy làm bài tập
examples.en: I and him do homework together

examples.vi: Cô ấy cùng bạn học nhạc
examples.en: She and her friends study music together

examples.vi: Em cùng anh băng qua đại dương
examples.en: I and you cross the ocean together

examples.vi: Mẹ cùng ba đi du lịch
examples.en: Mom and dad go traveling together

examples.vi: Chúng tôi cùng học bài
examples.en: We study together

examples.vi: Cả lớp tôi cùng ăn trưa
examples.en: Our classmates have lunch together

units.title: Together: với nhau
units.explanation: “Với nhau” is an expression that emphasizes the reciprocity in the action. Together, but with each other..

examples.vi: Chúng tôi ăn tối với nhau
examples.en: We have dinner with each othe r.

examples.vi: Họ nói chuyện với nhau hàng ngày.
examples.en: They talk to each other every day.

examples.vi: Nhóm bạn làm việc với
examples.en: Friends work with each other .

examples.vi: Chúng tôi tâm sự với nhau thường xuyên
examples.en: We often confide with each other

examples.vi: Họ chia sẻ cảm xúc với
examples.en: They share feelings with each other

examples.vi: Họ thích hát với
examples.en: They like to sing with each other

units.title: Together: cùng nhau
units.explanation: “Cùng nhau” is an expression that highlights the group nature of the activity..

examples.vi: Chúng tôi cùng nhau chuẩn bị bữa tối
examples.en: We prepare dinner together

examples.vi: Họ cùng nhau tập thể dục buổi sáng
examples.en: They exercise together in the morning. Gia

examples.vi: Chúng tôi cùng nhau giải quyết vấn đề
examples.en: We solve the problem together

examples.vi: Bạn bè cùng nhau tổ chức sự kiện
examples.en: Friends organize an event together

examples.vi: Nhóm bạn cùng
examples.en: Nhau xem

modules.title: How to say "in order to" in Vietnamese
modules.description: In Vietnamese, the phrase “in order to” is expressed using specific particles and verbs that indicate purpose. The most common terms used are “để,” “để cho,” and “nhằm.”.

faqs.question: How do you say “in order to” in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: The phrase “để” is commonly used to express “in order to” in Vietnamese.

faqs.question: Where do I place “để” in a sentence?
faqs.answer: “Để” is typically placed before the verb that follows the purpose, as in “Tôi học tiếng Việt để nói chuyện với bạn bè” (I study Vietnamese in order to talk with friends).

faqs.question: Can “để” be used for formal and informal?
faqs.answer: Yes, “để” is versatile and appropriate in both formal and informal contexts.

faqs.question: Is “để” only used with verbs?
faqs.answer: Primarily, yes. “Để” is used with verbs to indicate purpose, like “để học” (to study) or “để làm việc” (to work).

units.title: In Order To (để)
units.explanation: “Để” is the most common way to express purpose and is used similarly to “in order to.”.

examples.vi: Tôi học tiếng
examples.en: I study Vietnamese in order to work with Vietnamese people.

examples.vi: Việt để làm việc với người
examples.en: (continuation) ...work with Vietnamese people.

examples.vi: Việt hiệu quả
examples.en: In order to work with Vietnamese effectively, I learn Vietnamese

examples.vi: Cô ấy tập thể dục mỗi ngày để giữ sức khỏe.
examples.en: In order to stay healthy, she exercises everyday

examples.vi: Họ tiết kiệm tiền từ bây giờ để mua nhà trong 10 năm nữa.
examples.en: In order to buy a house in next 10 years, they start saving up money from now

examples.vi: Họ học từ vựng để nói trôi chảy
examples.en: They study vocabulary in order to speak fluently.

examples.vi: Tôi chạy bộ để giảm cân
examples.en: In order to lose weight, I run

examples.vi: Tôi làm việc chăm chỉ để có nhiều tiền
examples.en: In order to earn a lot of money, I word hard

units.title: With the Aim Of (nhằm)
units.explanation: “Nhằm” conveys a more formal or intentional purpose. PATTERN #1 Subject + Verb + nhằm + Verb PATTERN #2 Nhằm + Verb, Subject + Verb.

examples.vi: Chúng tôi làm việc chăm chỉ nhằm cải thiện chất lượng cuộc sống
examples.en: We word hard in order to improve the quality of life

examples.vi: Nhằm cải thiện chất lượng cuộc sống, chúng tôi làm việc chăm chỉ
examples.en: In order to improve the quality of life, we work hard

examples.vi: Vứt rác vào thùng rác nhằm bảo vệ môi trường
examples.en: Put trash into the bin in order to save the environment

examples.vi: Nhằm bảo vệ môi trường, xin đừng chặt cây
examples.en: In order to save the environment, please do not cut down the trees

examples.vi: Ba tôi quyên góp tiền nhằm giúp người nghèo
examples.en: My father makes a donation in order to help the poor

examples.vi: Nhằm giúp người nghèo, ba tôi kêu gọi quyên góp từ cộng đồng
examples.en: In order to help the poor, my father calls for a donation from community

modules.title: How to say "this" and "that" in Vietnamese
modules.description: In Vietnamese, “this” and “that” can be expressed as “này” and “đó” respectively. Understanding how to use these expressions can enhance your communication skills.

faqs.question: How do I say this and that in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: In Vietnamese, “this” is typically translated as này , and “that” as kia or đó , depending on context and distance.

faqs.question: How to say only in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Only in Vietnamese can be expressed using the words chỉ and mỗi , as well as both words under the expression chỉ…mỗi… . Subject + chỉ + verb/adjective Mỗi (only) + noun (except for time and date) Chỉ + verb + mỗi + noun (except for time and date) — Learn more about only in Vietnamese here.

faqs.question: How to say if in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: To express if in Vietnamese you’ll need to use the words Nếu or Nếu như. Nếu/ Nếu như + Clause 1, Clause 2 Nếu anh không đến được, nhớ báo em nhá! If you can’t come, let me know! — Learn more about how to say if in Vietnamese here.

units.title: This Is (cái này là)
units.explanation: “Cái này (là)…” means “This (is)…” and is used with the sentence structure below: PATTERN Cái này + (là) + Noun/ Adjective/Verb/Clause.

examples.vi: Cái này là món ăn tôi thích nhất
examples.en: This is my favorite dish

examples.vi: Cái này là cuốn sách tôi đã đọc
examples.en: This is the book I have read

examples.vi: Cái này rất đẹp
examples.en: This is very beautiful

examples.vi: Cái này rất dễ sử dụng
examples.en: This is very easy to use

examples.vi: Cái này không hợp với tôi
examples.en: This doesn’ t suit me

examples.vi: Cái này có hương vị
examples.en: This has a delicious flavor

units.title: That Is (cái đó là)
units.explanation: “Cái đó (là)…” means “That (is)…” and is used with the structure below:

examples.vi: Cái đó + (là) +
examples.en: Noun/ Adjective/Verb/Clause

examples.vi: Cái đó là món ăn tôi thích nhất
examples.en: That is my favorite dish

examples.vi: Cái đó là cuốn sách tôi đã đọc
examples.en: That is the book I have read

examples.vi: Cái đó rất đẹp
examples.en: That is very beautiful

examples.vi: Cái đó rất dễ sử dụng
examples.en: That is very easy to use

examples.vi: Cái đó không hợp với tôi
examples.en: That doesn’ t suit me

examples.vi: Cái đó có hương vị
examples.en: That has a delicious flavor

units.title: This + Noun (noun + này)
units.explanation: In order to say “this book, this pen, this paper, etc”, please use the pattern below: PATTERN Noun + này Cuốn sách này rất hay This book is very good Bức tranh này được vẽ bởi cô ấy. This painting was painted by her Cái áo này màu xanh This shirt is blue Bánh mì này rất ngon This bread is very delicious. Kế hoạch này cần thay đổi This plan needs to be changed Bộ phim này được đánh giá cao This movie is highly rated.

examples.vi: Cuốn sách này rất
examples.en: This book is very good

examples.vi: Bức tranh này được vẽ bởi cô ấy.
examples.en: This painting was painted by her

examples.vi: Cái áo này màu
examples.en: This shirt is blue

examples.vi: Bánh mì này rất
examples.en: This bread is very delicious.

examples.vi: Kế hoạch này cần thay đổi
examples.en: This plan needs to be changed

examples.vi: Bộ phim này được đánh giá
examples.en: This movie is highly rated

units.title: That + Noun (noun + đó)
units.explanation: In order to say “that book,that pen, that paper, etc”, you can use the following sentence structure: PATTERN Noun + đó Cuốn sách đó rất hay That book is very good Bức tranh đó được vẽ bởi cô ấy. That painting was painted by her Cái áo đó màu xanh That shirt is blue Bánh mì đó rất ngon That bread is very delicious. Kế hoạch đó cần thay đổi That plan needs to be changed Bộ phim đó được đánh giá cao That movie is highly rated.

examples.vi: Cuốn sách đó rất
examples.en: That book is very good

examples.vi: Bức tranh đó được vẽ bởi cô ấy.
examples.en: That painting was painted by her

examples.vi: Cái áo đó màu
examples.en: That shirt is blue

examples.vi: Bánh mì đó rất
examples.en: That bread is very delicious.

examples.vi: Kế hoạch đó cần thay đổi
examples.en: That plan needs to be changed

examples.vi: Bộ phim đó được đánh giá
examples.en: That movie is highly rated

modules.title: How to say "after all" in Vietnamese
modules.description: We often find ourselves reflecting on decisions and outcomes. The phrase “sau cùng” or “cuối cùng” in Vietnamese allows us to articulate those moments of realization or conclusions that come after careful thought.

faqs.question: How do you say “after all” in Vietnamese to indicate a conclusion?
faqs.answer: In Vietnamese, “sau tất cả” or “rốt cuộc” can be used to mean “after all” when summing up or reaching a conclusion.

faqs.question: Can “after all” expressions be used in both positive and negative sentences?
faqs.answer: Yes, terms like “sau tất cả” or “rốt cuộc” are neutral and can be used in sentences with both positive and negative meanings.

faqs.question: How can I use “after all” to add emphasis in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: To add emphasis, you can use “sau cùng thì” to stress that an action or outcome was expected all along.

faqs.question: Can I use “sau tất cả” in formal and informal settings?
faqs.answer: Yes, “sau tất cả” is versatile and can be used in both formal and informal settings to express the idea of “after all.”

units.title: Finally (cuối cùng)
units.explanation: Cuối cùng is an adverb frequently used in both spoken and written Vietnamese to describe the final outcome of a process , the last event in a sequence , or a decision reached after consideration. PATTERN Cuối cùng, Subject + Verb.

examples.vi: Cuối cùng, tôi đã quyết định không đi
examples.en: After all , I finally decided not to go

examples.vi: Cuối cùng, em vẫn phải làm bài tập
examples.en: After all , you still have to do your homework

examples.vi: Cuối cùng , anh vẫn bị mất tiền
examples.en: After all , he still lost money

examples.vi: Cuối cùng , ba đã hết giận
examples.en: After all , Dad is no longer angry

examples.vi: Cuối cùng , tôi cũng ngủ được bình thường
examples.en: After all , I can finally sleep normally

examples.vi: Cuối cùng , tôi đã có chồng
examples.en: After all , I got married

modules.title: How to say "moreover" in Vietnamese
modules.description: Moreover in Vietnamese can be expressed in two ways. When you want to add information, reinforce points, and enhance their arguments, you can use the phrases “hơn nữa” and “ngoài ra”.

faqs.question: How to say moreover in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: The word “hơn nữa” is commonly used to express “moreover” or “in addition” in Vietnamese, providing a way to add further information or reasons in a sentence.

faqs.question: Can hơn nữa be used at the beginning?
faqs.answer: Yes, “hơn nữa” can be used at the beginning of a sentence or clause to emphasize additional information, much like starting with “moreover” in English.

faqs.question: Any other words similar to hơn nữa ?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can also use “ngoài ra” (besides/aside from that) or “thêm vào đó” (in addition) for a similar meaning, although each has a slightly different nuance.

faqs.question: Does hơn nữa require a specific structure?
faqs.answer: Not necessarily. “Hơn nữa” is ble and can be added between clauses, after a pause, or at the start of a sentence to add information without changing sentence structure.

faqs.question: Is hơn nữa formal or informal?
faqs.answer: “Hơn nữa” is neutral and can be used in both formal and informal contexts, making it versatile for everyday conversations as well as written communication.

units.title: Moreover (hơn nữa)
units.explanation: Hơn nữa means “furthermore” or “moreover.” It is used to introduce additional information that supports or enhances the previous statement. This phrase is often used when emphasizing an extra point or reason that strengthens the preceding idea or argument. 1. Hơn nữa, Clause 2..

examples.vi: Tôi rất thích đọc sách.
examples.en: I really like reading books.

examples.vi: Hơn nữa , tôi thích
examples.en: I really like reading books. Moreover , I enjoy watching movies

examples.vi: Cô ấy là một học sinh xuất sắc.
examples.en: She is an excellent student.

examples.vi: Hơn nữa , cô ấy cũng là một học
examples.en: Sinh tham gia

examples.vi: Họ đã hoàn thành dự án đúng hạn.
examples.en: They completed the project on time.

examples.vi: Hơn nữa , họ đã nhận được giải thưởng
examples.en: They completed the project on time. Moreover, they received an award

examples.vi: Anh ấy rất thông minh.
examples.en: He is very smart.

examples.vi: Hơn nữa , anh ấy còn rất chăm chỉ
examples.en: He is very intelligent. Moreover, he is also very hardworking

examples.vi: Cô ấy nói tiếng
examples.en: She speaks Vietnamese.

examples.vi: Anh rất tốt.
examples.en: You are very kind.

examples.vi: Hơn nữa , cô ấy còn nói tiếng
examples.en: Moreover, she also speaks Japanese.

units.title: Besides (ngoài ra)
units.explanation: Ngoài ra means “moreover” or “besides/in addition.” It is used to introduce additional, separate information that might not directly support the previous statement but provides further context or variety. 1. Ngoài ra, Clause 2..

examples.vi: Tôi rất thích cà phê.
examples.en: I really like coffee.

examples.vi: Ngoài ra , tôi còn thích trà
examples.en: I really like coffee. In addition , I also enjoy tea

examples.vi: Học sinh cần làm bài tập về nhà.
examples.en: Students need to do homework.

examples.vi: Ngoài ra, họ nên đọc thêm sách để mở rộng kiến thức.
examples.en: Students need to do their homework. Besides , they should read more books to broaden their knowledge

examples.vi: Cô ấy rất giỏi nấu ăn.
examples.en: She is very good at cooking.

examples.vi: Ngoài ra , cô ấy cũng trang trí nhà rất đẹp
examples.en: She is very good at cooking. Moreover , she also decorates the house very beautifully

examples.vi: Đi bộ hàng ngày rất tốt cho sức khỏe.
examples.en: Walking every day is very good for health.

examples.vi: Ngoài ra, nó cũng giúp giảm
examples.en: Walking daily is very good for your health. In addition , it also helps reduce stress.

examples.vi: Món ăn này rất ngon.
examples.en: This dish is very delicious.

examples.vi: Ngoài ra , giá cả cũng rất hợp lý.
examples.en: This dish is very delicious. Besides , the prices are also very reasonable

units.title: In Addition (thêm vào đó)
units.explanation: Thêm vào đó means “in addition.” It is used to often emphasize that the new information is an extension or continuation of the previous point, adding value or relevance. 1. Thêm vào đó, Clause 2..

examples.vi: Thêm vào đó , anh ấy luôn đúng giờ.
examples.en: He works very hard. In addition, he is always punctual

examples.vi: Cô giáo giảng bài rất dễ hiểu.
examples.en: The teacher explains lessons very clearly.

examples.vi: Thêm vào đó , cô ấy rất kiên nhẫn với học
examples.en: The teacher explains lessons very clearly. In addition, she is very patient with students.

examples.vi: Quán cà phê này có không gian đẹp.
examples.en: This cafe has a beautiful space.

examples.vi: Thêm vào đó , đồ uống rất
examples.en: This

examples.vi: Anh ấy tập thể dục mỗi ngày.
examples.en: He exercises every day.

examples.vi: Thêm vào đó , anh ấy còn ăn uống lành mạnh.
examples.en: He exercises every day. In addition, he also eats healthily.

examples.vi: Cô ấy nói tiếng
examples.en: She speaks Vietnamese.

examples.vi: Anh rất tốt.
examples.en: You are very kind.

examples.vi: Thêm vào đó , cô ấy còn nói tiếng
examples.en: In addition, she also speaks Japanese.

modules.title: How to say "until" in Vietnamese
modules.description: In Vietnamese, the word “cho đến khi”, “đến khi”, “tới khi” are used to indicate a point in time up to which an action continues. Understanding how to use those expressions effectively will help enhance your conversations.

faqs.question: How to say until in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: The word “đến khi” is commonly used to express “until” in Vietnamese, indicating the point at which an action or situation will continue.

faqs.question: Can “đến khi” be used at the beginning?
faqs.answer: Yes, “đến khi” can be used at the start of a sentence to set a condition or timeframe, such as in “Đến khi anh về, em sẽ ở đây” (“Until you come back, I will be here”).

faqs.question: Any difference between “tới khi” and “đến khi”?
faqs.answer: No significant difference; both “tới khi” and “đến khi” mean “until” and are interchangeable. “Đến khi” may be slightly more common in usage.

faqs.question: Are there other expressions similar to “đến khi”?
faqs.answer: Yes, “cho đến khi” or simply “cho đến” can also be used, especially in more formal or literary contexts.

units.title: Until (cho đến khi)
units.explanation: “Cho đến khi” means “until” or “up until the point when.” It sounds slightly formal and emphasizes the continuation of an action or state up to a specific moment or condition.

examples.vi: Tôi sẽ chờ bạn cho đến khi bạn đến
examples.en: I will wait until you arrive

examples.vi: Họ sẽ làm việc, cho đến khi dự án hoàn thành
examples.en: They will work until the project is completed

examples.vi: Cô ấy học chăm chỉ, cho đến
examples.en: She studies hard until the exam is over

examples.vi: Chúng ta sẽ ở đây, cho đến khi trời tối
examples.en: We will stay here until it gets dark

examples.vi: Tôi sẽ đọc sách, cho đến khi cảm thấy buồn ngủ
examples.en: I will read a book until I feel sleepy

examples.vi: Chúng ta sẽ không rời đi, cho đến khi mọi người đến đủ
examples.en: We will not leave until everyone has arrived

examples.vi: Tôi sẽ làm bài tập, cho đến khi hoàn thành tất cả
examples.en: I will do my homework until I finish everything

examples.vi: Chúng ta sẽ không rời khỏi nhà, cho đến khi trời sáng
examples.en: We will not leave the house until it gets light

examples.vi: Anh ấy sẽ ăn kiêng, cho đến khi giảm được cân
examples.en: He will go on a diet until he loses weight

examples.vi: Tôi sẽ không ngủ, cho đến
examples.en: Khi xem

examples.en: I will do my homework until I finish everything

examples.en: We will not leave the house until it gets light

examples.en: Khi” is similar to “cho

examples.vi: Tôi sẽ chờ bạn đến khi bạn đến
examples.en: I will wait until you arrive

examples.vi: Họ sẽ làm việc, đến khi dự án hoàn thành
examples.en: They will work until the project is completed

examples.vi: Cô ấy học chăm chỉ, đến
examples.en: She studies hard until the exam is over

examples.en: I will read a book until I feel sleepy

examples.vi: Chúng ta sẽ không rời đi, đến khi mọi người đến đủ
examples.en: We will not leave until everyone has arrived

examples.vi: Tôi sẽ làm bài tập, đến khi hoàn thành tất cả
examples.en: I will do my homework until I finish everything

examples.vi: Chúng ta sẽ không rời khỏi nhà, đến khi trời sáng
examples.en: We will not leave the house until it gets light

examples.vi: Anh ấy sẽ ăn kiêng, đến khi giảm được cân
examples.en: He will go on a diet until he loses weight

examples.vi: Tôi sẽ không ngủ, đến
examples.en: Khi xem

units.title: Until (tới khi)
units.explanation: “ Tới khi ” also means “until,” and it indicates an endpoint for an action, often in a more casual, conversational tone.

examples.vi: Tôi sẽ chờ bạn tới khi bạn đến
examples.en: I will wait until you arrive

examples.vi: Họ sẽ làm việc, tới khi dự án hoàn thành
examples.en: They will work until the project is completed

examples.vi: Cô ấy học chăm chỉ, tới
examples.en: She studies hard until the exam is over

examples.vi: Chúng ta sẽ ở đây, tới khi trời tối
examples.en: We will stay here until it gets dark

examples.vi: Tôi sẽ đọc sách, tới khi cảm thấy buồn ngủ
examples.en: I will read a book until I feel sleepy

examples.vi: Chúng ta sẽ không rời đi, tới khi mọi người đến đủ
examples.en: We will not leave until everyone has arrived

examples.vi: Tôi sẽ làm bài tập, tới khi hoàn thành tất cả
examples.en: I will do my homework until I finish everything

examples.vi: Chúng ta sẽ không rời khỏi nhà, tới khi trời sáng
examples.en: We will not leave the house until it gets light

examples.vi: Anh ấy sẽ ăn kiêng, tới khi giảm được cân
examples.en: He will go on a diet until he loses weight

examples.vi: Tôi sẽ không ngủ, tới
examples.en: Khi xem

modules.title: "Must" & "have to" in Vietnamese
modules.description: Have to and must in Vietnamese are expressed using only one word: phải . We teach you how to use obligation in Vietnamese in the declarative, negative and interrogative form.

faqs.question: How to say must in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: In Vietnamese, there is only one word to express something that you must do or have to do. The word is “phải” . Subject + phải + verb Tôi phải đi học. I have to go to school Anh ấy phải đi làm.

faqs.question: He has to go to work How to say do not have to in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: You can add “không” before “phải” in order to turn the sentence in the negative form: Subject + không phải + verb Tôi không phải đi học. I don’t have to go to school Anh ấy không phải đi làm.

faqs.question: What do you have to do everyday?
faqs.answer: Tôi phải đi học mỗi ngày.

faqs.question: I have to go to school Is Vietnamese SVO or SOV?
faqs.answer: The Vietnamese language structure is SVO, meaning sentences are built according to Subject + Verb + Object. Learn more about the Vietnamese sentence structure here.

units.title: Must/Have To (phải)
units.pattern: Subject + phải + verb Tôi phải đi học I have to go to school Anh ấy phải đi làm He has to go to work Chị ấy phải ngủ 8
units.explanation: In Vietnamese, there is only one word to express something that you must do or have to do. The word is “phải” . tiếng mỗi ngày She has to sleep 8 hours everyday.

examples.vi: Chúng tôi phải nghỉ ngơi
examples.en: We have to take a rest

examples.vi: Em ấy phải ăn cơm lúc 7h00 tối
examples.en: She has to have dinner at 7pm

examples.vi: Anh phải học
examples.en: You must study Vietnamese.

examples.vi: Việt mỗi ngày
examples.en: You have to learn Vietnamese everyday

units.title: Must — Negative
units.pattern: Subject + không phải + verb Tôi không phải đi học I don’t have to go to school Anh ấy không phải đi làm He doesn’t have to go to work Chị ấy không phải ngủ 8

examples.vi: Chúng tôi không phải nghỉ ngơi
examples.en: We don’t have to take a rest

examples.vi: Em ấy không phải ăn cơm lúc 7h00 tối
examples.en: She doesn’t have to have dinner at 7pm

examples.vi: Anh không phải học
examples.en: You don't have to study Vietnamese.

examples.vi: Việt mỗi ngày
examples.en: You don’t have to learn Vietnamese everyday

units.title: What Must I Do?
units.pattern: Subject + phải + làm gì? Bạn phải làm gì mỗi ngày? Tôi phải đi học mỗi ngày What do you have to do everyday? I have to go to school Anh ấy phải làm gì mỗi ngày? Anh ấy phải đi làm mỗi ngày What does he have to do everyday? He has to go to work Chị ấy phải làm gì mỗi ngày? Chị ấy phải ngủ 8
units.explanation: This is the sentence structure to follow in order to make a question with “phải”: tiếng mỗi ngày What does she have to do everyday? She has to sleep 8 hours everyday.

examples.vi: Bạn phải làm gì mỗi ngày?
examples.en: What do you have to do every day?

examples.vi: Chúng tôi phải nghỉ ngơi mỗi ngày
examples.en: What do you have to do everyday? We have to take a rest

examples.vi: Em ấy phải làm gì lúc 7h00 tối?
examples.en: What does he/she have to do at 7pm?

examples.vi: Em ấy phải ăn cơm lúc 7h00 tối
examples.en: What does she have to do every 7pm? She has to have dinner at 7pm

examples.vi: Anh phải làm gì mỗi ngày?
examples.en: What do you have to do every day?

examples.vi: Anh phải học
examples.en: You must study Vietnamese.

examples.vi: Việt mỗi ngày
examples.en: What do I have to do everyday? You have to learn Vietnamese everyday

modules.title: How to say "used to" in Vietnamese
modules.description: In Vietnamese, expressing the idea of “used to” or past experiences is commonly done using structures like đã từng , từng , chưa từng , and chưa bao giờ . These expressions help convey whether someone has ever done something in the past or has never experienced it at all.

faqs.question: What is the difference between “đã từng” and “từng”?
faqs.answer: Both mean “used to” or “have ever,” but “đã từng” is slightly more formal and emphasizes the past experience, while “từng” is more neutral and commonly used in conversation.

faqs.question: Can I use “chưa từng” and “chưa bao giờ” interchangeably?
faqs.answer: Yes , they both mean “have never,” but “chưa bao giờ” is often stronger and more emphatic. “Chưa từng” is more concise and slightly more formal.

faqs.question: Where should “từng” or “đã từng” be placed in a sentence?
faqs.answer: They typically appear before the main verb. For example: Tôi đã từng sống ở Hà Nội (“I used to live in Hanoi”).

faqs.question: Is “đã từng” always necessary to talk about past experiences?
faqs.answer: No , sometimes just using “đã” + verb is enough in context. “Đã từng” adds emphasis that the experience is now in the past and may not happen again.

faqs.question: Can I use these expressions in both positive and negative sentences?
faqs.answer: Yes . You can say Tôi đã từng đi Đà Nẵng (I used to go to Da Nang) or Tôi chưa từng đi Đà Nẵng (I have never been to Da Nang).

units.title: Ever/Once (từng/đã từng)
units.explanation: The phrase từng/đã từng is used to express an action or state that happened in the past, but is no longer true or does not happen anymore . It corresponds to “ used to ” in English..

examples.vi: Tôi đã từng sống ở
examples.en: I used to live in Ha Noi.

examples.vi: Cô ấy đã từng là bạn thân của tôi
examples.en: She used to be my best friend

examples.vi: Họ đã từng đi bơi mỗi cuối tuần
examples.en: They used to go swimming every weekend

examples.vi: Chúng tôi đã từng ăn cùng nhau mỗi ngày
examples.en: We used to eat together every day

examples.vi: Tôi đã từng thăm ông bà mỗi mùa hè
examples.en: I used to visit my grandparents every summer

examples.vi: Anh ấy đã từng làm việc tại một ngân hàng
examples.en: He used to work at a bank

units.title: Never (chưa từng)
units.explanation: This pattern is used to express the negative form of “used to” meaning “did not use to…yet”..

examples.vi: Tôi chưa từng sống ở
examples.en: I have never lived in Ha Noi.

examples.vi: Cô ấy chưa từng là bạn thân của tôi
examples.en: She did not use to be my best friend.

examples.vi: Họ đã chưa đi bơi mỗi cuối tuần
examples.en: They did not use to go swimming every weekend.

examples.vi: Chúng tôi chưa từng ăn cùng nhau mỗi ngày
examples.en: We did not use to eat together every day

examples.vi: Tôi chưa từng thăm ông bà mỗi mùa hè
examples.en: I did not use to visit my grandparents every summer

examples.vi: Anh ấy chưa từng làm việc tại một ngân hàng
examples.en: He did not use to work at a bank

units.title: Have You Ever...? (đã từng...chưa?)
units.explanation: This pattern is used to express the yes/no question of “ Did you use to…/Have you ever done…yet? ” PATTERN #1 Subject + đã từng/đã bao giờ + verb + chưa? PATTERN #2 Yes: Rồi, subject + đã từng + verb PATTERN #3 Not yet: Chưa, subject + chưa từng/chưa bao giờ + verb.

examples.vi: Bạn đã từng đi Paris chưa ? –
examples.en: Have you ever been to Paris?

examples.vi: Rồi , tôi đã từng đi Paris rồi –
examples.en: Yes, I've been to Paris before.

examples.vi: Chưa, tôi chưa từng đi
examples.en: Paris Have you been to Paris? -Yes, I’ve already been to Paris -No, I’ve not been there yet

examples.vi: Anh đã từng yêu chưa ? –
examples.en: Have you ever been in love?

examples.vi: Rồi , anh từng yêu 3 người –
examples.en: Yes, I've been in love with 3 people.

examples.vi: Chưa , anh chưa bao giờ yêu ai
examples.en: Have you ever fallen in love with someone? -Yes, I used to love 3 girls -No, I’ve not ever loved anyone

examples.vi: Em đã từng thất nghiệp chưa ? –
examples.en: Have you ever been unemployed?

examples.vi: Rồi , em đã từng thất nghiệp –
examples.en: Yes, I was unemployed before.

examples.vi: Chưa , em chưa từng thất nghiệp
examples.en: Did you use to be unemployed? -Yes, I used to be unemployed -No, I’ve not ever been unemployed

examples.vi: Ba đã từng bơi chưa ? –
examples.en: Have you (Dad) ever swum?

examples.vi: Rồi , ba đã từng bơi 500m rồi –
examples.en: Yes, I once swam 500 meters.

examples.vi: Chưa , ba chưa bao giờ bơi
examples.en: Did you use to swim dad? -Yes, I use to swim 500m -No, I’ve never swum yet

examples.vi: Bạn đã bao giờ đi concert chưa ? –
examples.en: Have you ever been to a concert?

examples.vi: Rồi , tôi đi concert năm ngoái rồi –
examples.en: Yes, I went to a concert last year.

examples.vi: Chưa , tôi chưa bao giờ đi concert
examples.en: Have you been to any concert? -Yes, I went to one last year -No, I’ve not been yet

examples.vi: Chị ấy đã bao giờ nấu ăn chưa ? –
examples.en: Has she ever cooked?

examples.vi: Rồi , chị ấy đã từng nấu ăn cho 5 người mỗi ngày –
examples.en: Yes, she used to cook for 5 people every day.

examples.vi: Chưa , chị ấy chưa bao giờ nấu ăn
examples.en: Did she use to cook? -Yes, she used to cook for 5 people everyday -No, she’s never cooked yet

modules.title: "To try something" in Vietnamese
modules.description: In Vietnamese, expressing the concept of “ try ” can be done using various structures depending on the context, such as attempting something or trying for a specific purpose. Let’s explore how to use “try” in different ways in Vietnamese!

faqs.question: What’s the difference between “thử” and “cố gắng”?
faqs.answer: “Thử” means to try something out or test it, usually once or experimentally. “Cố gắng” means to make an effort or try hard to achieve something over time.

faqs.question: Can I use “thử” and “cố gắng” in the same sentence?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can. For example: Tôi sẽ cố gắng thử làm món này (“I’ll try my best to give this dish a try”).

faqs.question: Where does “thử” go in a sentence?
faqs.answer: “Thử” typically comes right before the verb it modifies. For example: Thử ăn món này đi! (“Try eating this dish!”).

faqs.question: Is “cố gắng” followed by any specific grammar structure?
faqs.answer: Yes , “cố gắng” is often followed by a verb or verb phrase, such as cố gắng học tiếng Việt (“try to study Vietnamese”).

faqs.question: Can “thử” be used in the past tense?
faqs.answer: Yes , you can use it with time indicators or context. For example: Tôi đã thử rồi (“I already tried it”).

units.title: Try + Verb (thử)
units.explanation: “ Thử ” is used when talking about trying something new or testing something. It suggests an attempt, often for the first time, to do something or experience something. PATTERN Thử + Verb.

examples.vi: Tôi sẽ thử làm chiếc bánh này
examples.en: I will try to make this cake

examples.vi: Cô ấy muốn thử nói tiếng
examples.en: She wants to try speaking Vietnamese.

examples.vi: Anh ấy thử chơi đàn
examples.en: He tried playing the piano

examples.vi: Chúng ta nên thử đi du lịch đến một quốc gia mới
examples.en: We should try traveling to a new country

examples.vi: Họ đang thử học tiếng
examples.en: They are trying to learn Japanese.

examples.vi: Tôi sẽ thử vẽ hôm
examples.en: I’m going to try painting today

units.title: Verb + Try (casual)
units.explanation: This structure is used to express the idea of t rying out an action or trying something with the intention of experiencing or testing it . It implies that the action is being attempted but not necessarily with high effort, and it is often for discovery or enjoyment. PATTERN Verb + Thử.

examples.vi: Tôi sẽ làm thử chiếc bánh này
examples.en: I will try to make this cake

examples.vi: Cô ấy muốn nói thử tiếng
examples.en: She wants to try speaking Vietnamese.

examples.vi: Anh ấy chơi thử đàn
examples.en: He tried playing the piano

examples.vi: Chúng ta nên đi du lịch thử đến một quốc gia mới
examples.en: We should try traveling to a new country

examples.vi: Họ đang học thử tiếng
examples.en: They are trying to learn Japanese.

examples.vi: Tôi sẽ vẽ thử hôm
examples.en: I’m going to try painting today

units.title: Make Effort (cố gắng)
units.explanation: This is the most common way to express “ try ” in the sense of making an effort or attempting to do something. It implies putting in effort toward achieving a goal. PATTERN Cố gắng + Verb.

examples.vi: Tôi sẽ cố gắng học chăm chỉ hơn.
examples.en: I will try to study harder

examples.vi: Cô ấy đang cố gắng hoàn thành dự án đúng hạn.
examples.en: She is trying to finish the project on time.

examples.vi: Anh ấy đang cố gắng học cách chơi guitar
examples.en: He is trying to learn how to play the guitar.

examples.vi: Chúng tôi đang cố gắng cải thiện kỹ năng của mình
examples.en: We are trying to improve our skills

examples.vi: Họ đang cố gắng thức dậy sớm mỗi ngày
examples.en: They are trying to wake up early every day

examples.vi: Tôi sẽ cố gắng đạt được mục tiêu này trong năm nay.
examples.en: I will try to reach the goal this year

modules.title: "The more...the more" in Vietnamese
modules.description: In Vietnamese, we use certain patterns to express the idea of “the more… the more.” This structure highlights a correlation between two actions or states, often showing an increase in one thing as another increases.

faqs.question: Can “càng…càng” be used with both verbs and adjectives?
faqs.answer: Yes , “càng…càng” can be used with both verbs and adjectives to express increasing intensity or correlation between two actions or states.

faqs.question: Is word order important when using “càng…càng”?
faqs.answer: Yes , the structure follows a clear pattern: càng + subject + verb/adjective, càng + subject + verb/adjective . Maintaining proper word order ensures clarity and correctness.

faqs.question: Can I omit the second subject in “càng…càng” sentences?
faqs.answer: Yes , if the subject remains the same in both clauses, you can omit the second one. For example: Càng học, càng giỏi.

faqs.question: What is the difference between “càng…càng” and “mỗi lúc một”?
faqs.answer: Both express intensification over time, but “càng…càng” highlights a correlation between two factors, while “mỗi lúc một” focuses on a single action or state increasing over time.

faqs.question: Can “càng…càng” be used in negative sentences?
faqs.answer: Yes , but it’s less common. You can express a decreasing trend or worsening condition, e.g., Càng nói, càng sai (“The more you speak, the more wrong you are”).

units.title: The More...The More
units.explanation: PATTERN Càng + [Verb], Càng + [Verb].

examples.vi: Em càng nghe nhạc, em càng có hứng thú làm bài
examples.en: The more you listen to music, the more excitement you have in doing homework

examples.vi: Anh càng quan tâm
examples.en: Em, em

examples.vi: Anh càng
examples.en: La em, em

examples.vi: Tôi càng không làm việc, tôi càng lười biếng
examples.en: The more I do nothing, the lazier I become

examples.vi: Tôi càng ăn mặn, tôi càng uống nước nhiều
examples.en: The more I eat salted, the more water I drink

units.title: More and More (càng ngày càng)
units.pattern: Càng ngày + càng + Adj (more and more)
units.explanation: PATTERN Càng + [Adjective], Càng + [Adjective].

examples.vi: Bạn càng già, bạn càng đẹp
examples.en: The older you get, the more beautiful you are

examples.vi: Trời càng nóng, tôi càng khó chịu
examples.en: The hotter the weather is, the more uncomfortable I am

examples.vi: Tôi càng bệnh, tôi càng mệt
examples.en: The more my sickness is, the more t ired I feel

examples.vi: Bạn càng lớn, bạn càng thông
examples.en: The older you get, the smarter you are

examples.vi: Bạn càng già, bạn càng yếu
examples.en: The older you get, the weaker you are

examples.vi: Bạn càng tức giận, bạn càng mệt
examples.en: The angrier you get, the more tired you feel

units.title: The More...The More (bao nhiêu...bấy nhiêu)
units.explanation: PATTERN Càng + [Adjective/Verb], Càng + [Adjective/Verb].

examples.vi: Càng học nhiều, điểm số càng tốt
examples.en: The olde r you get, the more you understand

examples.vi: Càng luyện tập nhiều, càng giỏi hơn
examples.en: The more you study, the better your grades will be

examples.vi: Càng lớn tuổi, càng hiểu biết nhiều hơn
examples.en: The more you practice, the better you become

examples.vi: Càng lạnh, càng cần áo ấm.
examples.en: The colder it gets, t he more we need warm clothes

examples.vi: Càng làm việc chăm chỉ, càng thành công hơn
examples.en: The harder you work, the more successful you will be.

examples.vi: Càng nghe nhạc nhiều, tôi càng thích nó hơn
examples.en: The more I listen to the music, the more I like it

modules.title: Making suggestions in Vietnamese
modules.description: In Vietnamese, making suggestions can be done using various expressions, such as …nhé! and Hay là…? . Understanding these patterns will help you communicate more effectively.

faqs.question: How to use nhé in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Adding “…nhé” at the end of a sentence softens the tone, making the suggestion sound friendly and inviting.

faqs.question: For example, “Chúng ta đi ăn nhé?
Let’s go eat, okay?
faqs.answer: ) feels gentle and polite.

faqs.question: How to use hay là in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: “Hay là…” is often used to offer an alternative or a suggestion.

faqs.question: For example, “Hay là chúng ta xem phim?
How about we watch a movie?
faqs.answer: ) allows for a choice and is casual and friendly.

faqs.question: How to use sao không in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: “Sao không…” (Why not…) suggests something that could or should be done, often to nudge someone toward an idea.

faqs.question: For instance, “Sao không đi dạo một chút?
Why not take a walk?
faqs.answer: ) gently encourages an action.

faqs.question: Is hay là suitable for both formal and informal?
faqs.answer: “Hay là…” is more casual and typically used in informal settings, like with friends or family. It may feel too familiar in formal contexts.

units.title: Softening with nhé!
units.explanation: “…nhé!” is often used at the end of a sentence to make a suggestion in a friendly manner, similar to “Let’s” in English. PATTERN Clause, nhé! Chúng ta đi xem phim nhé ! Let’s go watch a movie! Đi ăn pizza nhé ! Let’s eat pizza Học bài cùng nhau nhé ! Let’s study together Ba ăn tối với con nhé ! Let’s have dinner together Con đừng về muộn nhé ! Don’t come home late , okay Chúng ta đi ngủ nhé ! Let’s go to sleep.

examples.vi: Chúng ta đi
examples.en: Xem phim

examples.vi: Đi ăn pizza nhé !
examples.en: Let’s eat pizza

examples.vi: Học bài cùng nhau nhé !
examples.en: Let’s study together

examples.vi: Ba ăn tối với con nhé !
examples.en: Let’s have dinner together

examples.vi: Con đừng về muộn nhé !
examples.en: Don’t come home late , okay

examples.vi: Chúng ta đi ngủ nhé !
examples.en: Let’s go to sleep

modules.title: Expressing intentions in Vietnamese
modules.description: In Vietnamese, expressing intentions is essential for discussing plans, goals, and desires. Key expressions such as định (intend), lên kế hoạch (making plans), and sẽ (will) allow speakers to communicate their intentions with clarity and nuance.

faqs.question: How to use Định in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: “Định” means “to intend to” and is placed before a verb to indicate intention. For example, “Tôi định học tiếng Việt” (I intend to study Vietnamese).

faqs.question: What’s the difference between muốn and định ?
faqs.answer: “Muốn” expresses a desire (want) to do something, while “định” specifically indicates an intention or plan. For example, “Tôi muốn đi du lịch” (I want to travel) versus “Tôi định đi du lịch” (I intend to travel).

faqs.question: Can “sẽ” be used to express intention, or is it only for the future tense?
faqs.answer: “ Sẽ ” generally indicates a future action but can imply intent if the context is clear. However, “ định ” or “muốn” are more specific for intentional actions.

faqs.question: Can “đang định” be used to indicate an immediate intention?
faqs.answer: Yes, “đang định” implies an intention that you are actively considering or about to do soon. For example, “Tôi đang định gọi cho bạn” (I am about to call you).

units.title: Will (sẽ)
units.explanation: The word “sẽ” indicates a future action or intention. You can use it using the following structure:.

examples.vi: Tôi sẽ đi du lịch vào cuối tuần.
examples.en: I will travel this weekend

examples.vi: Ba tôi sẽ học tiếng
examples.en: My dad will learn Vietnamese.

examples.vi: Anh mỗi ngày.
examples.en: My father will study English everyday

examples.vi: Tôi sẽ mua sắm vào thứ
examples.en: I will go shopping on Saturday.

examples.vi: Bảy này.
examples.en: I will go shopping on this Saturday

examples.vi: Mẹ tôi sẽ
examples.en: Tham gia

examples.vi: Tôi sẽ đi du học năm sau

units.title: Intend To (định)
units.explanation: The word “định” suggests a planned action or intention. PATTERN Subject + định + verb Tôi định đi du lịch vào cuối tuần. I intend to travel this weekend Ba tôi định kinh doanh My father intends to open a business Tôi định đi công tác vào thứ Bảy này. units.title: Plan To (dự định)
units.explanation: “Dự định” means “plan to” and is used when a person or group has a clear plan or idea to accomplish something in the future. It’s similar to “intend to,” but with a slightly stronger sense of having thought about or planned for the action. PATTERN Subject + dự định + verb Tôi dự định đi du lịch vào cuối tuần. I plan to travel this weekend Cô ấy dự định học tiếng Anh She plans to study more English Chúng tôi dự định mở quán cà phê vào tháng tới. We plan to open a café next month. modules.title: Expressing surprise in Vietnamese
modules.description: In Vietnamese, expressing surprise is often vivid and emotional, using short exclamations that convey shock, disbelief, or amazement. This lesson will introduce common phrases like Thật à , Không ngờ , and Trời ơi to help you sound more natural in everyday conversations.

faqs.question: What is the difference between “Thật à?
faqs.answer: ” and “

faqs.question: Thật không?
Both express surprise, but “Thật à?
faqs.answer: ” is more neutral/informal and used in spoken

faqs.question: Vietnamese, while “Thật không?
faqs.answer: ” can carry more disbelief or request for confirmation. Is “Trời ơi!” considered rude or inappropriate? No, “Trời ơi!” is very common and expressive in daily conversation, but tone and context matter. Use it carefully in formal settings.

faqs.question: Can “Không ngờ” be used at the beginning of a sentence?
faqs.answer: Yes , “Không ngờ…” (I didn’t expect…) often starts a sentence to show surprise or shock about an outcome.

faqs.question: Are there any formal ways to express surprise in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: While most surprise expressions are informal, phrases like “Tôi thật sự ngạc nhiên” (I’m truly surprised) are more formal or written.

faqs.question: Can I combine these expressions in one sentence?
faqs.answer: Yes, native speakers often layer expressions for emphasis, like “Trời ơi, không ngờ luôn đó!” , especially in spoken language.

units.title: Really?? (thật á?)
units.explanation: “ Thật à? ” is used to express astonishment upon hearing something unexpected or shocking! It’s similar to “ Really? ” or “ Is that true? ” in English. PATTERN Thật à? + Clause.

examples.vi: Cô ấy có ba đứa con thật à?
examples.en: She has three kids, really ?

examples.en: Really?

examples.vi: Bạn sắp đi du học? Really ?
examples.en: You’re going to study abroad?

examples.vi: Bạn đã hoàn thành công việc sớm thế thật à?
examples.en: You finished the work so quickly, really ?

examples.vi: Thật à?
examples.en: Really?

examples.vi: Đây là lần đầu tiên bạn đến đây? Really ?
examples.en: This is your first time here?

examples.vi: Anh ta nói trôi chảy tiếng
examples.en: He speaks Vietnamese fluently.

examples.vi: Nhật thật à?
examples.en: He speaks Japanese fluently, really ?

units.title: OMG! (trời ơi!)
units.explanation: “ Trời ơi! ” expresses surprise, shock, or even exasperation. It’s equivalent to “ Oh my God! ” or “ Oh dear! ” in English and is widely used in informal contexts. PATTERN Trời ơi! + Clause.

modules.title: Expressing regret in Vietnamese
modules.description: In Vietnamese, there are few phrases to express regret or a sense of sorrow, wishing things were different, or acknowledging a missed opportunity. This lesson will show you how to express regret in Vietnamese.

faqs.question: Can I use “đáng lẽ ra” in both spoken and written Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Yes, “đáng lẽ ra” is used in both formal and informal contexts, but it’s more commonly found in reflective or narrative speech and writing to express regret or missed opportunities.

faqs.question: What’s the difference between “thật tiếc là” and “đáng lẽ ra”?
faqs.answer: “Thật tiếc là” simply expresses regret or sympathy over something unfortunate, while “đáng lẽ ra” suggests that something should have happened differently, implying a sense of missed obligation or expectation.

faqs.question: How do I use “biết vậy thì” in a sentence?
faqs.answer: “Biết vậy thì…” means “If I had known, then…” and is usually followed by a statement indicating what the speaker would have done instead. It expresses regret based on hindsight.

faqs.question: Are there other common phrases to express regret in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Yes, other expressions include giá mà (if only), ước gì (I wish), and lẽ ra (should have), all of which can express similar feelings of regret or missed chances.

faqs.question: What level of Vietnamese do I need to use these structures correctly?
faqs.answer: These expressions are generally suited for learners at the B1 (intermediate) level or above, as they require some familiarity with conditional and past narrative forms.

units.title: It's a Pity (thật tiếc là)
units.explanation: “Thật tiếc là” means “It’s a pity that” or “It’s unfortunate that.” It’s used to express disappointment about a situation that has happened or is currently happening. PATTERN Thật tiếc là + clause.

examples.vi: Thật tiếc là tôi không thể tham dự buổi tiệc.

examples.en: It’s unfortunate that he didn’t agree with this idea

examples.vi: Thật tiếc là chúng ta đã bỏ lỡ cơ hội đó.

units.title: If Only (giá như)
units.explanation: “Giá như” expresses deep regret about unrealized scenarios, often referring to the past. PATTERN Giá như + claus e.

units.title: Should Have (đáng lẽ ra)
units.pattern: Đáng lẽ ra + Subject + nên/đã + Verb
units.explanation: “Đáng lẽ ra” means “should have” or “ought to have,” highlighting regret for a missed responsibility or action. PATTERN Đáng lẽ ra + Clause.

modules.title: Expressing hope in Vietnamese
modules.description: In this lesson, you’ll learn how to express hope in Vietnamese using common phrases like hy vọng , mong , and more. These expressions are useful in both casual and formal situations to share your wishes, expectations, or optimism about the future.

faqs.question: What’s the difference between “hy vọng” and “mong”?
faqs.answer: “Hy vọng” is a more general and neutral way to express hope. While “mong” can sound slightly more formal or polite and is often used in expressions of wish or expectation.

faqs.question: Can “mong” be used with pronouns like “tôi” or “bạn”?
faqs.answer: Yes , “mong” can be used with personal pronouns. For example: Tôi mong bạn thành công (I hope you succeed).

faqs.question: Is there a grammatical structure I should follow when using “hy vọng”?
faqs.answer: Yes , “hy vọng” is often followed by rằng (that) or a clause, e.g., Tôi hy vọng rằng bạn sẽ đến sớm (I hope that you will come early).

faqs.question: Can “mong” be used in formal writing or letters?
faqs.answer: Absolutely . “Mong” is very common in formal or polite Vietnamese, especially in written requests or closing remarks in emails and letters, like Mong nhận được phản hồi sớm (Hope to receive your reply soon).

faqs.question: At what level should I learn to use “hy vọng” and “mong”?
faqs.answer: These expressions are suitable for A2 to B1 level learners, as they involve basic sentence structures and polite forms commonly used in everyday conversation.

units.title: Hope (hy vọng)
units.explanation: “Hy vọng” means “to hope.” It is commonly used in formal and informal settings to express a general wish or expectation for something to happen..

examples.vi: Tôi hy vọng ngày mai trời sẽ nắng.
examples.en: I hope it will be sunny tomorrow

examples.vi: Chúng tôi hy vọng dự án sẽ thành công.
examples.en: We hope the project will succeed Play Hy

examples.vi: Tôi hy vọng họ sẽ hiểu ý tôi.
examples.en: I hope they will understand my point. Play Hy

units.title: Hope/Wish (mong)
units.explanation: “Mong” expresses a more personal wish, often with a tone of politeness. People usually use “mong” to express their needs or requests to someone politely..

examples.vi: Tôi mong bạn sớm hồi âm.
examples.en: I hope you reply soon Mong

examples.vi: Anh ấy mong được gặp lại cô ấy.
examples.en: He hopes to see her again Mong

examples.vi: Chúng tôi mong chuyến đi sẽ thuận lợi.
examples.en: We hope the trip goes smoothly Mong

units.title: Pray/Wish (cầu mong)
units.explanation: “Cầu mong” is used to express deep hope or prayers, often in situations involving strong emotions, such as worry, gratitude, or love..

examples.en: We pray that everyone stays safe.

units.explanation: “ Ước” is often used to express wishes, either realistic or fantastical. It is commonly used in reflective or imaginative contexts..

modules.title: Probability in Vietnamese
modules.description: In this lesson, you will learn how to express probability in Vietnamese using common phrases such as hình như , có lẽ , chắc là , and có thể . These expressions help you talk about uncertainty, possibility, and likelihood in everyday situations.

faqs.question: What is the difference between “hình như” and “có lẽ”?
faqs.answer: Both express uncertainty, but hình như often implies personal perception or assumption, while có lẽ is a more neutral way to say “maybe” or “probably.”

faqs.question: Can I use “chắc là” and “có thể” interchangeably?
faqs.answer: Not exactly. Chắc là implies higher certainty (“surely”), while có thể suggests possibility without commitment (“might”).

faqs.question: Where do these expressions go in a sentence?
faqs.answer: They typically appear at the beginning or before the verb phrase: e.g., Có lẽ anh ấy chưa đến. (“Maybe he hasn’t arrived.”)

faqs.question: Are these expressions formal or informal?
faqs.answer: They are generally neutral and appropriate in both casual and polite contexts, depending on tone and situation.

faqs.question: How can I practice using these expressions naturally?
faqs.answer: Try making predictions about current events or guessing what might happen using these structures, and get feedback from native speakers or a teacher.

faqs.question: Can I go study in Vietnam with ?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can! You can study with us in Saigon or Hanoi, in both group or individual classes. You’ll even have the opportunity to live with a Vietnamese family, thanks to our homestays .

units.title: Might (có thể)
units.explanation: “Có thể” is used to express possibility or probability in the present or future..

examples.vi: Cô ấy có thể trở thành một bác sĩ giỏi.

examples.vi: Họ có thể đến vào chiều

examples.en: He probably doesn’t want to participate

units.title: Apparently (hình như)
units.explanation: “Hình như” means “it seems” or “apparently,” indicating speculation based on observations or limited evidence. PATTERN Hình như + Clause.

examples.vi: Hình như anh ấy đang buồn.
examples.en: It seems he’s feeling sad

units.explanation: “Có lẽ” is used to express moderate probability or speculation..

modules.title: Purpose of action in Vietnamese
modules.description: In this lesson, you’ll learn how to express the purpose of an action in Vietnamese using common structures like để , nhằm , and vì . These words help explain why someone does something and are essential for forming clear, goal-oriented sentences.

faqs.question: What’s the difference between “để” and “nhằm” when expressing purpose?
faqs.answer: “Để” is commonly used in both spoken and written language to express purpose. “Nhằm” is more formal and often appears in written or official contexts.

faqs.question: Can I use “vì” and “để” interchangeably?
faqs.answer: No. “Vì” introduces the reason or cause of an action, while “để” shows the purpose or intended result. They are not interchangeable.

faqs.question: Where in the sentence should “để” or “nhằm” be placed?
faqs.answer: They are typically placed before a verb or verb phrase to introduce the purpose, e.g., Tôi học tiếng Việt để giao tiếp tốt hơn (I study Vietnamese to communicate better).

faqs.question: Is it okay to omit “để” in casual speech?
faqs.answer: While native speakers might occasionally drop “để” in very casual or rushed speech, it’s generally better for learners to use it clearly to avoid confusion.

faqs.question: What’s the difference between “vì muốn” and “để”?
faqs.answer: “Vì muốn” introduces a personal motivation ( because [I] want to… ), while “để” focuses on the intended goal of the action.

extracted_patterns: Subject + Verb + vì + Noun/ Purpose Clause

units.title: In Order To (để)

modules.title: Expressing potential in Vietnamese
modules.description: In this lesson, you will learn how to express potential in Vietnamese using phrases like “có thể” , “có cơ hội” , and “có khả năng” . These expressions help you talk about possibilities, opportunities, and abilities in different contexts.

faqs.question: What is the difference between “có thể” and “có khả năng”?
faqs.answer: “Có thể” is often used to express general possibility or permission, while “có khả năng” emphasizes someone’s ability or potential to do something.

faqs.question: Can I use “có cơ hội” in place of “có thể”?
faqs.answer: No, “có cơ hội” refers to having an opportunity, not just the possibility or ability to do something. It’s more context-dependent.

faqs.question: Is “có khả năng” formal or casual?
faqs.answer: “Có khả năng” is more formal and often used in written or professional contexts, while “có thể” is more common in everyday conversation.

faqs.question: How do I negate these expressions of potential?
faqs.answer: You can negate them by adding “không” in front, such as “không thể” (cannot), “không có khả năng” (lack the ability), or “không có cơ hội” (no opportunity).

faqs.question: Can these expressions be used with both verbs and nouns?
faqs.answer: Yes, especially “có thể” and “có khả năng” can be followed by verbs ( có thể đi – can go) or noun clauses ( có khả năng thành công – potential for success).

units.title: Can (có thể)
units.explanation: “Có thể” is used to express possibility or probability in the present or future..

examples.vi: Cô ấy có thể trở thành một bác sĩ giỏi.
examples.en: She can become a good doctor

examples.vi: Dự án này có thể thành công.
examples.en: This project can succeed

examples.vi: Tôi có thể hoàn thành công việc trước thời hạn.
examples.en: I can finish the work ahead of schedule

examples.vi: Trẻ em có thể học rất
examples.en: Children can learn very quickly

examples.vi: Họ có thể đến vào chiều nay.
examples.en: They can come this afternoon

units.title: Be Capable Of (có khả năng)
units.explanation: “Có khả năng” is used to emphasize someone’s or something’s capability or ability..

examples.vi: Dự án này có khả năng thành công
examples.en: This project has a high potential for success

examples.vi: Cô ấy có khả năng thuyết phục người khác.
examples.en: She has the ability to persuade others

examples.vi: Chúng ta có khả năng hoàn thành mục tiêu.
examples.en: We have the potential to achieve the goal

examples.vi: Trẻ em có khả năng thích
examples.en: Children have the potential to adapt quickly

examples.vi: Công nghệ này có khả năng thay đổi thế giới.
examples.en: This technology has the potential to change the world

units.title: Have the Opportunity (có cơ hội)
units.explanation: “Có cơ hội” is used to express the opportunity of someone to do something..

examples.vi: Cô ấy không có cơ hội để giải thích.
