modules.title: Expressing contrast in Vietnamese
modules.description: In Vietnamese, expressing contrast helps you show differences between ideas, actions, or situations. This lesson will teach you how to use structures like “mặc dù” , “trong khi” , and “ngược lại” to clearly communicate opposition or comparison in your sentences.

faqs.question: Can I use “mặc dù” and “nhưng” in the same sentence?
faqs.answer: Yes . While “mặc dù” introduces the contrast, “nhưng” can be added in the second clause to emphasize it, similar to “although… but…” in English. For example: Mặc dù trời mưa, nhưng tôi vẫn đi học.

faqs.question: What’s the difference between “trong khi” and “ngược lại”?
faqs.answer: “Trong khi” means “while” and is used to contrast two simultaneous actions or situations. “Ngược lại” means “on the contrary” or “in contrast” and introduces an opposite idea or result.

faqs.question: Is “mặc dù” formal or informal?
faqs.answer: “Mặc dù” is neutral and commonly used in both spoken and written Vietnamese. It is slightly more formal than “dù” but both are acceptable in most contexts.

faqs.question: Can I start a sentence with “ngược lại”?
faqs.answer: Yes . “Ngược lại” can begin a sentence to introduce a contrasting idea. Example: Tôi thích ở nhà. Ngược lại, anh ấy thích đi chơi.

faqs.question: Are there other ways to express contrast in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Yes. Besides “mặc dù”, “trong khi”, and “ngược lại”, you can also use “tuy nhiên” (however), “dù sao đi nữa” (anyway), and “trái lại” (conversely) depending on the tone and context.

units.title: Even Though (mặc dù...nhưng)
units.explanation: “ Mặc dù… nhưng… ” is similar to “ Tuy… nhưng… ” and means “ Even though… but… ” It emphasizes the unexpected result or contradiction. PATTERN Mặc dù + clause, nhưng + contrast clause.

examples.vi: Mặc dù trời mưa nhưng chúng tôi vẫn đi ra ngoài
examples.en: Even though it is raining, we still go out

examples.vi: Mặc dù anh ấy trẻ, nhưng anh ấy rất thông minh.
examples.en: He is young, but he is very smart

examples.vi: Mặc dù cô ấy mệt, nhưng cô ấy vẫn tiếp tục làm việc
examples.en: Even though she is tired, she continues working.

examples.vi: Mặc dù bộ phim dài, nhưng nó rất thú vị.
examples.en: The movie is long, but it is interesting.

examples.vi: Mặc dù thời tiết nóng, nhưng chúng tôi vẫn đi leo núi
examples.en: Even though the weather is hot, we go hiking

examples.vi: Mặc dù anh ấy không thích cà phê, nhưng anh ấy uống mỗi sáng
examples.en: He doesn’t like coffee, but he drinks it every morning.

units.title: On the Contrary (ngược lại)
units.explanation: “ Ngược lại ” means “ on the contrary ” and is used to introduce an opposite idea or result. PATTERN Clause, ngược lại + contrast clause.

examples.vi: Tôi nghĩ cô ấy buồn, ngược lạ i, cô ấy lại
examples.en: I thought she was sad, but on the contrary , she was happy

examples.vi: Anh ấy trượt kỳ thi, ngược lại, bạn anh ấy đậu
examples.en: He failed the test, on the contrary , his friend passed it.

examples.vi: Hôm qua thời tiết lạnh, ngược lại , hôm nay lại ấm
examples.en: The weather was cold yesterday, but today it is warm, on the contrary.

examples.vi: Cô ấy trầm lặng, ngược lại, chị của cô ấy rất hay nói
examples.en: She is quiet, on the contrary, her sister is very talkative.

examples.vi: Anh ấy nhút nhát, nhưng bạn anh ấy, ngược lại , rất hướng ngoại.
examples.en: He is shy, but his friend, on the contrary , is outgoing.

units.title: While (trong khi)
units.explanation: “ Trong khi ” means “ while ” and is used to express contrast between two simultaneous actions or states. PATTERN Trong khi + clause, contrast clause.

examples.en: Trong khi

examples.vi: Cô ấy đang làm việc,
examples.en: Trong khi em trai

examples.vi: Anh ấy thích cà phê,
examples.en: T rong khi

examples.vi: Tôi đang nấu bữa tối,
examples.en: Trong khi

examples.vi: Họ đang chờ xe buýt,
examples.en: Trong khi

modules.title: Giving advice in Vietnamese
modules.description: Giving advice is a common part of everyday conversation in Vietnamese. In this lesson, you’ll learn how to use expressions like khuyên , thử , and nghĩ to offer suggestions, make recommendations, and gently guide others in a natural and culturally appropriate way.

faqs.question: What’s the difference between “khuyên” and “góp ý”?
faqs.answer: Khuyên is used for giving advice with the intent to help or guide someone, often from a position of more experience. Góp ý is more about offering suggestions or constructive feedback, often in a neutral or collaborative tone.

faqs.question: Can I use “nên” instead of “khuyên” when giving advice?
faqs.answer: Yes. Nên is often used directly in sentences to suggest what someone should do (e.g. Bạn nên nghỉ ngơi. ). Khuyên typically introduces the act of advising (e.g. Tôi khuyên bạn nên nghỉ ngơi. ).

faqs.question: Is “thử” always used to suggest something?
faqs.answer: Not always. Thử literally means “to try.” It can be used to suggest trying something ( Bạn thử làm cái này đi. ), but can also describe an attempt ( Tôi thử học tiếng Việt. ).

faqs.question: How do I soften my advice to make it sound more polite?
faqs.answer: You can add softeners like nhé , xem sao , or use a questioning tone (e.g.

faqs.question: Bạn thử làm như vậy xem sao nhé?
faqs.answer: ) to avoid sounding too direct.

faqs.question: When should I use “nghĩ là” in giving advice?
faqs.answer: Nghĩ là (I think that…) introduces a personal opinion and is often used to gently suggest an action, especially when you don’t want to impose (e.g. Tôi nghĩ là bạn nên nói chuyện với cô ấy. ).

units.title: I Think You Should...
units.explanation: This is one of the most direct ways to offer advice in Vietnamese. It translates to “I think you should…” and is followed by the verb in its root form . This phrase is polite and widely used..

examples.vi: Tôi nghĩ bạn nên quên anh ấy đi vì anh ấy không yêu bạn đâu!
examples.en: I think you should forget him because he doesn’t love you!

examples.vi: Tôi nghĩ bạn nên cố gắng hơn
examples.en: I think you should try harder.

examples.vi: Tôi nghĩ anh ấy nên nói xin lỗi và giải thích tất cả cho bạn
examples.en: I think he should apologize and explain everything to you.

examples.vi: Anh ấy nghĩ bạn nên
examples.en: Xem anh

examples.en: My mom thinks I should go to college first, then work.

examples.vi: Ba nghĩ mẹ nên bình tĩnh trước khi nói
examples.en: My dad thinks my mom should calm down before speaking.

units.title: You Should Try...
units.explanation: This phrase suggests that the other person should give something a try . It is more casual and often used when offering suggestions or alternatives..

examples.vi: Bạn cô đơn.
examples.en: You are lonely.

examples.vi: Bạn thử yêu xem sao?
examples.en: You are lonely. You should try loving someone.

units.title: To Recommend (khuyên)
units.explanation: This phrase is a more formal way to give advice. It is suitable for serious or professional contexts.

examples.vi: Chị khuyên em đi học đi!
examples.en: I advise you to go to school!

examples.vi: Anh khuyên em nên có ước mơ của riêng mình!
examples.en: I advise you to have your own dreams!

examples.en: They advise me to marry him.

modules.title: How to order food in Vietnamese
modules.description: When dining in Vietnam, knowing how to order food and ask for the bill is essential for a smooth experience. In this lesson, we’ll go over the most common phrases and structures used when ordering at a restaurant in Vietnam.
modules.main_pattern: Cho + 1st pronoun + counter + name of the dish or drink

faqs.question: What is the most polite way to order food in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: The most polite way is to use “Cho tôi…” (Give me…) or “Tôi muốn…” (I would like…). You can also add “làm ơn” (please) to sound more courteous.

faqs.question: You can say: “Cho tôi xem thực đơn được không?
faqs.answer: ” which means “

faqs.question: May I see the menu?
How do I ask for the bill at a restaurant?
faqs.answer: To ask for the bill, simply say: “Tính tiền, nhé!” or more politely, “Làm ơn cho tôi xin hóa đơn.”

faqs.question: What’s the difference between “món này” and “cái này” when ordering?
faqs.answer: “Món này” refers to this dish (used specifically for food), while “cái này” means this thing in general and is less specific when talking about food.

faqs.question: Is it okay to point at the menu when ordering?
faqs.answer: Yes, it’s quite common and acceptable to politely point at the menu while saying the name of the dish or “Tôi muốn món này.”

extracted_patterns: Cho + 1st pronoun (tôi, em, con, anh, chị, etc) + counter + name of the dish or drink

units.title: Give Me... (cho tôi)
units.explanation: To order something, we often start with “Cho tôi” , which means “Give me” . After that, you can mention the number , counter (like a unit for measuring items), and the name of the dish or drink . 1st pronoun (tôi, em, con, anh, chị, etc) + counter + name of the dish or drink.

units.explanation: You might want to know how much your meal costs or ask for the bill. Here are some helpful phrases: Cái này bao nhiêu tiền? How much is it? Tính tiền! Bill, please Tôi trả bằng tiền mặt I pay in cash Tôi trả bằng chuyển khoản / thẻ I pay by bank account / card.

units.title: Dine In or Take Away
units.pattern: Ăn ở đây hay mang về?
units.explanation: In Vietnamese restaurants, you’ll be asked whether you’re eating here or taking your food to go . Here are some common ways to express that: Ăn ở đây Eat here Mang về Take-away.

examples.vi: Ăn ở đây Eat here Mang về
examples.en: Eat here / Take-away

units.title: Ordering Food — Examples
units.explanation: View Dialogue 1 Customer: Chào anh! Cho tôi một bát phở bò và một ly cà phê sữa đá. (Hello! Give me one bowl of beef pho and one iced coffee with condensed milk.) Waiter: Dạ, anh muốn ăn ở đây hay mang về? (Yes, would you like to eat here or take it to go?) Customer: Ăn ở đây. (I’ll eat here.) Waiter: Vâng, anh phải đợi khoảng 10 phút. (Okay, you’ll have to wait about 10 minutes.) Customer: Cái này bao nhiêu tiền? (How much is this?) Waiter: Tổng cộng là 80.000 đồng. (The total is 80,000 VND.) Customer: Tính tiền cho tôi nhé. (Bill, please.) Waiter: Dạ, cảm ơn anh. (Okay, thank you.) View Dialogue 2 Customer: Chào cô! Cho tôi một suất cơm gà và một chai nước suối. (Hello! Give me one serving of chicken rice and one bottle of mineral water.) Vendor: Anh muốn ăn ở đây hay mang về? (Do you want to eat here or take it to go?) Customer: Mang về. (Take it to go.) Vendor: Cái này bao nhiêu tiền? (How much is this?) Customer: Tính tiền luôn nhé! (Please give me the bill!) Vendor: Dạ, anh phải đợi khoảng 5 phút nữa. (Okay, you’ll have to wait about 5 more minutes.) Customer: Phải đợi bao nhiêu phút? (How many minutes do I have to wait?) Vendor: Khoảng 5 phút nữa thôi. (About 5 minutes more.) Customer: Tôi sẽ đợi. Cảm ơn! (I’ll wait. Thank you!).

modules.title: Adjective Clause with "Đến mức" in Vietnamese
modules.description: In this lesson, you’ll learn how to use “đến mức” in Vietnamese to express the extent or result of an action or quality — similar to “so… that” or “to the point that” in English. This structure helps add depth and clarity when describing intensity or consequences.

faqs.question: What does “đến mức” mean in Vietnamese grammar?
faqs.answer: “Đến mức” means “to the extent that” or “so…that” and is used to express the degree or result of an action or description.

faqs.question: How is “đến mức” different from “quá… đến nỗi”?
faqs.answer: While both indicate result or extent, “quá… đến nỗi” emphasizes excessiveness, whereas “đến mức” can describe both moderate and extreme degrees more neutrally.

faqs.question: Can I use “đến mức” in both spoken and written Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Yes, “đến mức” is appropriate in both contexts and is commonly used in formal and informal situations to show consequence or intensity.

faqs.question: Where does “đến mức” appear in the sentence?
faqs.answer: “Đến mức” typically follows an adjective or verb phrase and precedes a clause or descriptive phrase showing the outcome or degree.

faqs.question: Are there synonyms for “đến mức” in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Yes , phrases like “tới mức” or “đến nỗi” can be used similarly, though they may differ slightly in tone or emphasis depending on context.

units.title: So...That (đến mức)
units.explanation: The phrase “ đến mức “ in Vietnamese is used to indicate the extent or degree of something . It is similar to “to the point that” or “to the extent that” in English. This structure helps add depth and emphasis when describing situations or characteristics..

levels.label: Intermediate
levels.description: Advanced grammar — passive voice, nominalization, quotation, nuanced expressions, and complex clause structures.

modules.title: Nominalization in Vietnamese
modules.description: Nominalization (danh từ hóa) helps transform actions and qualities into abstract nouns , making your sentences sound more natural and academic. In this lesson, you’ll learn how to use nominalization correctly and effectively in Vietnamese! There are two main ways to create nominalization in Vietnamese:

faqs.question: What is nominalization in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Nominalization is the process of turning verbs or adjectives into nouns using words like sự , việc , or chuyện , allowing abstract ideas or actions to be expressed as nouns.

faqs.question: When should I use “sự” instead of “việc” or “chuyện”?
faqs.answer: Use sự for formal, abstract nouns (e.g. sự thành công – success), while việc refers more to actions or tasks ( việc học – the act of studying), and chuyện is used for events or stories, often informal.

faqs.question: Can I use “sự” before any verb?
faqs.answer: Not all verbs can be nominalized with sự . It typically applies to Sino-Vietnamese words and more formal verbs. Native speakers may not use it with purely native or colloquial words.

faqs.question: Is there a difference between “việc học” and “sự học”?
faqs.answer: Yes. Việc học emphasizes the activity of studying, while sự học is more abstract and formal, referring to the concept or process of education in a broader sense.

faqs.question: Is nominalization common in spoken Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Việc and chuyện are quite common in everyday speech, while sự appears more frequently in written or formal contexts like news, reports, or academic texts.

units.title: Nominalization with sự
units.explanation: The word “sự” is often used to nominalize adjectives and some verbs , making them function as nouns. PATTERN Sự + Verb/Adjective → Noun.

modules.title: Passive voice in Vietnamese
modules.description: Vietnamese speakers usually use a positive passive voice when they want to stay humble and show their respect to the other person or to express their honor by doing something. We guide you through the structure and usages of được and bị , helping you express actions from a recipient’s perspective.

faqs.question: How to form the passive voicce in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: The passive voice is typically formed using “bị” (when the action has a negative outcome) or “được” (when the action has a positive or neutral outcome) before the main verb. For example, “Tôi bị mắng” (I was scolded) and “Tôi được khen” (I was praised).

faqs.question: Should I use “bị” or “được” in the passive voice?
faqs.answer: Use “bị” when the action has an undesirable effect on the subject (e.g., “Tôi bị mất ví” – “I had my wallet stolen”). Use “được” when the action benefits the subject or is neutral (e.g., “Tôi được tăng lương” – “I got a raise”).

faqs.question: Is the passive voice commonly used?
faqs.answer: The passive voice is less common in Vietnamese compared to English. Active voice is preferred unless it’s necessary to focus on the subject receiving the action.

faqs.question: Can passive be used with all verbs?
faqs.answer: No, the passive voice is generally used with transitive verbs (verbs that can take a direct object). Not all verbs in Vietnamese can form a passive sentence.

extracted_patterns: Subject + không được + Verb + bởi + someone/something
Subject + không bị + Verb + bởi + someone/something

modules.title: Quoting someone in Vietnamese
modules.description: In Vietnamese, quoting can be done either directly or indirectly, similar to how it is in English. This lesson will explain how to form both direct and indirect quotes, highlighting the differences in structure and word choice.

faqs.question: How do you form direct quotes in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Direct quotes are enclosed in quotation marks, and the speaker’s exact words are used without changes. For example: “Anh ấy nói, ‘Tôi sẽ đi làm sớm.'”

faqs.question: What difference between direct and indirect?
faqs.answer: Direct quoting uses the speaker’s exact words, while indirect quoting paraphrases the statement, often involving changes in tense, pronouns, or sentence structure. For example: “Anh ấy nói rằng anh ấy sẽ đi làm sớm.”

faqs.question: Do quotation marks get used in indirect quotes in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: No, quotation marks are only used in direct quotes. Indirect quotes do not require quotation marks and often start with a reporting verb like “nói rằng” (said that).

faqs.question: Can indirect quotes omit the word “rằng” in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Yes, in informal speech, “rằng” (that) can sometimes be omitted in indirect quotes. For example, “Anh ấy nói anh ấy sẽ đến” (He said he would come) can omit “rằng.”

extracted_patterns: Subject + quoting words + rằng (that) + speech clause

units.title: Direct Quoting
units.explanation: Direct quoting is the practice of reproducing someone’s exact words or statements, typically enclosed in quotation marks. In writing, this allows you to present another person’s speech, writing, or thoughts verbatim, without altering their meaning or form..

examples.vi: Nói rằng:
examples.en: “…” Say → Say that : “…”

examples.vi: Viết rằng:
examples.en: “…” Write→ Write that: “…”

examples.vi: Nghĩ rằng:
examples.en: “…” Think → Think that : “…” La → La

examples.vi: Hát rằng:
examples.en: “…” Sing → Sing that : “…” “Anh

examples.vi: Anh ấy nói rằng : “Anh yêu em” “I love you” → He said: “I love you” “Con đói”
examples.en: He said: "I love you."

examples.vi: Cô ấy viết rằng : “Con đói” “I am hungry” → She wrote: “I am hungry” “Buồn quá!”
examples.en: She wrote: "I am hungry."

examples.vi: Anh ấy nghĩ rằng: “Buồn quá!” “So sad” → He thought: “So sad” “Yêu đời quá!”
examples.en: He thought: "So sad!"

examples.vi: Cô ấy hát rằng: “Yêu đời quá”
examples.en: “I love this life” → She sang: “I love this life”

units.title: Indirect Quoting
units.pattern: Subject + quoting words + rằng (that) + speech clause
units.explanation: Indirect quoting, also known as indirect speech or reported speech, is when you paraphrase someone’s words rather than quoting them exactly. The original statement is rephrased, and quotation marks are not used. Indirect quotes often involve changes in tense or pronouns to fit the context..

examples.vi: Anh ấy nói rằng anh ấy yêu tôi
examples.en: He said that he loved me. (indirect)

examples.vi: Con gái viết rằng con đói
examples.en: She wrote that she was hungry. (indirect)

examples.vi: Anh ấy nghĩ rằng anh ấy buồn quá
examples.en: He thought that he was so sad. (indirect)

examples.vi: Cô ấy hát rằng cô ấy yêu đời quá
examples.en: “I love this life” → She sang that she loved this life

modules.title: Expressing hearsay in Vietnamese
modules.description: In Vietnamese, expressing hearsay or indirect information is essential for everyday communication, especially when you’re not the original source of the news. This lesson introduces common structures like nói rằng , nghe nói là , đồn rằng , and theo , helping you share what you’ve heard or read from others.

faqs.question: What’s the difference between “nghe nói là” and “đồn rằng”?
faqs.answer: “Nghe nói là” is often used when the speaker has personally heard something from someone, while “đồn rằng” implies that something is a rumor or gossip that is being spread around.

faqs.question: Can I use “nói rằng” in both written and spoken Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Yes , “nói rằng” is neutral and can be used in both contexts, though in casual conversation, people often omit “rằng” and use just “nói…” .

faqs.question: Is “theo” always followed by a source?
faqs.answer: Yes, “theo” is used to introduce the source of the information (e.g., “Theo báo Tuổi Trẻ…” ), similar to “according to” in English.

faqs.question: Are these hearsay expressions interchangeable?
faqs.answer: Not always. While they all relate to reporting information, they differ in tone and context. For example, “nghe nói” is more casual, while “nói rằng” is more formal or direct.

faqs.question: Can these structures be used to report opinions as well as facts?
faqs.answer: Yes , hearsay expressions in Vietnamese can be used to report both factual information and personal opinions, depending on the context.

units.title: I Heard That (nghe nói là)
units.explanation: “ Nghe nói là ” translates to “ I heard that ” and is used to introduce information obtained from someone else..

examples.vi: Nghe nói là công ty sẽ đóng cửa.
examples.en: I have been living here for 5 years.

units.title: Rumored That (đồn rằng)
units.explanation: “Đồn rằng” means “ it’s rumored that “ and is often used for unverified or widely spread information..

examples.vi: Đồn rằng cô ấy sẽ kết hôn vào tháng sau.
examples.en: It is rumored that she is getting married next month

examples.vi: Đồn rằng công ty sẽ đóng cửa.
examples.en: It is rumored that the company is going to close down

examples.vi: Đồn rằng họ sẽ chuyển đến thành phố khác
examples.en: It is rumored that they are moving to another city

examples.vi: Đồn rằng thời tiết ngày mai sẽ nắng.
examples.en: It is rumored that the weather will be sunny tomorrow

examples.vi: Đồn rằng anh ấy sẽ đi du học
examples.en: It is rumored that t he is going abroad for study

examples.vi: Đồn rằng họ đã giành chức vô địch.
examples.en: It is rumored that they won the championship

units.title: Said That (nói rằng)
units.explanation: This phrase highlights that the information comes from another person..

examples.vi: Cô ấy nói rằng cô ấy sẽ đến lúc 7 giờ tối.
examples.en: She said that she would arrive at 7 p.m.

examples.vi: Họ nói rằng họ sẽ đi biển vào cuối tuần này.
examples.en: They said that they are going to the beach this weekend.

examples.vi: Anh ấy nói rằng anh ấy cảm thấy tốt hơn bây giờ.
examples.en: He said that he is feeling better now.

examples.vi: Cô giáo nói rằng chúng ta cần học chăm chỉ hơn.
examples.en: The teacher said that we need to study harder.

examples.vi: Mẹ tôi n ói rằng bữa tối sẽ sẵn sàng sớm.
examples.en: My mom said that dinner will be ready soon.

examples.vi: Họ nói rằng họ đang lên kế hoạch cho một chuyến đi nước ngoài.
examples.en: They said that they are planning a trip abroad.

modules.title: Expressing degree in Vietnamese
modules.description: In this lesson, you’ll learn how to express different degrees or intensities in Vietnamese using words like hết sức , rất , càng ngày càng , and quá . These expressions help you emphasize how strong, extreme, or gradual an action or quality is, allowing for more vivid and accurate communication.

faqs.question: What is the difference between “rất” and “hết sức”?
faqs.answer: Both mean “very,” but “hết sức” is more intense and formal than “rất” . “Hết sức” can translate to “ extremely ” or “utmost.”

faqs.question: Can I use “quá” in both positive and negative sentences?
faqs.answer: Yes , but “quá” often carries a negative tone (e.g., nóng quá! = “too hot!”), unless it’s clearly meant to emphasize excitement or praise.

faqs.question: How do I use “càng ngày càng”?
faqs.answer: “Càng ngày càng” means “more and more” or “increasingly.” It is used before an adjective or verb to show a growing degree over time (e.g., càng ngày càng đẹp = “more and more beautiful”).

faqs.question: Is it okay to combine these words in one sentence?
faqs.answer: Usually, no. Since each word already emphasizes degree, combining them (e.g., rất quá đẹp ) would sound unnatural. Use only one degree modifier per idea. Which of these words is used in formal vs. informal contexts? “Rất” and “quá” are used in both casual and formal situations. “Hết sức” is more formal, while “càng ngày càng” is neutral and suits all contexts.

units.title: A Little (hơi/một chút)
units.explanation: “H ơi / Một chút ” means “ a little ” or “ slightly ” and is used to indicate a mild or slight degree. PATTERN Hơi + Adjective Adjective + Một chút.

examples.vi: Món canh hơi mặn
examples.en: The soup is a little salty.

examples.vi: Hôm nay tôi cảm thấy hơi mệt
examples.en: I feel slightly tired today

examples.vi: Bộ phim này hơi buồn chán
examples.en: This movie is a bit boring

examples.vi: Thời tiết lạnh một chút
examples.en: The weather is a little cold.

examples.vi: Món ăn cay một chút
examples.en: The food is slightly spicy

examples.vi: Tôi lo lắng một chút
examples.en: I am a little bit nervous

units.title: More and More (càng ngày càng)
units.pattern: Càng ngày càng + Adjective (more and more)
units.explanation: “ Càng ngày càng ” means “ more and more ” and is used to express a progressive increase in degree or intensity over time. PATTERN Càng ngày càng + Adjective.

examples.vi: Thời tiết càng ngày càng lạnh
examples.en: The weather is getting colder and colder .

examples.vi: Thành phố càng ngày càng đông đúc.
examples.en: The city is becoming more and more crowded

examples.vi: Sức khỏe của anh ấy càng ngày càng tồi tệ
examples.en: His health is getting worse and worse

examples.vi: Anh của tôi càng ngày cà
examples.en: n g

examples.vi: Vấn đề càng ngày càng nghiêm trọng
examples.en: The problem is becoming more and more serious

examples.vi: Cuốn sách càng ngày càng thú vị khi tôi đọc
examples.en: The book is becoming more interesting as I read

units.title: Too (quá)
units.explanation: The structure “ quá ” is used to indicate that something is excessively or extremely high in degree. It is equivalent to “ too ” or “excessively” in English. PATTERN Quá + Adjective.

examples.vi: Cái áo này quá lớn đối với tôi.
examples.en: This shirt is too big for me

examples.vi: Món ăn quá
examples.en: The food is too spicy

examples.vi: Bây giờ đã quá muộn để ra ngoài
examples.en: It’s too late to go out now

examples.vi: Cô ấy quá mệt để tiếp tục làm việc
examples.en: She is too tired to continue working

examples.vi: Bộ phim quá dài.
examples.en: The movie is too long

examples.vi: Thời tiết hôm nay quá nóng
examples.en: The weather is too hot today

units.title: Very (rất)
units.explanation: “ Rất ” is used to indicate a high degree of a quality or action, similar to “ very ” or “ extremely ” in English. While not always excessive, it often emphasizes intensity. PATTERN Rất + Adjective.

examples.vi: Cái áo này rất lớn đối với tôi.
examples.en: This shirt is very big for me

examples.vi: Món ăn rất
examples.en: The food is very spicy

examples.vi: Bây giờ đã rất muộn để ra ngoài
examples.en: It’s very late to go out now

examples.vi: Cô ấy rất mệt để tiếp tục làm việc
examples.en: She is very tired to continue working

examples.vi: Bộ phim rất dài.
examples.en: The movie is very long

examples.vi: Thời tiết hôm nay rất nóng
examples.en: The weather is very hot today

units.title: Extremely (hết sức)
units.explanation: This phrase emphasizes maximum effort or degree in performing an action. PATTERN Adjective / Verb + hết sức Hết sức + Adjective/verb.

examples.vi: Cô ấy làm việc hết sức để hoàn thành dự án
examples.en: She worked very hard to finish the project.

examples.vi: Tôi sẽ hết sức giúp bạn
examples.en: I will try my best to help you.

examples.vi: Anh ấy đang học tiếng
examples.en: He is studying Vietnamese with all his effort.

examples.vi: Anh hết sức
examples.en: He is doing his best to learn English

examples.vi: Chúng ta cần phải hết sức cẩn thận.
examples.en: We need to be very careful.

examples.vi: Tôi sẽ ủng hộ bạn hết sức
examples.en: I will support you to the utmost

examples.vi: Họ đang cố gắng hết sức để giành chiến thắng trong cuộc thi
examples.en: They are trying their best to win the competition

modules.title: How to say "only" in Vietnamese
modules.description: In this lesson we will learn how to use chỉ and mỗi to express “only” in Vietnamese. Both are used to convey exclusivity or limitations, and need to be used correctly in a sentence. Let’s have a look together.

faqs.question: How to say only in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Only in Vietnamese can be expressed using the words chỉ and mỗi , as well as both words under the expression chỉ…mỗi… .

faqs.question: Love you only How to use “chỉ” in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: This is a general term used to indicate that something is limited or restricted. Subject + chỉ + verb/adjective Em chỉ ngủ khi trời sáng. I only sleep in the morning.

faqs.question: How to say if in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: To express if in Vietnamese you’ll need to use the words Nếu or Nếu như. Nếu/ Nếu như + Clause 1, Clause 2 Nếu anh không đến được, nhớ báo em nhá! If you can’t come, let me know! — Learn more about how to say if in Vietnamese here.

units.title: Only with chỉ
units.explanation: Only in Vietnamese can be expressed in three ways, the first one uses the word Chỉ . This is a general term used to indicate that something is limited or restricted. It is used with verbs and adjectives, and could be translated in English by “only”, “just”.

examples.vi: Em chỉ mệt khi chạy hơn
examples.en: 2km I am only tired after 2-km-running

examples.vi: Em chỉ chạy bộ
examples.en: Sau khi

units.title: Only/Each with mỗi
units.explanation: Mỗi is a second way to express only in Vietnamese, and is followed by nouns. PATTERN Mỗi (only) + noun (except for time and date) ăn mỗi cơm eat only rice uống mỗi nước drink only water thích mỗi anh love you only.

examples.vi: Mỗi ngày
examples.en: = every day ・

examples.vi: Mỗi tháng
examples.en: = every month ・

examples.vi: Mỗi năm
examples.en: = every year

units.title: Only...Each (chỉ...mỗi)
units.explanation: This phrase is used to emphasize a single item or person out of a larger set, often with a sense of exclusivity. PATTERN Chỉ + verb + mỗi + noun (except for time and date) chỉ ăn mỗi cơm eat only rice chỉ uống mỗi nước drink only water chỉ thích mỗi anh love you only.

examples.vi: Anh chỉ yêu mỗi em
examples.en: I only love you.

modules.title: How to say "instead" in Vietnamese
modules.description: To suggest an alternative or express doing one thing “instead” of doing another in Vietnamese, two terms are commonly used. In this lesson we teach you how to use “thay vì” and “thay vào đó” .
modules.main_pattern: Thay vì + clause A, clause B (alternative option)

faqs.question: How to say instead in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: To express doing one thing “instead” of doing another thing in Vietnamese is Thay vào đó . It is used to introduce an alternative or replacement action, often after stating what was done or considered. It’s like saying “in place of that” or “instead of that.” Clause A + Thay vào đó + clause B (alternative option) Anh ngủ ở nhà cả ngày rồi! Thay vào đó, anh nên chạy bộ 5 phút You’ve been sleeping all day!You should go running in 5 minutes instead !

faqs.question: How to say instead of in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: To express doing one thing “instead” of doing another in Vietnamese, the following structure is used. It introduces an alternative action or choice that replaces something else.

faqs.question: How to say if not in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: If not in Vietnamese is Nếu không . You need to use the following structure: Nếu không + Verb, Clause Nếu không thích, anh cứ về If (you) not like it, just leave — Learn more about if in Vietnamese here.

units.title: Instead (thay vì)
units.pattern: Thay vì + clause A, clause B (alternative option)
units.explanation: IN VIETNAMESE To suggest an alternative or express doing one thing “instead” of doing another in Vietnamese, two terms are commonly used. In this lesson we teach you how to use “thay vì” and “thay vào đó” . INSTEAD OF… To express doing one thing “instead” of doing another in Vietnamese, the following structure is used. It introduces an alternative action or choice that replaces something else. PATTERN Thay vì + clause A, clause B (alternative option) Thay vì chị chia tay anh ấy, chị nên lắng nghe anh ấy giải thích Instead of breaking up with him, you should listen to his explanation Thay vì ăn bánh mì, mình muốn ăn phở Instead of eating banh mi, I want to eat Pho Thay vì đi du lịch Nhật Bản, mình muốn đi du lịch Hàn Quốc Instead of traveling to Japan, I want to travel to Korea.

examples.vi: Anh ngủ ở nhà cả ngày rồi! Thay vào đó, anh nên chạy bộ 5 phút
examples.en: You’ve been sleeping all day!You should go running in 5 minutes instead !

examples.vi: Em xem phim 30 phút rồi! Thay vào đó, em học
examples.en: You've watched movies for 30 mins! Instead, go study Vietnamese.

examples.vi: Anh 30 phút
examples.en: You’ve been watching a movie in 30 minutes!You should study English in 30 minutes instead !

examples.vi: Đi chơi với anh chán quá! Thay vào đó, em thích ở nhà và
examples.en: Xem phim

examples.vi: Em không thích học đại học lúc 18 tuổi. Thay vào đó, em đi làm
examples.en: I don’t like going to university at 18. I work instead .

examples.vi: Bạn ngồi khóc làm gì! Thay vào đó, bạn nên đi ngủ
examples.en: You are crying for what?! You should sleep instead !

modules.title: How to say "even though" in Vietnamese
modules.description: The phrase “even though” is commonly expressed as a contrast between two clauses. The primary patterns used in Vietnamese for “even though” are “mặc dù,” “dù,” and “tuy…nhưng.” All these expressions indicate a contrast or concession.

faqs.question: How to say “even though” in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: In Vietnamese, “even though” can be expressed with phrases like mặc dù, dù, or tuy.

faqs.question: Can I use “mặc dù” and “dù” interchangeably?
faqs.answer: Yes, mặc dù and dù both mean “even though,” but mặc dù is more formal, while dù is often used in informal contexts.

faqs.question: How to say “with” in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: With in Vietnamese can be said by using multiple words. The most common sentence structures are: Subject 1 + Verb + với + Subject 2 Tôi về nhà với mẹ tôi. I come home with my mother someone, something “with” something: Subject 1 + Verb + cùng + Subject 2 Tôi về nhà cùng mẹ tôi. I come home with my mother Subject 1 + với (with) + Subject 2 + verb + cùng nhau (together) Tôi với mẹ về nhà cùng nhau . I come home together with my mom — Learn more about how to use with in Vietnamese here.

faqs.question: Is Vietnamese SVO or SOV?
faqs.answer: The Vietnamese language structure is SVO, meaning sentences are built according to Subject + Verb + Object.

faqs.question: Where to find more lessons like this?
faqs.answer: Check out our Vietnamese Grammar Bank , where you will find free lessons for levels A1 to B1. If you prefer to study from home, check out our online courses .

units.title: Although: mặc dù...nhưng
units.explanation: “Mặc dù…nhưng” emphasizes the contrast between two clauses, similar to “even though” or “despite” in English. It’s commonly used in both spoken and written language, especially in formal or official contexts. 1, nhưng + Clause 2 Mặc dù trời mưa, nhưng tôi vẫn đi ra ngoài Even though it rains, I still go outside Mặc dù cô ấy bận, nhưng cô vẫn giúp tôi Even though she is busy, she still helps me Mặc dù họ không thích món ăn này, nhưng họ vẫn ăn Even though they don’t like this dish, they still eat it Mặc dù anh ấy không khỏe, nhưng anh đi làm Even though he is not well, he goes to work Mặc dù trời lạnh, nhưng họ vẫn chơi bóng đá Even though it’s cold, they still play soccer Mặc dù việc khó khăn, nhưng tôi vẫn cố gắng Even though the task is difficult, I still try.

examples.vi: Mặc dù trời mưa, nhưng tôi vẫn đi ra ngoài
examples.en: Even though it rains, I still go outside

examples.vi: Mặc dù cô ấy bận, nhưng cô vẫn giúp tôi
examples.en: Even though she is busy, she still helps me

examples.vi: Mặc dù họ không thích món ăn này, nhưng họ vẫn ăn
examples.en: Even though they don’t like this dish, they still eat it

examples.vi: Mặc dù anh ấy không khỏe, nhưng anh đi làm
examples.en: Even though he is not well, he goes to work

examples.vi: Mặc dù trời lạnh, nhưng họ vẫn chơi bóng đá
examples.en: Even though it’s cold, they still play soccer

examples.vi: Mặc dù việc khó khăn, nhưng tôi vẫn cố gắng
examples.en: Even though the task is difficult, I still try

units.title: Although: dù...nhưng
units.explanation: “Mặc dù…nhưng” emphasizes the contrast between two clauses, similar to “even though” or “despite” in English. It’s commonly used in both spoken and written language, especially in formal or official contexts. 1, nhưng + Clause 2 Mặc dù trời mưa, nhưng tôi vẫn đi ra ngoài Even though it rains, I still go outside Mặc dù cô ấy bận, nhưng cô vẫn giúp tôi Even though she is busy, she still helps me Mặc dù họ không thích món ăn này, nhưng họ vẫn ăn Even though they don’t like this dish, they still eat it Mặc dù anh ấy không khỏe, nhưng anh đi làm Even though he is not well, he goes to work Mặc dù trời lạnh, nhưng họ vẫn chơi bóng đá Even though it’s cold, they still play soccer Mặc dù việc khó khăn, nhưng tôi vẫn cố gắng Even though the task is difficult, I still try DÙ … NHƯNG “Dù…nhưng” is often used interchangeably with “mặc dù” but is less formal. It conveys a similar idea of contrast and can be used more in a daily conversation.

examples.vi: Mặc dù trời mưa, nhưng tôi vẫn đi ra ngoài
examples.en: Even though it rains, I still go outside

examples.vi: Mặc dù cô ấy bận, nhưng cô vẫn giúp tôi
examples.en: Even though she is busy, she still helps me

examples.vi: Mặc dù họ không thích món ăn này, nhưng họ vẫn ăn
examples.en: Even though they don’t like this dish, they still eat it

examples.vi: Mặc dù anh ấy không khỏe, nhưng anh đi làm
examples.en: Even though he is not well, he goes to work

examples.vi: Mặc dù trời lạnh, nhưng họ vẫn chơi bóng đá
examples.en: Even though it’s cold, they still play soccer

examples.vi: Mặc dù việc khó khăn, nhưng tôi vẫn cố gắng
examples.en: Even though the task is difficult, I still try

examples.vi: DÙ …
examples.en: Even though...

examples.vi: NHƯNG “Dù…nhưng”
examples.en: Is often used interchangeably with

examples.vi: Dù +
examples.en: Clause 1,

examples.vi: Dù trời mưa, nhưng chúng tôi vẫn đi dạo
examples.en: Even though it rains, we still go for a walk

examples.vi: Dù không ngủ đủ, nhưng tôi vẫn cảm thấy tỉnh táo
examples.en: Even though I didn’t sleep enough, I still feel awake

examples.vi: Dù có căng thẳng, nhưng tôi vẫn giữ bình tĩnh
examples.en: Even though I’m stressed, I still remain calm

examples.vi: Dù là cuộc thi khó, nhưng tôi vẫn
examples.en: Even though it’s a difficult competition, I still participate

examples.vi: Dù trời lạnh, nhưng họ vẫn chơi bóng đá
examples.en: Even though it’s cold, they still play soccer

examples.vi: Dù việc khó khăn, nhưng tôi vẫn cố gắng
examples.en: Even though the task is difficult, I still try

units.title: Although: tuy...nhưng
units.explanation: “Tuy…nhưng” is typically used to imply that, despite a certain condition, an unexpected result follows. 1, nhưng + Clause 2 Tuy trời mưa, nhưng chúng tôi vẫn đi picnic Even though i t rains, we still go on a picnic Tuy anh ấy mệt, nhưng anh vẫn làm việc Even though he is tired, he is still working Tuy giá cao, nhưng sản phẩm này rất tốt Even though the price is high, this product is very good Tuy đường kẹt, nhưng họ vẫn đến đúng giờ.

examples.vi: Mặc dù trời mưa, nhưng tôi vẫn đi ra ngoài
examples.en: Even though it's hard, I don't give up.

modules.title: How to say "for now" in Vietnamese
modules.description: In Vietnamese, the phrase “tạm thời” effectively communicates temporary situations or decisions. It helps us express what is applicable at the moment but may change later.

faqs.question: How do you say for now in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: You can say “tạm thời” to indicate “for now” or “temporarily.” For example, “Tôi sẽ làm việc này tạm thời” means “I will do this for now.”

faqs.question: Can “tạm thời” be used with formal and informal?
faqs.answer: Yes, “tạm thời” is versatile and can be used in both formal and informal situations when referring to something temporary.

faqs.question: Is “tạm thời” used in everyday conversations?
faqs.answer: Yes, “tạm thời” is frequently used in everyday Vietnamese to refer to temporary situations or arrangements, making it a practical expression for learners.

faqs.question: How to say after all in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: In Vietnamese, “sau tất cả” or “rốt cuộc” can be used to mean “after all” when summing up or reaching a conclusion. — Learn more about how to say after all in Vietnamese here.

units.title: For Now (tạm thời)
units.explanation: The expression “ tạm thời ” is often used as an adverb to indicate something that is valid or applicable only for the time being. PATTERN Tạm thời, Subject + Verb.

examples.vi: Tạm thời, tôi sẽ ở lại đây
examples.en: For now, I will stay here

examples.vi: Tạm thời, chúng ta không cần lo lắng
examples.en: For now , we don’t need to worry

examples.vi: Tạm thời, cô ấy sẽ làm việc từ
examples.en: For now , she will work remotely

examples.vi: Tạm thời, bạn nên giữ bí mật
examples.en: For now, you should keep it a secret

examples.vi: Tạm thời, tôi không muốn nói chuyện với bạn
examples.en: For now , I don’t want to talk to you

examples.vi: Tạm thời, cậu có thể sử dụng máy tính của tôi
examples.en: For now , you can use my computer

units.title: Just for Now! (idiomatic)
units.explanation: The phrase “tạm thời là vậy!” can be added at the end of a statement to emphasize acceptance of a temporary situation. It’s similar to saying “that’s just how it is for now!” in English. PATTERN Statement + tạm thời là vậy! Tôi sẽ ở lại đây. Tạm thời là vậy!

examples.vi: Tôi sẽ ở lại đây.
examples.en: I will stay here for now.

examples.vi: Tạm thời là vậy!
examples.en: For now, I will stay here

examples.vi: Chúng ta không cần lo lắng.
examples.en: We don't need to worry for now.

examples.vi: Tạm thời là vậy!
examples.en: For now , we don’t need to worry

examples.vi: Cô ấy sẽ làm việc từ xa.
examples.en: She will work remotely for now.

examples.vi: Tạm thời là vậy!
examples.en: For now , she will work remotely

examples.vi: Bạn nên giữ bí mật.
examples.en: You should keep it a secret for now.

examples.vi: Tạm thời là vậy!
examples.en: For now, you should keep it a secret

examples.vi: Tôi không muốn nói chuyện với bạn.
examples.en: I don't want to talk to you for now.

examples.vi: Tạm thời là vậy!
examples.en: For now , I don’t want to talk to you

examples.vi: Cậu có thể sử dụng máy tính của tôi.
examples.en: You can use my computer for now.

examples.vi: Tạm thời là vậy!
examples.en: For now , you can use my computer

modules.title: Expressing "seem to be" in Vietnamese
modules.description: In Vietnamese, expressions like “hình như” , “dường như” , and “có vẻ như” are used to convey uncertainty or make assumptions, similar to “seem to be” in English. This lesson will show you how to use these structures naturally in different contexts.

faqs.question: What is the difference between “hình như” and “dường như”?
faqs.answer: Both mean “seem” or “apparently,” but hình như is more commonly used in spoken Vietnamese and can suggest a bit more uncertainty. Dường như tends to sound slightly more formal or literary.

faqs.question: Can I use “hình như” and “dường như” at the beginning of a sentence?
faqs.answer: Yes! Both expressions usually appear at the beginning of the sentence to indicate uncertainty or assumption, similar to “It seems that…” in English.

faqs.question: Are there other ways to say “seem to be” in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Yes. In addition to hình như and dường như , you can use có vẻ như (it appears that) or dường như là (it seems that) depending on the context and formality.

faqs.question: Is “hình như” used in questions?
faqs.answer: Yes, it can be. Using hình như in a question implies that the speaker is unsure and seeking confirmation, e.g.,

faqs.question: Hình như bạn chưa ăn sáng?
You haven’t eaten breakfast, have you?
Can these expressions be used with different tenses?
faqs.answer: Yes. Hình như , dường như , and có vẻ như are compatible with past, present, or future contexts. The time reference is understood from the rest of the sentence.

units.title: Apparently (hình như)
units.explanation: “ Hình như ” is used when the speaker suspects something might be true based on unofficial information. It’s equivalent to “ it seems like ” or “ apparently ” in English. PATTERN Hình như + Subject + Verb/Adjective.

examples.vi: Hình như anh ấy đang buồn.
examples.en: It seems he’s feeling sad

examples.vi: Hình như anh ấy đang buồn.
examples.en: It seems he’s feeling sad.

examples.vi: Hình như họ chưa biết tin này.
examples.en: It seems they don’t know this news yet.

examples.vi: Hình như cô ấy không thích món này.
examples.en: It seems she doesn’t like this dish.

examples.vi: Hình như trời sắp mưa.
examples.en: It seems like it’s going to rain.

examples.vi: Hình như
examples.en: Anh ta

examples.vi: Hình như họ chưa quyết định
examples.en: It seems they haven’t finished deciding.

units.title: Seem To (có vẻ)
units.pattern: Có vẻ + Adj / Có vẻ như + clause
units.explanation: “ Có vẻ ” is used when you give a statement/ an opinion/ prediction which is based on your own observation..

examples.vi: Cô ấy có vẻ vui hôm
examples.en: She seems happy today.

examples.vi: Cuốn sách này có vẻ thú vị
examples.en: This book seems interesting.

examples.vi: Họ có vẻ thích nơi này
examples.en: They seem to like this place.

examples.vi: Chúng tôi có vẻ đã đến muộn
examples.en: We seem to be late.

examples.vi: Bữa tiệc có vẻ đông
examples.en: The party seems lively.

units.title: It Appears That (dường như)
units.explanation: “ Dường như ” is slightly more formal and literary, often used to express a thoughtful or reflective observation. PATTERN Dường như + Clause.

examples.vi: Dường như anh ấy đang suy nghĩ điều gì.
examples.en: It appears that he is thinking about something.

examples.vi: Dường như cô ấy đã quên hẹn.
examples.en: It appears that she forgot the appointment

examples.vi: Dường như họ không hài lòng.
examples.en: They don’t seem satisfied

examples.vi: Dường như trời sắp tối.
examples.en: It appears that it’s getting dark

examples.vi: Dường như
examples.en: Anh ta

examples.vi: Dường như không
examples.en: Ai quan

modules.title: Include & exclude in Vietnamese
modules.description: In this lesson, you’ll learn how to express inclusion and exclusion in Vietnamese using common phrases like gồm có , bao gồm , không tính , and trừ . Mastering these structures will help you clearly state what is or isn’t part of a group, list, or total.

faqs.question: What’s the difference between “không tính” and “trừ” when excluding something?
faqs.answer: Both express exclusion, but “trừ” is often used in more formal or written contexts, while “không tính” sounds more conversational and is common in speech.

faqs.question: Can “gồm có” and “bao gồm” be used the same way?
faqs.answer: Yes, they both mean “include,” but “bao gồm” is a bit more formal. “Gồm có” is commonly used in lists or descriptions.

faqs.question: What does “bao hàm” mean compared to “bao gồm”?
faqs.answer: “Bao hàm” also means “to include” but usually refers to abstract or conceptual inclusion, like ideas or meanings, whereas “bao gồm” often lists tangible elements.

faqs.question: How do I say “The price includes breakfast” in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: You can say: Giá bao gồm bữa sáng or Giá gồm có bữa sáng.

faqs.question: Is it correct to use “tính cả” to express inclusion?
faqs.answer: Yes, “tính cả” is a natural and informal way to say “including,” especially when adding extra elements: Tính cả tôi thì có 5 người.

extracted_patterns: Subject + không bao gồm/ không gồm có + Nouns/Items

units.title: Include (bao gồm)
units.explanation: “ Bao gồm ” emphasizes inclusion , often used in formal contexts or when listing items.

examples.vi: Bữa ăn bao gồm nước uống.
examples.en: The meal includes drinks.

examples.vi: Chuyến đi bao gồm vé vào cổng.
examples.en: The trip includes entrance tickets

examples.vi: Hộp này bao gồm 10 cái bút.
examples.en: This box includes 10 pens

examples.vi: Công việc bao gồm làm báo cáo hằng ngày.
examples.en: The job includes making daily reports

examples.vi: Lớp học bao gồm
examples.en: 30 sinh

units.title: Comprise (gồm có)
units.explanation: “ Gồm ” is more conversational synonym of “ bao gồm ,” often used interchangeably.

examples.vi: Món ăn gồm có rau, thịt và cơm.
examples.en: The dish includes vegetables, meat, and rice. Danh

examples.vi: Giá vé gồm có bảo hiểm du lịch.
examples.en: The ticket price includes travel insurance

examples.vi: Sách gồm có hai phần: lý thuyết và bài tập.
examples.en: The book includes two parts: theory and exercises

examples.vi: Kế hoạch gồm có các bước chi tiết.
examples.en: The plan includes detailed steps

examples.vi: Đội bóng gồm có 11 cầu thủ chính.
examples.en: The team includes 11 main players

units.title: Including (tính cả)
units.explanation: “ Tính cả ” highlights inclusion, especially when counting or totaling items ..

examples.vi: Giá này tính cả tiền vận chuyển.
examples.en: This price includes shipping fees

examples.vi: Lớp học có 35 người, tính cả giáo viên.
examples.en: The class has 35 people, including the teacher

examples.vi: Nhóm nghiên cứu tính cả sinh viên quốc tế.
examples.en: The research team includes international students

examples.vi: Bữa tiệc có 50 khách, tính cả trẻ
examples.en: The party has 50 guests, including children Chi

examples.vi: Báo cáo tính cả số liệu năm ngoái.
examples.en: The report includes last year’s data.

units.title: Encompass (bao hàm)
units.explanation: “Bao hàm” is used for abstract or conceptual inclusions, indicating that something encompasses broader meanings or ideas..

examples.vi: Lý thuyết này bao hàm nhiều khái niệm phức tạp.
examples.en: This theory encompasses many complex concepts.

examples.vi: Quyền tự
examples.en: Do bao

examples.vi: Ý kiến của anh ấy bao hàm sự quan tâm sâu sắc.
examples.en: His opinion implies deep care

examples.vi: Luật này bao hàm các quy định về an toàn lao động.

examples.vi: Bài thơ bao hàm ý nghĩa sâu
examples.en: The poem implies profound meaning

examples.vi: Sách này bao hàm nhiều vấn đề khoa học.

units.title: Subject + không bao gồm/ không gồm có + Nouns/Items
units.explanation: For.

examples.vi: Giá này không bao gồm thuế.
examples.en: This price doesn’t include tax.

examples.vi: Chuyến đi không bao gồm vé máy
examples.en: The trip doesn’t include plane tickets Chi

examples.vi: Hợp đồng không bao gồm bảo hiểm.
examples.en: The contract doesn’t include insurance

examples.vi: Giá phòng không bao gồm tiền dịch vụ.
examples.en: The room price doesn’t include service charges.

examples.vi: Vé vào cổng không bao gồm trò chơi.

units.title: Subject + trừ + Nouns/Items
units.explanation: For.

examples.vi: Mọi người đều được mời, trừ
examples.en: Everyone is invited, except him. Play

examples.vi: Tất cả đều làm bài tập, trừ
examples.en: Minh. Everyone did the homework, except Minh. Play

examples.vi: Nhóm gồm 10 người, trừ tôi.

examples.vi: Ai cũng đi chơi, trừ cô ấy.

examples.vi: Tất cả đều đồng ý, trừ

units.title: Subject + không tính + Nouns/Items
units.explanation: For.

examples.vi: Giá này không tính phí giao hàng.
examples.en: This price excludes shipping fees. Chi

examples.vi: Lớp học có 40 người, không tính giảng viên.

modules.title: Certainty & uncertainty in Vietnamese
modules.description: In this lesson, you’ll learn how to express certainty and uncertainty in Vietnamese using common words like chắc chắn , có thể , có lẽ , and more. These expressions are essential for sharing opinions, making assumptions, or stating facts with varying degrees of confidence.

faqs.question: What is the difference between chắc chắn and có thể ?
faqs.answer: Chắc chắn expresses certainty (definitely/surely), while có thể indicates possibility (might/may). For example: Tôi chắc chắn anh ấy sẽ đến vs. Anh ấy có thể sẽ đến .

faqs.question: Can có lẽ and có thể be used interchangeably?
faqs.answer: Not always. Both express uncertainty, but có lẽ often implies a stronger guess or assumption based on context, while có thể emphasizes possibility.

faqs.question: How do you politely express uncertainty in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: You can use phrases like tôi nghĩ là… , có lẽ là… , or theo tôi thì… to soften your opinion and avoid sounding too direct.

faqs.question: Is there a word for “probably” in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Yes, có lẽ and chắc là can both be translated as “probably,” depending on the tone and certainty level intended.

faqs.question: Can I use chắc by itself to express certainty?
faqs.answer: Yes, c hắc is a shortened form of chắc chắn and is commonly used in casual speech. However, it can also mean “probably” depending on intonation and context.

units.title: Surely (chắc chắn)
units.explanation: “Chắc chắn” conveys strong confidence, equivalent to “definitely” or “ surely. ” It is used to confirm facts or guarantees..

examples.vi: Tôi chắc chắn anh ấy sẽ đến.
examples.en: I’m sure he will come.

examples.vi: Chúng tôi chắc chắn sẽ thắng trận đấu.
examples.en: We are certain we will win the match

examples.vi: Điều này chắc chắn là sự thật
examples.en: This is definitely the truth

examples.vi: Cô ấy chắc chắn biết câu trả lời
examples.en: She definitely knows the answer

examples.vi: Kế hoạch này chắc chắn sẽ thành công
examples.en: This plan will definitely succeed

examples.vi: Chắc chắn anh ấy hiểu ý bạn
examples.en: He definitely understands your point

units.title: Guarantee (đảm bảo)
units.explanation: “Đảm bảo” emphasizes a guarantee or assurance. It is commonly used for promises or obligations..

examples.vi: Tôi đảm bảo bạn sẽ hài lòng.
examples.en: I guarantee you will be satisfied.

examples.vi: Họ đảm bảo giao hàng đúng hẹn
examples.en: They ensure timely delivery.

examples.vi: Cô ấy đảm bảo hoàn thành công việc
examples.en: She guarantees to complete the task.

examples.vi: Dịch vụ này đảm bảo chất lượng
examples.en: This service guarantees high quality.

examples.vi: Chúng tôi đảm bảo bảo mật thông
examples.en: We guarantee information security.

examples.vi: Giá rẻ nhưng đảm bảo sản phẩm chính hãng
examples.en: Low price but guaranteed authentic products.

units.title: Certainly (nhất định)
units.explanation: “Nhất định” emphasizes determination when you make a decision or an intention which will happen 100%..

examples.vi: Tôi nhất định sẽ
examples.en: Tham gia

examples.vi: Anh ấy nhất định phải hoàn thành dự án
examples.en: He must complete the project

examples.vi: Chúng ta nhất định sẽ gặp nhau
examples.en: We will definitely meet each other

examples.vi: Điều này nhất đị
examples.en: n h

examples.vi: Tôi nhất định không bỏ cuộc
examples.en: I will certainly not give up

examples.vi: Anh ấy nhất định sẽ quay lại
examples.en: He will surely come back

units.title: Maybe (chắc là)
units.explanation: “Chắc là” expresses uncertainty that you are not sure about whether your statement is true or not. PATTERN Chắc là + subject + verb/adjective/noun.

examples.vi: Chắc là họ đã về nhà rồi.
examples.en: They’ve maybe gone home already.

examples.vi: Chắc là hôm nay trời sẽ nắng.

examples.vi: Chắc là cô ấy đang ở văn phòng.

examples.vi: Chắc là anh ấy không muốn

units.title: Might Be (có thể)
units.explanation: “Có thể” does not only mean “can” but also “might be” to express something might or might not happen..

examples.vi: Cô ấy có thể trở thành một bác sĩ giỏi.

examples.vi: Dự án này có thể thành công.
examples.en: This project might succeed

examples.vi: Tôi có thể hoàn thành công việc trước thời hạn.
examples.en: I might finish the work ahead of schedule

examples.vi: Trẻ em có thể học rất
examples.en: Children might learn very quickly

examples.vi: Họ có thể đến vào chiều
examples.en: They might come this afternoon

units.title: Not Sure (chưa chắc)
units.explanation: The antonym of “ Chắc chắn ” is “C hưa chắc “. You can use the latter when you are not sure about something..

examples.vi: Tôi chưa chắc có thể đến kịp
examples.en: I’m not sure I can make it on time

examples.vi: Chưa chắc cô ấy đã đồng ý
